title: Air Terjun Takah Pengkoi
translated_review: The last time I visited this place was in 2021, and now 2024 I ve come back again. Since it was a Friday, which is part of the weekend in Johor, I expected the place to be fully occupied. To my surprise, it was completely empty no one else was there! We had the whole place to ourselves, which made the experience even better.We started by cooking Indian banana leaf curry rice and enjoyed some delicious payasam for dessert. Later in the evening, we prepared Korean steamboat for dinner, played UNO cards, and even danced the night away. The room was pleasantly cold even without turning on the fan, and the linens were clean and fresh.The next morning, we cooked breakfast and enjoyed the amazing waterfall shower it was such a refreshing experience. The waterfall itself is beautiful and serene. For lunch, we cooked again, and during that time, we noticed a group of motorbikes and some Singaporean cars entering the area. They stayed for about an hour to shower and then left.We eventually left in the evening, feeling incredibly satisfied. This time, having the entire waterfall to ourselves made the trip unforgettable. It truly felt like the whole place belonged to us! Highly recommend this unique experience.

title: Air Terjun Takah Pengkoi
translated_review: This campsite waterfall is located in Segamat district, Johor. Nearest town from Takah Pengkoi is Bekok town. You can get supplies here before entering the oil palm plantation where the campsite is located. Bear in mind there is very limited phone signal at the campsite. To get there, it is most convenient to locate Sekolah Kebangsaan Kampung Kudong then go pass it till you find the Takah Pengkoi sign board on your left. Turn left, go straight and you will arrive at the entrance of the campsite. Register and pay for the entrance fee. There is a kak Ros there who does the registration. Fees cost RM40 per pax per night, or RM70 per pax for 2 nights or RM15 per pax for day trip. Operation hours 9am to 7pm. The campsite parking can fit in 20 30 cars. Otherwise just do roadside parking and walk a bit to the river campsite. Basic toilet and shower facilities are available male female separated . Do not expect luxury. But water is strong for shower and the flush works. The campsite is well maintained, no garbage strewn around, no funny smell, not much mosquito, no wild dogs cats, no monkeys. It is good. But watch out for squirrels they ll eat any fruit left unsupervised. There are still other insects that bite like mosquito though. Crowds peak on Friday and leave by late Saturday afternoon because Johor starts to work on Sunday. Solar lights are placed nearby toilets, walkway and parking area. To see stars at night, walk further away from camp to avoid the lights. There are several spots where you can safely enjoy the waterfall cascades. Feel the water rush that massages your back. Get some nice photos. Water is relatively clear at the upper levels and lesser at the lower part of the river. Some areas are shallow and it is suitable for children. Camp right in front of the river. Awesome experience to relax while keeping an eye on your children and doing barbecue. There are lots of shade and you can feel the cool temperature.

title: Azahari Boat Services
translated_review: Just came back for day trip using Azahari Boat Services.. overall nothing much to shout out.. tekong boat crew were friendly , our trip was in Feb which is still in Monsoon season, the sea waves were quite rough, half the spots in the intenary we unable to go because the boatman said the spots were too risky.. we visited just 4 spots.. 2 spots for snorkeling.. the water was not very crystal clear .. can see many dead corals.. so were quite dissapointed with that, the operator should had informed us earlier before we set out .at least we need to be refunded discount due to this issues on the spots that we cannot visit., to cut short, all of us inside the boat have to return to jetty on 3pm .. Not sure if I will visit Redang again after this this.. the view is very unique breathtaking..but the whole sea bed coral was too dissaptiong.. not sure about other beach or spot like Shark Bay as we didn t go there..so a friendly reminder not to go during this period in Feb...not worth your time money.. Redang I can say quite overrated

title: Azahari Boat Services
translated_review: Our trip to Redang Island was absolutely incredible, thanks to the outstanding service we received! The snorkeling experience was beyond amazing! Crystal clear waters, vibrant coral reefs, and so many colorful fish and marine life especially the sea turtle, I named one to called Jaja. The views were breathtaking, with pristine beaches and stunning ocean vistas at every turn.What really made our trip special was the fantastic crew especially Aliff and team. They are professional, friendly, and always going the extra mile to ensure we had the best time. They were knowledgeable about the best snorkeling spots, kept us safe, and made the whole experience fun and memorable.From the seamless arrangements to the breathtaking underwater sights, everything was perfect. We couldn t have asked for a better trip! Highly recommend this tour to anyone visiting Redang, you won t be disappointed! Thank you for an unforgettable adventure!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: a lovely baba nyonya heritage experience in a well maintained museum made from the furniture and old house of a rich malaccan trader. the audio tour easy access through scan codes is worthistening to, as it is narrated by one of the family members, giving you some insight how malaccan life was 70 years ago

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: A must go place if you re visiting Malacca. This place is so beautiful that it s hard to believe someone lived here 200 years ago.There is a guided tour by staff, and self guided tour by book. Unfortunately when we went there wasn t a guided tour, but the staff was very professional in briefing us before we enter.The house is beautiful, most of the items here are original and really beautiful. Walking through, you feel like you ve travelled back in time, just imagine what it was like to live here. The items are clean, refurbished, and it didn t feel like things were fake or replicas. Unfortunately too, we came at the wrong time of year as the kitchen was undergoing some restoring so we couldn t see it.Highly recommended for history buffs, and also for the family to walk through history together.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: A truly classic museum of Peranakan Chinese culture When I first saw the city of Malacca in a Google search and then saw this museum, I immediately dreamed of one day visiting. And when that dream came true, I was ecstatic.It was a visit I will never forget.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: A very beautiful place must visit!! very beautiful bout history of Melaka baba nyonya . the house so classical and gorgeous!!! We can see the original ancient Chinese Hokkien belief, practices, cultures costume !! British colonial era house design furnitures etc! dont forget to scan the QR codes to listen to the house owner history!!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: An interesting museum that allows for a diverting hour at most .My American Chinese in laws found it very interesting but it was a little less grabby for a non Asian like myself.It s a nicely maintained house though.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Baba Nyonya heritage museum on Heeren street gives a glimpse into the culture and history of the Peranakan community or the Baba Nyonya community in Melaka. It s a pretty museum to explore although I d have loved a guided tour. We were given a guide book which was quite helpful too.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Beautiful and splendid museum exploring the local history of Peranakans in Malacca through the lens of a prominent Chan family. Adult tix costs only 25MYR and we spend a good 1h exploring the surprisingly vast museum and listening to the Audio guides. It was also a welcome reprieve from the heat!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Beautiful historical place. Worth a visit! The pictures say it all. Explanation in English or Chinese Mandarin. Visited on Saturday, quite packed with tourists from China, Korea, France and locals.Visitors need to take off their shoes to go to first floor which has bedroom and study room. Beautiful peranakan windows throughout. Steps are quite steep and narrow, maybe around 20 steps.There s a drink water dispenser on ground floor near the kitchen.Toilet is clean and there are 2 benches outside the toilet before the exit, for elderly visitors to rest.Entry fee is RM 18 person

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: beautiful museum and very peaceful. it felt like home even though i don t live there!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Compare this to the similar Nyaung Museum in Penang, it s a little bit smaller, but storytelling is more better.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Definitely a must visit when in Malaka. Was the actual abode of the Chan family until the 1970s. You will be greeted by a current descendent of the family which is a nice touch. There are no guided tours but plenty of QR codes to explain each part of the house. There is free wi fi to access an app with an English commentary. I highlight interesting details that you might have missed below.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Educational tour of the baba and Nyonya museum in Melaka. The furniture and fixtures are so elaborate that you can really feel their opulence lifestyle. Well preserved furniture! Interesting place to spend 1 plus hour.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Entry costed 18 Ringgits per head, which we paid in cash at the entrance. The staff gave us an introduction about the place and asked us to self explore. Has a collection of items used by Baba and Nyonya during their time. Footwear needs to be taken off before getting upstairs.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Every thing here is just stunning! To be honest, I have never seen such a magnificent residential home in China. Everything is so well preserved that it feels like time has stood still. The current owner still takes care of this residence, and inside, along with its intriguing architecture and beautiful interior, there are stunning pieces of furniture worth seeing. It s a part of Chinese history and represents the cultural wealth that the Chinese have created and preserved outside of China. The visit is consistently awe inspiring and impressive. I was fortunate to meet the heir and current owner of the ancestral home and have a conversation with them. Such museum homes are quite common in Southeast Asia, especially in Malaysia. I hope to have the opportunity to visit and compare them with those in other places.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Great place to explore and learn! Well maintained exhibits, informative displays, and a peaceful atmosphere. Perfect for all ages.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Have had a wonderful time exploring and understanding this heritage as passed down from the early babas and nonyas. Very well maintained and preserved exhibits and artifacts which are passed down from generations to generations. Watch out for the floor tiles, the well carved well maintained too! doors, furnishings, decorative displays, paintings and photos and the various daily utensils for eating and cooking. Friendly host informed on what to do and not to do and where to sit and not sit through booklet which was returned after visit. The stairs and steps to 2nd level has its own designs to behold and admire too. Amazing place, worth the time taken to visit and spent like 2 hours there. Parking is really tricky at this place but the staff there mentioned there is a parking place with 3RM for whole day behind a particular block. Need parking coupons otherwise.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: I have been to Melaka for many times but never had the chance to visit this place. This time we make time after checking out from the hotel which is nearby and come to this place.If you love to see historic items, then this is a must. There is a ticket for entry but it is worth it since the place is well maintained.In order to enjoy it better, listen to the audio tour that comes in variety of languages. For Malaysian you may want to listen to Baba which is entertaining.Be careful when taking the stairs up since it is very narrow and steep. A lot to see and thanks to the family for showcasing one of the heritage of Melaka.At the end of the tour, you will exit at their small souvenir shop. Enjoy!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: I love this place. We can see the heritage of our culture through the interior

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: I visited the Baba Nyonya Heritage Museum in Malacca, and it was a very interesting experience. The house is beautifully preserved and gives a real sense of the Peranakan culture and lifestyle. The interior is full of detailed antiques, furniture, and decorations that reflect the rich history of the community. It s a well maintained place and worth visiting if you want to learn more about Malacca s heritage.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: I was super impressed by this museum it has so much character and personality. The information plaques and QR code pages are interesting, short and punchy just how it should be! I can imagine it s less fun when it s really busy, so try and choose a quiet time to visit.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: In Malacca, which flourished as a hub for intermediary trade, we visited the homes of the Baba Nyonya community, descendants of Chinese immigrants and local Malaysians. These residences, built in the Peranakan architectural style, reflect both tax saving designs such as narrow facades and feng shui principles.The buildings feature long, narrow layouts, skylights, and inner courtyards distinct characteristics of Peranakan architecture that also reveal the clever adaptations of the people who once lived there. Touring these homes offers a glimpse into their way of life and a chance to imagine daily life in those times.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Interesting historical record of Baba Nyonya families in Malaysia. The on line self guided tour was very good to listen to as you around.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: It s a private museum with exhibitions about baba nyomya culture. If you want to know the culture of baba nyonya, it s a perfect place to know from origin, life style to architecture. The entrance fee is rm25, which I found a bit expensive compared with other museums. But this is a Private museum so the price is fair enough. Rather, thankful to the people here keeping the baba nyonya culture alive to the public. Highly recommend to visit

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: June 2025 Entrance is paid. To get here you need to walk. The area is on the street of the Jonker Walk building. Use google maps to walk. Before visiting there is a 5-minute explanation session. To get more detailed information you can scan QR. To visit here you must have an interest in the history of civilization, migration and culture as well as art. The atmosphere is vintage, calm and cool.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Large, extensive local style museum. To be fair, it s one of few similar existing in the same city and old quarter, yet its exhibition display is way more extensive, interesting and, what s more important for the tourist, informative for the visitor.Well done museum, please keep it up!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Museum was clean and well maintained. Guided tour are available depends on the time of the day but it wasn t available when we got there, but the information book was informative enough for us. RM 16 charge was money well spent, we learnt quite a bit whilst there. There s signs clearly say don t touch the antique items but sadly seems the signs were still ignored by some visitors. There was insufficient staffs to enforce any kind of control. People really need to respect these historic items to preserve them for our future generations..

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Nice heritage museum. Beautifully preserved house with lots of history and culture to explore. The guided tour was informative and really brought the place to life. A must visit in Melaka if you want to learn about Baba Nyonya heritage!

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Nice place to visit if you are around the area. Can go without guided tour as booklet detailing each room and areas is provided. My kids enjoyed the visit and got to learn more about the culture. The place is well maintained to with access to toilet.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Nicely restored and long history of the Chan Family. Some of their family heritage and showcasing the old days how a typical Baba family life.The ticket is RM25 each.Walking tour guide was handy, just scan and listen as they narrate each area.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Pretty cool, official tour at 11 so we missed it but the audio guide s quite helpful. Nice to see and learn how the Baba Nyonyas lived back in the day.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Really great house heritage museum. Gorgeous and well curated house with great history. Also, staff is really friendly and professional.They have free English tours at 11am on Saturday and free Chinese tours at 3pm on Saturday. However, I didn t arrive at the time. Instead, I used the free WiFi and free audio tour guide that is provided by QR codes throughout the house.There is also a place to refill water, bathrooms, and certain areas with seating and air conditioning. I would spend about 1 hour exploring here.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: Such a great place to experience a very authentic Baba and Nonya heritage. The items were very well taken care of and displayed, and the cultural lessons were priceless. My second visit here.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: The Baba Nyonya Heritage Museum is a treasured cultural heritage site in Malacca s heart. It authentically recreates the lives of the Peranakan Chinese, a group that emerged from the long term integration of early Chinese immigrants with the local Malaysian community. Formerly the century old residence of the Zeng family, the museum was converted into a museum in 1985, to systematically preserve and display their unique architecture, furnishings, clothing, and everyday items. Guided tours offer insightful explanations of the social structure, religious beliefs, and customs of the Baba and Nyonya people, providing valuable insights into and understanding Malaysia s diverse cultural landscape.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio
translated_review: The heritage house is well maintained by the family.Walking around will give us glimpses of past life. The decorations are mesmerizing while the architecture is really top notch.Wonderful experience for those who are interested in the history of Baba Nyonya as we get to see their dresses, house items, house design, old technology etc or else it ll be an old house with old items being displayed.

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio

title: Baba Nyonya Heritage Studio

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: I was staying in sukau at the homestay similarly with this place, but it known Balai Kito homestay mustara haji Rangon. Ezall I probably meet him that time shown a good person, honestly and understand a travelers. Price RM30 breakfast, RM50 person you meal. He can take you to the journey or river cruise with smile and full of knowledge about rainforests.

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: In Sukau we checked in at homestay Balai Kito , well sign posted and about 500m after the village mosk, following the road along the river. The owner Ezall, living with his mother and brother in this 9 room house was very helpful in organizing the boat trips. We paid 80MR for the simple air conditioned room with toilet shower ensuite, including 3 meals. He charged us 60MR pax for a boat trip 2 hours each of which we had two and 125MR pax for the elephant spotting boat trip. The elephants were far away down stream. That s why he charge us that much. The first tailor made journey started 4pm. We saw lots of animals birds, a crocodile, lots of different monkeys, and a snake, lying in a tree. All together it lasted 2 hours. Ezall spotted animals easily. Sometimes with the help of his boats man. After that we had a lovely dinner, prepared by his mother. Ezall s late grandfather had taught him everything about the jungle when he was a kid. His late grandfather must have been a real bushman. He also fought the Japanese in the jungle in WW II. The next morning we left for the second boat trip at 6am. This time in another boat with another boats man on another part of the river more up stream . This time there was also a short walk in the jungle included. Ezall s knew his way, as there were no paths. He explained how to escape if attacked by an animal, explained about trees and other interesting stuff. We saw a lot of animals again, some different e.g. eagles as the previous day. We also saw an enormous crocodile swimming about 4 5 meters long! . The journey lasted again at least 2 hours. After arriving back at the homestay, we had our simple breakfast with delicious homemade pastries. At about 10am we drove ourselves to the Gomantong Caves, 25 minutes drive, which was interesting. We even spotted a Oran Utan with a baby! After hiking in the surroundings of the cave, we went back to have a tasteful lunch at 1pm. At 2pm we had our third boat trip, with Ezall and a different boats man. Al the journeys at was Ezall, boatman, my son and I. This trip was aimed at elephant spotting. The supposedly were far away downstream. It took us 2 hours boating to find them. A herd of elephants. Males, females, younger ones and a baby. We saw the baby getting attacked by the younger elephants, who tried to kill the baby! They were at the riverbank with lush vegetation and we were up close. I found it a miracle that Ezall and his mate spotted them. I would never have. Even being so close. They said they could smell them and see the fresh footprints. It was amazing. I have seen elephants in Africa on different occasions, but never encounters this close. The natural setting was also very different. Impressive! On our way back another 2 hours by boat , we were so lucky to see a single elephant crossing the enormous river. Amazing! I have to say that Ezall showed great respect to all the animals we spotted and to the natural environment in general. I would highly recommend staying at homestay Balai Kito and Ezall Rohaizad Mohd. He has a hawk for wild life spotting and he stops wherever you like. Ezall is entertaining and knows a lot about the area. He has interesting stories to tell. The food is good. The homestay is spacious and clean. Ezall does everything in his power to make you never forget the trip. The whole packages was very reasonable priced and although paying for 2 hour trips, it never bothered that they took longer because of requests for several stops as we spotted where we came for. Again, Ezall is highly recommended. You show up at Homestay Balai Kito or you contact him by phone 601 45587675 or email balaikito2000 gmail.com

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: Me and two other friends had a lovely stay at Balai Kito. We booked a 2 nights 3 days package with them and it was one of our best experiences in Borneo. They picked us up at Sandakan, had lunch together with their family, did several boat trips to spot wild life, made local handycraft, played a traditional game with the family, danced on traditional music, etc. Recommended!!

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: My husband and I stayed with Ahbam and his family for two nights. They were extremely friendly and welcoming. They had lots of activities planned for us and we really enjoyed our stay. We had to change our dates around and they were really helpful. We stayed in an air conditioned room to the side of their house and their door was always open. They provided a full programme of river trips, trekking, going to Gommantang caves, dancing and even showed us some work on reforestation that they are doing so we helped them plant some trees. Well worth a visit and they will change the schedule based on your interests.

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: Two friends and my partner and me showed up at this homestay at around 8pm after our actual accomoodation failed to accommodate us and we asked for available rooms. Luckily for us they had two double rooms availabe and we could move in immediately. The rooms were very friendly and clean and so was the whole house. For 30RM night person you got a room with ensuite bathroom and a nice breakfast. The early morning river cruise 50RM person we undertook was great, although the boatman arrived half an hour later then scheduled which meant that our chances of seeing crocodiles were very low because it was too late when we finally started the cruise. Unfortunately we could only stay for one night and therefore didn t have much time to talk to the friendly and knowledgable owners of this guesthouse. I certainly recommend to take your time and stay for at least two nights to really get the chance to talk to the owners and the other villagers and get to know their culture and way of living a bit better.

title: Balai Kito Homestay
translated_review: We booked the 4Days 3Nights Package to stay with Mr. Bam and his family. As we are traveling on a small budget we are looking wisely on what to spend our money. And this was definetly worth it At the first day we were lucky because it was the end of Rammadan and Mr. Bam took us with them to their family. They were all really welcoming and the food was so delicious! The accomodation was basic but totally fine. Keep in mind that you are living with a local family when you book a homestay and not a 5 star lodge so you have to adjust to their standard of living. Whe stayed at a double room on the other side of the house. Because I am allergic to cats it was better for us than staying with them. But Mr. Bam offered us to stay in their living room, too. The Food was great and most of the time we ate with them. The TV was switched on all the time, but I guess that is just normal for him and his eight kids and felt like we were integrated in their daily life. The activities were great fun. We learned a lot about their culture and the conservation efforts in the Kinabatangan area. One of our highlights was the traditional dancing and karaoke night at our last day. All the kids were participating and it felt like they had as much fun as we had. When we booked the package, we were a little bit afraid that it would feel arranged and forced, but that wasnt the case at all . The best were definetly the river cruises!! We have seen so many amazing animals including the pygme elephants, rare hornbills, lots of monkeys and so on. We ve even seen an orangutan,too but it was a bit far away. Keep in mind that the Kinabatangan river is not a zoo, so they can t garantee anything. We did the cruises with Mr. Bams son with no other tourists except us. He is not yet a professionell guide, but he grew up next to the jungle so he can answer you a lot of questions. Even if it was not everything perfectly in time, it was well organized and we liked that it was not the perfectly arranged touristy experience. Mr. Bam works at an orangutan research organization called HUTAN and does a lot of conservation projects in the village. For us it was so interesing to talk to him and get to know his perspective on palm oil plantations, tourism etc. He is eager to answer any questions you might have All in all it was one of our best experiences in Borneo and a very special chance to get to know the local way of living, the culture and also the problems of Sukau..

Each student was able to practice creating art with the melted wax and finished off their own batik designs with dying the fabric.To finish up the experience, we were given a tour of the batik factory where Batik Boutique works with local artisans to create their designs in house. It was a wonderful experience!

title: Batik Coloring Workshop in Kuala Lumpur
translated_review: A famous spot for batik art painting at Pasar Seni! From the entrance, walk straight to the outside area, and the shop will be right in front of you.Choose a batik design from the rack outside, with prices ranging from RM20 to RM40. Expect a 5 10 minute queue P S It s a popular spot for both tourists and locals . You can also share one artwork and paint together.The staff are amazing always ready to teach the basics. The color scheme is displayed on the table, so you can follow the suggested colors or get creative with your own choices. They ll check if you need fresh water and provide more paint if needed. Not sure if there s a time limit, but since the staff didn t mention anything, I don t think you ll run out of time before finishing your painting.They ll dry and pack your artwork for you! The colors look even more stunning after drying. Perfect for all ages , kids, couples, and anyone looking for a fun, hands on experience!

translated_review: I accidentally booked this trip and tried to cancel a couple minutes later, but since it was in the non refundable window, I couldn t get a refund. I understand the non refundable policy, but I feel like there should be a grace period of at least an hour when it comes to things like this.

title: Biking through Malay Countryside
translated_review: I did not get to experience much of the tour because I promptly fell and broke my wrist. The service could not have been better or more compassionate. As soon as I determined that I could not go on because of the pain, the guide called for the driver Eddie in this case to come and pick me up. Eddie took me to the local hospital and stayed with me the whole time it all took a couple of hours . When my credit card would not work, he paid with his own. When after consultation and X ray the doctor stated that the wait to see a specialist would be several hours and suggested I go to a private hospital in George Town, Eddie drove me back, recommended a few places and dropped me at one of them closer to my Airbnb. Finally when I wanted to compensate him for his time and trouble, he would not accept any money. Before leaving me, he gave me his phone number in case I needed future help. Take the experience knowing you will be cared for in case of need.

title: Biking through Malay Countryside
translated_review: Pretty average tour. I felt like there was pretty poor communication. Eddie did not respond to my initial message a few months ago. The night before my tour we didn t receive a confirmation or reminder message, despite it raining heavily. In the morning it was still raining heavily but they only gave us the option to cancel after they drove us an hour to the location. The bikes were in ok condition but not very good. The locations were fine, but it didn t feel like anything special or unique, aside from seeing some animals in the wild. There were a ton of mosquitos since it had just rained and we were taken through the forest. He was very very friendly and knowledgeable but I didn t feel connected beyond that, he didn t even introduce himself in the morning so I didn t know if it was our host or the driver. title: Biking through Malay Countryside

title: Biking through Malay Countryside

Eddie was very knowledgeable and friendly, and he provided tidbits that you would only get while exploring smaller villages and remote areas. Very cool to see paths paved for walking and biking through fields and villages. The nature was gorgeous, going from lush palm tree plantations to Chinese fishing villages. Bike ride was easy, just have to watch out for traffic in some spots. Highlight was feeding and petting the baby goats at the animal farm! My husband and I enjoyed this experience and recommend to anyone visiting the area.

title: Borneo Cultural Village
translated_review: The basic guided tour within the park involves staff leading you through the traditional houses of various indigenous groups, providing explanations, and the staff are happy to answer any questions. Afterwards, you ll be taken to sample the tribe s traditional snacks, one of which is quite unique there are seven such traditional houses. Finally, you ll have the opportunity to make your own curry chicken and pancakes. Before lunch, you ll watch a captivating indigenous performance. Lunch is a buffet, with unique and delicious flavors. Overall, it s a very enjoyable experience.

title: Borneo Cultural Village
translated_review: A fantastic cultural immersion with the Kadazandusun tribe!We had a wonderful time learning about the traditions and daily life of the local indigenous community. The experience was well organized, with multiple interactive stations and a knowledgeable guide shoutout to Colleen hope I spelled that right! who made the tour both informative and enjoyable. Despite the activities, it never felt overcrowded.Some of our favorite moments included Sampling authentic cultural dishes and drinks yes, even the butod sago palm weevil larvae ! Testing our skills with a blowpipe dart challenge. Exploring traditional huts built with different techniques across various eras. Watching captivating cultural performances. Making and eating! our very own roti canai with curry chicken.A big thank you to the team for such a memorable and well run experience! We highly recommend for anyone looking to connect with Sabah s indigenous heritage!

title: Borneo Cultural Village
translated_review: Borneo Cultural Village in Papar is a must visit, just a 30 minute drive from KK town. The ticket price varies for locals and non locals, but it s well worth it! The admission includes a guided tour available in English, Bahasa, and Mandarin , a hands on experience making your own Roti Canai Curry Chicken, and a buffet lunch. The guides were friendly, knowledgeable, and really brought the village to life. We loved learning about the different tribes of Sabah, sampling unique foods like sago worms, tapioca, and rice wine, and even trying out a blowpipe and joining in a fun Murut activity! The visit ended with an energetic cultural dance performance. Highly recommended for anyone visiting KK!

title: Bot Ronda Murah Tasik Santuari Hopping

The boat driver is also friendly, nice. 3. There are two options to board the boat. One here. Another one nearby this place is the tasoh tin dam square. Both prices are the same. translated_review: Let's come to Perlis! In this tin tasoh freshwater fish sanctuary, there are many types of activities you can do! Here you can ride a patrol boat around the lake Boat for fishing Kayaking Camping camping For eating fish For eating Goats sheep rabbits Rabbits Sour products and freshwater fish This area is a myKP fishing community project under the supervision of the Fisheries Department and the Perlis Department of Fisheries. title: Bot Ronda Murah Tasik Santuari Hopping
translated_review: We came here for camping 2 days one night. Check-in time is in the morning and check-out time is 3 pm, it's not my fault. Have to book. Fee RM50.00. Deposit RM25.00. Pay the balance on the day of the incident. Entrance road Tarred road. Buses can also enter. The car park is quite big. There is 1 restaurant there. Only at night I saw him close. Out on the main road there is a small grocery store. Wet stuff xde. There is only one supermarket 10 minutes from this place. Surau Mix men and women but separated by a curtain. There is no female ablution place. If it's a man, he can take ablution outside. Because there is one pipe.Toilet In the women's toilet there are 2 shower rooms and 2 toilet shower rooms.Plugpoints are available at each site. Bright light at night. There is a sink, you have to share. There is a barbeque place on every site. Other activities Fishing, sightseeing by boat, kayak. The atmosphere is a view of the lake. Comfortable. The night outside the tent is cold. Overall, recommended.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A Beautiful Garden Marred by Mischievous Monkeys I recently visited this stunning botanical garden, and it was truly a feast for the eyes. The variety of plants and flowers on display was breathtaking, and the garden s layout was well designed and easy to navigate.Note that it has many many hiking trails, one of them is connected the Penang HillThe scenery was picturesque, with plenty of tranquil spots to sit and take in the surroundings. I particularly enjoyed the scenic views and the sounds of nature.However, my experience was slightly marred by the presence of aggressive monkeys. They were quite bold and stole food from visitors, which was frustrating. I would advise visitors to be cautious and keep a close eye on their belongings.Despite this issue, I would still recommend the garden to anyone who loves nature and botany. With some measures to manage the monkey population, this garden could easily become a 5 star destination. For now, I ll give it 4 stars.

The path towards the garden from the car park is covered by trees most of the way.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A must visit for nature lovers and everyone who enjoys getting away from the city for a while.There are plenty of benches to sit and rest in the shade all over the big area.It s possible to walk on smaller paths into the forest, to small rivers and the lilly pond with turtles and lizards. There s even a path to hike up to Penang Hill.You can see a lot of lizards minding their own business. They re very calm and don t get aggressive.WARNING There are quite a few monkeys that can hunt you down when eating so better be careful when having a snack or doing a picnic.Even though the park visit is free at the time of writing this, it would certainly be worth coming here even if they charged an entry fee.Since it s a big area it can t really get all that crowded.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A must visit place for nature lovers and hikers. Free entrance fee. A good place for a morning stroll or evening walk. The garden is spacious, with a lot of greeneries, trees and flowers, and there are signboards that stated what plants are those together with their scientific names, as well as QR code for more information I didn t scan the code though . There are many smaller parks inside the big garden such as ferns house, bamboo park and others.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A nice relaxing walk through some lovely scenery. There are plenty of benches and places to rest, some in the sun and some in the shade. Make sure you have plenty of water as there s only 1 small shop near the entrance.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A tranquil manicured oasis a distance away from Georgetown. Note that vehicular access to the gardens is restricted and vehicles have to turn around more than 500m away from the entrance.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A true treasure of peacefulness and tranquility located, surprisingly, inside the city. There are trails for every hikers level but I suggest a guide as you can get turned around quite easily. Bring lots of water. It is hot!

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: A wonderfully natural and super lush tropical botanical garden. There is NO ENTRANCE FEE at the Penang tropical garden as well.Uniquely different from the equally excellent KL and Singapore tropical gardens.This garden is a more natural, more lush, and not nearly as well manicured garden as either the KL garden or the Singapore gardens. That is precisely its charm!There are loads of trails winding through various displays and presentations. Walkers, hikers, and joggers seem to enjoy the multitude of trails.Monkeys are EVERYWHERE.There are displays of herb gardens, orchid gardens, fern displays, aeroid trails, and much more.The original gardens were established in 1884 from an old quarry site, under the supervision of Charles Curtis, who was the first superintendent, making it one of the oldest botanic gardens built by the British in a colonial settlement.Penang Botanic Gardens is part of Penang Hill Biosphere Reserve, recognized by UNESCO as the third Biosphere Reserve in Malaysia listed in the World Network of Biosphere Reserve WNBR . 2 Currently, 12 2024, there is construction of a cable car system taking place, which limits access at the very beginning of the road leading into the garden.Restrooms are limited.Handicap doable in many areas. Some areas are paved roughly and might present issues for those who are unsteady at walking..Final take...HIGHLY RECOMMENDED.Be prepared to gain elevation as this garden winds uphill through super lush tropical canyons.There is a restaurant serving fresh coconut near the entrance. A total godsend after a day exploring these wonderful gardens.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang

title: Botanical Gardens Penang

Unfortunately a few sections were not maintained, most notably the lily pond with no lilies and overgrown weeds near the pond area, practically felt abandoned. title: Botanical Gardens Penang

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: Awesome green lung space in Penang. It can get really crowded on weekends and public holidays. We went quite early in the morning by Grab and thankfully so. It was teeming and drivers were desperately looking for parking spaces. We got off near the gate and it was quite crowded inside too. Once you re in and walking there is ample space and the greenery takes over the senses.It is a beautiful space. Trees and plants from all over the world. Many are labelled quite well. There are benches and gazebos as well where one can rest and catch ones breath. The gardens are quite well maintained and the cleaners are already on duty early in the day. So are the monkeys. Food inside is a strict no no with these mischievous little fellas on the prowl.The flowers are a sight to behold as were the lotus ponds. We will keep coming back regularly, if possible on weekdays. Fresh air is such a wonderful thing and so is this verdant space in Penang.

The pair are so cute and they are so near to us.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: I visited Penang Botanical Gardens on a weekday morning and found a peaceful, green space close to George Town. Entry is free and the main gate has a small cafe for overpriced coffee and cold drinks. There is a small foodcourt too by the entrance.I walked the main path from the gate, past the lily pond and along the stream toward the quarry and secret garden, and the whole route is shady, easy to follow and full of birdlife. If you like bird watching this place is excellent. I added the birds found in this area image to my review for you birdwatchers out there A small stream with gentle falls runs through the park and there are lawns and benches for resting. Trails are signposted and some lead into longer hikes toward Penang Hill if you want to explore further. Wildlife sightings are frequent I saw dusky leaf monkeys, long tailed macaques, lots of birds and many butterflies.Practical tips go in the morning for the best experience, bring binoculars and a camera with a zoom lens for birdwatching, and keep snacks zipped away because macaques can get bold .The park map and the bird ID images I attached are helpful for matching what you see to names. Overall, the main walking loop from the gate to the quarry and back will give you the best variety in one visit and can easily take two to three hours if you stop and watch.Facilities are basic but generally clean, with public toilets near the entrance and limited parking. Bring water, sun protection and insect repellent for comfort.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang

title: Botanical Gardens Penang

The variety of flora, from exotic orchids to towering palms, showcases a stunning diversity that captivates the senses. Well maintained pathways guide visitors through themed sections, each a delightful exploration of different horticultural wonders. The attention to detail extends to the well curated plant labels, providing educational insights for both casual visitors and avid botanists. The tranquil ambiance invites contemplation, making it an ideal escape from the hustle and bustle of daily life. Whether you re a gardening enthusiast or simply seeking a peaceful retreat, the Amazing Garden offers a visually stunning and rejuvenating experience.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang
translated_review: The Penang Botanical Gardens is one of the most refreshing spots to escape into nature while still being close to the city. It s a beautifully landscaped area with wide open lawns, shaded walking paths, and a mix of curated gardens and wild greenery. Nature SceneryThe gardens are spacious and well maintained, featuring a variety of plants including orchids, ferns, palms, and unique tropical trees. Some areas, like the lily ponds and fountain gardens, are perfect for a slow, relaxing stroll. There are also small waterfalls and streams, which add a natural charm to the setting. The Understorey Florarium is an interesting stop if you enjoy learning about local plant species. WildlifeAs you walk around, you ll likely spot plenty of monkeys and squirrels. The monkeys can be cheeky, so it s best not to carry food openly or get too close. Bird sounds fill the air, adding to the tranquil atmosphere. Walking HikingThere are several trails that connect to nearby hills and forest areas, making this a good starting point for longer hikes if you re up for it. Otherwise, the paved paths are comfortable for a casual walk, even with family or kids. AtmosphereThe overall vibe is peaceful, though weekends can get crowded with joggers, families, and tourists. Mornings are the best time to visit for cooler weather and fewer crowds. Entry AccessibilityEntry is free, which makes this an incredible value. It s also accessible for wheelchairs and strollers in the main areas, though some trails are more rugged. Parking is available near the entrance. OverallA must visit in Penang if you enjoy nature. It s not only a botanical garden but also a blend of manicured beauty and wild rainforest edge. Perfect for a walk, photography, or just to enjoy fresh air away from the city buzz.

title: Botanical Gardens Penang

They will grab your food so be alert when you carry food with you. Good place to jog around.Feel the real nature here. No noise, no pollution. Only peace. The parking is free, Entry is free. You can reach there with GRAB but it s a struggle to get InDrive or Grab there when you want to return back. There are limited Indian chinese cafes and everything is a bit expensive there. Here are some photos that i captured there. Enjoy .... detailed review The Penang Botanic Gardens offer a lovely escape from the hustle and bustle of city life. Having visited recently, I wanted to share my experience.Atmosphere The gardens are undeniably beautiful, with lush greenery, diverse plant life, and tranquil waterfalls. The overall atmosphere is peaceful and relaxing, perfect for a leisurely stroll or a picnic. It s a great place to connect with nature and enjoy some fresh air.Parking Parking can be a bit challenging, especially on weekends and public holidays. The available parking spaces near the entrance tend to fill up quickly. Consider arriving early or be prepared to park a little further away and walk.Entry Fee Tickets Good news! Entry to the Penang Botanic Gardens is currently free, which is a major plus. This makes it an accessible attraction for everyone.Food There are a few food stalls and vendors within the gardens, offering light snacks and drinks. However, the selection can be limited. If you re planning to spend a longer time there, it might be a good idea to pack your own food and drinks, especially if you have specific dietary needs. There are also restaurants outside the garden, which can be a good option before or after your visit.Pros Free Entry A huge advantage, making it accessible to all. Beautiful Scenery The gardens are well maintained and offer stunning views. Diverse Flora A wide variety of plants to see and learn about. Peaceful Atmosphere A great place to relax and escape the city noise. Photo Opportunities Plenty of picturesque spots for photos.Cons Parking Can be difficult to find parking, especially during peak hours. Limited Food Options The food available within the gardens is somewhat limited. Crowds Can get crowded, especially on weekends and public holidays. This can impact the tranquil atmosphere. Maintenance Minor While generally well maintained, I noticed some areas could use a little more attention.Overall The Penang Botanic Gardens are definitely worth a visit. Despite the parking and food challenges, the beauty and tranquility of the gardens make it a must see attraction in Penang. I recommend visiting on a weekday if possible to avoid the crowds. Bring your own snacks and drinks, and be prepared for potentially limited parking. Despite the minor cons, I thoroughly enjoyed my visit and would recommend it to anyone looking for a peaceful escape in nature.

title: Chew Jetty
translated_review: Chew Jetty, the largest and most famous of the historic Clan Jetties in George Town, Penang, is a fascinating UNESCO World Heritage site offering a unique glimpse into a traditional waterfront lifestyle. It is not a single floating structure, but a community of floating houses or, more accurately, stilt houses built entirely over the water on wooden piers. These rustic, interconnected wooden homes have been continuously inhabited by descendants of the Chew clan since the 19th century, creating a living, breathing village seemingly suspended between land and sea.Visitors can explore the long wooden walkway to witness this unique way of life. While primarily a residential area, the jetty has become a popular tourist attraction, with many ground level rooms in the houses converted into small shops selling souvenirs and local treats.For those interested in the water perspective, boating is a popular activity. Tour operators often offer short boat rides from the jetty, providing scenic views of the stilt houses and the George Town coastline from the sea.The cultural and religious heart of the community is marked by its temples. There are two significant Chinese temples on Chew Jetty. The temple near the entrance is dedicated to the God of Heaven Jade Deity , and another temple at the very end of the main jetty is dedicated to the Taoist Sea Deity, Haisen, to ensure good fortune and safe journeys at sea. While the primary temples follow Taoist and traditional Chinese folk religion, a prominent, striking structure adjacent to the Chew Jetty is the beautiful Hean Boo Thean Kuan Yin Temple, often referred to as the Floating Temple. While this temple is not physically on Chew Jetty, its stunning location perched right on the water makes it a major highlight when viewing the jetties from the coast or on a boat tour.

title: Chew Jetty
translated_review: It was my first visit to Chew Jetty and totally loved it. I decided to visit during the evening as the sun was setting. The commercial shops were already closed or were closing up when I got there. It was not crowded at that time and I felt it was an enjoyable visit then. There were many houses and shops on stilts along the Jetty.Some tips As there are residents who live along the jetty, the visiting hours are from 9am to 9pm. There is no cost to visit Chew Jetty. Evenings work out to be the better time to visit as it is cooler besides having lesser crowds then. There is a chinese taoist temple very close to the entrance of the jetty. It is free to visit and it is a beautifully, well maintained temple. Worth checking it out. I have a separate review for this temple . It is not the longest or largest jetty but probably the most visited by tourists. A visit to Chew Jetty without patronising the shops but to see and take photos videos, should take between half hour to an hour top. It is really up to you as to how long you would like to spend time here. When visiting, always keep a lookout for motorcyclists as they may ride by you to get in and out of their residences on the jetty.This is definitely a must visit spot in my humble opinion. I fully appreciate why this is a UNESCO heritage site and just beautiful to spend time here.

title: Chew Jetty
translated_review: We visited Chew Jetty close to sunset, and I must say it was the perfect time. The heat of the day had mellowed, and the golden rays of the setting sun cast a magical glow over the entire jetty. The wooden houses on stilts, the gently rippling water, and the silhouettes of boats in the distance made it feel like I had stepped into a postcard.Walking along the planks with the evening breeze brushing past, I felt completely at ease. There were fewer tourists at this hour, which gave us more time to soak in the peaceful charm and snap some really stunning photos without the crowds. The colors in the sky reflected beautifully off the water, adding to the calm, laid back vibe of the place.It s amazing how this historical clan jetty, with its rustic homes and narrow boardwalks, still stands strong amidst modern George Town. A mix of old world charm and everyday life, all quietly coexisting over the water.Definitely recommend coming around golden hour it s cooler, calmer, and visually unforgettable.

title: Chillax Paddy Field Sunset Biking
translated_review: In one word, this experience was magical. And that was because of this amazing combination of the location, the activity and the most incredible host, Mr Goh. I would like to cover each one separately. The location it is at the countryside in an area called Balik Pulau. It is quite far from Penang City, from areas like Georgetown. It will take almost 45 50 minutes to drive down. But, the way is beautiful and it is worth it to travel this far for this experience. The area is full of paddy fields and sparse population. You get to experience the real countryside whereby you see farmers harvesting paddy. The activity it is an adventure like no other on bicycles. Mr Goh, takes you first to a cycle renting place. Here we chose 2 X double riding bicycles. One was for me and my wife. And the other was for my two daughters, 18 years and 10 years of age. My wife doesn t know how to cycle, but yet both of us managed beautifully. Yes, there is quite a bit of cycling to be done for almost 1 hour in total, but not continuously. With enough stops and rests. Only in the evening while coming back to the starting point after sunset, it requires quite a bit of cycling and you might get a bit tired. But, nothing s worth it without a bit of hard work and adventure. And this tour is worth everything. So, after picking up your bicycles, you are taken to the paddy fields to experience paddy harvesting. You cycle through a mix of concrete and gravel roads, but all easy to ride on. Mr Goh who accompanies you everywhere also on a cycle, will take many pictures of you with lovely backgrounds and great posing ideas. This lasts for almost an hour before you start moving towards 2 sunset points next to the sea. Here you have to do a bit of trekking and reach those end points like places which overlook the setting sun on the sea. It is beautiful. The sunset is wonderful to watch and experience. You spend another hour before you ride back to the starting point which takes about 20 minutes of non stop cycling. Once you deposit back your cycles, it is dark already. Mr Goh then takes you for dinner at a local street food place which is fantastic. He rides on his motorbike and you follow him. It took us half an hour to reach the dinner place which is an area with full of food stalls and in between are many tables to sit and enjoy fantastic local food. Mr Goh does all the ordering after doing a check on your preferred diet. The fried kway teow is simply fantastic. And then the satay. And then the coconut apom. Finally you finish dinner and say your goodbyes to Mr Goh. Few things to keep in mind. The tour starts at 5 pm and finishes around 9.30 pm after which you have to drive back wherever you are staying. Please wear sports shoes. Also eat something before you start as you will feel very hungry in between the tour. Wear clothes which cover your body and protect you from mosquitoes. But wear light clothes as it is hot and you will sweat after all the cycling. Mr Goh will give you water bottles, mosquito repellent and cycle torches to help riding back after sunset. Lastly about our host Mr Goh. One word for him. He is such a wonderful person. Friendly, funny and always taking care of you. And to top it all he is your personal photographer and he will take hundreds of these fantastic photographs which he will later share with you on google drive. Never before in any tour did we get so many photographs of ours and each and every one is so good. That s what makes this magical and builds a bond with him. You actually feel bad when you part ways. He is such a selfless person who takes care of you all the time and always working hard to give you a good experience. Our family only wants to say a BIG THANK YOU to him! I would very strongly recommend that you do this magical tour with Mr Goh!

title: Choley Kampung Warna Warni Kuala Perlis
translated_review: If you ve ever wondered what it s like to step into a postcard that s been left out in the sun a little too long come visit Kampung Warna Warni. You ll find seven kampungs stitched together with bridges, murals, recycled art, and homes that look like they ve been dipped in rainbow sherbet. Some are stunning. Others are, sadly, losing the battle against the UV rays. It can get quite hot, depending on the time of day the kind of heat that makes you question why you re not just watching a travel vlog from your sofa. Most locals stay indoors because of it, only coming out when it cools down.Don t expect too many warm greetings you might get a few curious looks, but that s about it. It s not that people are unfriendly you re simply walking through someone s actual neighbourhood, and it feels like it. It s a bit like Penang s Clan Jetties, but rougher around the edges. You re never quite sure whether the locals are pleased about the foot traffic or simply tolerating it.Parts of the village are well kept, while others clearly need some love, with pools of black, stagnant water filled with discarded plastic bottles and an unpleasant smell. What is undeniably beautiful is the view from the bridge connecting the village to the Kuala Perlis jetty sweeping river views and a breeze in your face.There are a few stalls dotted around, and an open air restaurant at the car park if you re desperate for food or shade nothing fancy, but you can grab some local snacks like shrimp fritters or dodol pulut hitam. Prices are fair, and the food hits the spot.Unless you enjoy slow cooking yourself on foot, you can also pay RM35 and hop on a choley a local motor tricycle . There s quite a lot to see, and it s the easiest way to explore. The drivers know their way around and will point out interesting spots. Don t expect National Geographic style commentary more like, This aunty makes the best dodol. It s hard to make up your mind about this place. If Kampung Warna Warni wants to be a proper tourist spot, it probably needs better upkeep and perhaps a 7 Eleven or two though I m also not sure the residents would be too thrilled about their home being gentrified.

title: Cozy Leisure Farm (Bertam Valley)
translated_review: A Hidden Gem in Cameron Bertam Valley Cozy Leisure Farm My recent visit to Cozy Leisure Farm was nothing short of magical. Nestled approximately 1200m above sea level, the journey up the steep hill was thrilling in itself an adventure that set the tone for the rest of the experience. The moment we arrived, we were greeted by crisp, cooling air and panoramic views that made the climb absolutely worth it. What truly made the visit special was the founder himself, whose warmth and hospitality were unmatched. He personally walked us through his estate and campsite, sharing stories of how he cultivated the land over the past 30 years. His passion was evident in every corner of the farm from the flourishing avocado trees to the vibrant grapes, passion fruits, yellow golden fruits, and even robusta and arabica coffee plants. It felt like stepping into a living, breathing encyclopedia of tropical agriculture. The farm also features two beautifully designed camping sites, perfect for a weekend escape into nature. Whether you re a seasoned camper or just looking to unplug, the serene atmosphere and fresh mountain air make it an ideal retreat. To top it all off, the owner served us a delightful fruit platter, showcasing the very produce grown on his land. It was a sweet and refreshing end to an already unforgettable experience. And for families with children, there s even a mini zoo with rabbits and birds, adding a playful and educational touch to the visit. If you re looking for a unique, nature filled getaway with a personal touch, Cozy Leisure Farm is a must visit.

title: Cwoks Batik workshop
translated_review: I recently organised a company session at Cwoks Batik Workshop last Saturday 26 8 2023 and I must say, it was an incredible experience from start to finish! The workshop was well organized, informative, and a lot of fun.The instructor s passion for batik was evident as they guided us through the history, techniques, and creative process of batik making. Even as a beginner with no prior experience in batik, I felt comfortable and supported throughout the entire workshop.The workshop space was clean, well lit, and had all the necessary materials neatly set up for us. The instructor took their time explaining each step, making sure everyone was on the same page before moving forward. I appreciated the hands on approach, and the instructor was patient in answering all our questions.The highlight of the workshop was definitely the hands on batik creation. The instructor provided various designs to choose from, but also encouraged us to let our creativity flow and design our own patterns. It was a therapeutic and rewarding process, and I was amazed at the end result.By the end of the workshop, I not only had a beautiful piece of batik art to take home but also a newfound appreciation for the art form. The entire experience was enriching and enjoyable. I highly recommend the Cwoks Batik Workshop to anyone interested in learning about batik or exploring their creative side. Whether you re a beginner or have some experience, this workshop is a great way to dive into the world of batik with a knowledgeable and friendly instructor.

translated_review: The vast place is more of an agricultural area. In my opinion, the ticket price is expensive and does not match the attractions available. Adults RM15 and children RM10. Pet food per basket RM5. Children are interested in the mini zoo but the animals are not enough and they are not satisfied to see them. There is a rabbit area, some birds, 2 ostriches, cows and horses. In other areas, there are deer and other animals that you don't go to see because it's wide and tiring to walk. Train facilities are available but limited for visitors if there are many. Otherwise expensive fees are charged. The surau at the top of the cafe shares the men's and women's ablutions. The women's prayer section is blocked and covered with curtains for fear of revealing the nakedness. Surau is hot but thankful for being able to pray. Going a second time? Maybe think long. It is not suitable for the elderly or those with crutches or wheelchairs. My sister had gone before and stated that the landscape had changed and agreed with my opinion. We stopped by because the location between Muar Batu Pahat lacks interesting places to visit.

I would recommend calling or texting WhatsApp the given number to check if they have dodol available.Owner was really nice and welcoming, and allowed us to sample the dodol too! It was really nice and isn t jelak at all, plus it wasn t too sweet and doesn t stick much. The individual packaging was a really nice addition. It came to 38 ringgit for 36 small dodol. They also seems to have large dodol containers and smaller gift packs available.Highly recommend the detour to check their shop out!

title: Dodol Warisan Hajah Tijah (Uncle D) Dodol Melaka
translated_review: We just found out about this shop this morning so we didn t order in advanced. It s in a Kamping and the road there is quite small and not obvious so keep your eyes peeled. When we got there we didn t see anyone. Just then, the daughter of the shop s owner came by. She told us that she would check if any dodol was in stock. She allowed us to explore the area. It was quite the experience for me as I had never been to a Kampung before. They kindly let us test some dodol before we bought it.That was my first time trying dodol and it was really good! It was chewy but not too chewy, sweet but not too sweet and just melts in your mouth. My mother loves dodol and she said that this one was very very good. The people there were all very kind and generous. They gave us 3 extra dodol to take home! They were very friendly as well. Overall, I would definately recommend this place to people who want authentic, delicious dodol!

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: I love it here. Everything. The people, the view, the food, the vibes What's up pls book Uncle Halim! He is a good boat driver and so good at explaining things to us. I suggest you make a better online reservation, just come and pay in the cooperative. Best of all, one boat just for you, no other people involved except your circle but I don't know how many people the limit is for 1 boat. The road to Kg Sg Melayu goes through the palm trees, the road is tarred but there are a few puddles. Parking is very limited. There are those who park on the side of the road in front of people's houses by the drains, follow the sustenance. Better pad your stomach before coming, if you stop to eat at a restaurant later on the boat, you'll be afraid of vomiting. The jetty is scary because you only use boards. After getting on the boat, the first thing we do is go into the swamp, if you're lucky you can see crocodiles turn around in the swamp and see the mangroves. Exiting the swamp, we go around the river and release the crab cage, proceed to the place where the mussels are caught, and go round and round again for 1 hour. You can see the forest city. Later, after the native village, you can see Singapore, then the raft house and so on. After you are satisfied with the boat ride, you can stop for a meal at the Sri Pantai Restaurant. Recommended You have to come and try it yourself and you will get the feeling. I want to tell more tired

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: A good place for a weekend gateway. We arrived at 11am, no waiting time. The tourguide already waiting in front of the office. Ticket price rm35 per head. U can experience around 1hour tour around the mangrove river short visit to mussel farm.Recommended place to calm your mind.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

Only RM35 per pax for a period of 1 hour 1 hour 30 minutes. If you're lucky, you can see crocodiles, snakes, storks, fish, hanging out healing in the mangrove trees. The asam pedas restaurant by the jetty has delicious asam pedas dishes. The best. Must try!

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: A very interesting boat trip.Attractions Go through the mangrove swamp area in the Melayu River See wild snakes Mussel breeding center Catching crabs will be taken to a place to place crab traps, will be collected again when returning to land. Unfortunately, there is no sustenance. The trip takes almost an hour. The souvenir shop sells mussel crackers, clam crackers, crab crackers. If you want to buy a hat, you can. After getting on the boat, if you want to eat seafood, there is a popular sour and spicy restaurant, Sri Pantai Restaurant. Reasonably priced, worth the adventure experience. Don't wait long, don't need an appointment when the weekend comes.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: Come on Monday, at 3 00 pm. It's best to book first, get the phone number on Google map. RM35 per head. Boat tour, crab catching, mussels farm visit. For those who want to get mangrove forest experience, it is highly recommended. Tour guide uncle so friendly. Anything you can ask him, don't just stay silent on the boat, you'll get bored just catching crabs, there's no sustenance because the bait has to eat the fish. Because if we get crabs, we can bring them back. If there is a chance, you can see mangrove snakes, crocodiles and tree-climbing crabs. The road to the village has many potholes, so please drive safely and do support our local eco tourism

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

Start with uncle and aunt putting the crab trap and when we come back we get a crab and a puffer fish. Stop by the mussel raft and feed the fish there while taking pictures. A fun experience at a reasonable price. Life jackets are provided.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: Late to drop a review, but this hidden gem in Johor Bahru truly deserves the spotlight.People often say JB doesn t have much to offer but this spot proves them wrong. It s a surprisingly fun and refreshing escape, perfect for couples and families alike. After working non stop at jobs that sometimes don t feel worth the paycheck, this place felt like a well earned breather. I had fun, still having fun, and I wholeheartedly recommend it.What made it even more special was the amazing guide we had someone passionate enough to share stories about the majestic river running through Kampung Melayu. It added a rich layer of heritage and charm to the visit.Honestly? I d pick this over Puteri Harbour any day. A rare gem that blends relaxation, culture, and good vibes all in one.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu
translated_review: Located in Gelang Patah, Kampung Sungai Melayu is a small Malay village mostly occupied by 100 Malays who live as fishermen. This village is located at the southernmost point of the state of Johor, close to the Singapore border. According to history, this village is about 100 years old and was once used as a base for the Japanese Army to attack Singapore during the war. Kampung Sungai Melayu is located in the middle of the mangrove forest, which makes it a paradise for bird life and other mangrove residents. Bird watching enthusiasts will be happy to see various species such as eagles, storks, herons, swallows and eagles. Sungai Melayu village is also a paradise for fresh seafood such as fish, crabs, prawns, clams and others. Villagers also process various types of seafood such as dried fish, belacan and more. What to do? Bird watching activities in the mangrove forest. Overnight at local residents' homes through the Homestay program. Enjoy a river cruise and join the locals in searching for crabs. Visit traditional Johor-style Malay houses. Watch traditional games and handicraft making.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

The boat ride was so fun and got to see 4 5 snakes, 50 monkeys , blue crab etc. for a 40 min packed boat ride , the RM35 was a great price. We had 2 yr old kid and child friendly with infant life jackets provided. I must mention the locals around the kampung area are so kind and helpful. Our car got 2 tyres punctured due to a sharp unnoticed stone while we departed from the parking space. The local people let us stay in their home, offered drinks and lunch until the tow truck came in. Such a lovely day despite the vehicle issue.

title: Eco Tourism Kampung Sungai Melayu

Boat trip was an hour, most of it in the mangrove and also a visit to the mussel farm. Boatman only spoke Malay but was very helpful and tried to explain things to us as much as he could.Saw monkeys, kingfishers, herons and a mangrove snake. Very nice and peaceful outing. Rounded up with a lunch at the Asam Pedas restaurant.Definitely recommended if you like nature and some time away from the busy city life.

title: Explore Kampung Baru with a Local
translated_review: Air BnB experiences are a way for travellers to step into the shoes of a local and to experience their way of life for a fleeting moment. Fuad s 3 hour experience of Kampung Baru delivers this and more. Essentially, it is an in depth and personalised experience which is, at a visceral level, an experience which will transcend language, culture and religions. You will start the tour as strangers and leave as part of his keluarga family . Fuad is a 3rd generation resident of the kampung, and has witnessed first hand the manner in which modernism has impacted the quaint kampung life. He will bring you through the kampung providing information and insights which no travel book will provide. My wife and I found ourselves snacking on freshly picked fruits rambutan, guava and jambu, and were given a literal taste of life there. The best part of this journey was to be given an opportunity to visit his home. There, we were welcomed by his wife, Kak Siti. We found ourselves spending the evening engaged in revelling conversations. We were also given an assortment of unique savoury snacks and kuihs, before we continued with the tour of the kampung. The evening ends with a dinner at nasi lemak wanjo, which is arguably thy national dish of Malaysia. The setting is no frills, but the place is hygienic and the food is delectable. I will recommend the rendang ayam chicken which is slow cooked in herbs and spices with a special coconut blend . It was truly a memorable night and we can t thank these wonderful people enough. Terima Kasih Fuad and Kak Siti.

title: Explore Kampung Baru with a Local

Fuad is a fun, friendly, welcoming host who takes you on a journey in the neighborhood he grew up in, explaining the history and importance of this special place as you walk and eat together. His home is magical, his family is super sweet, and makes you feel like you are part of the family. We laughed, ate, discussed culture, music, life, and learned about each other. We have a deeper understanding and appreciation. of Malay culture after this tour. We love Malay food and KL even more after this tour. We feel like Fuad is a real friend and we will definitely reach out when we return to KL. Don t hesitate, all the reviews are true this is the tour you want to take in KL

All the Malaysian sweets we had for the first time were so delicious that the kids had to have more than one. Every piece of furniture, picture, and ornament in the house had a history, and we enjoyed talking about them. We talked about his family, about education in Malaysia, and many more. My children were noisy but he and she were very kind to them. I learned a lot from him and her. I was impressed by their hospitality. At the end of the evening, we had an original sati at a local restaurant and it was a very satisfying evening. Without him I would not have known about such a wonderful place. title: Explore Kampung Baru with a Local
translated_review: If you re in Kuala Lumpur, you have to book this tour. Fuad and his family are some of the kindest, most generous people we ve met in Malaysia. We spoke a lot with Fuad about the benefits and challenges of KL s fast development and what it means for his neighborhood and those who have lived there for generations. We also had a wonderful time having dinner with his wife and daughter, who are SO lovely and very easy to talk to. It was so interesting to learn more about Malay culture and customs from them, and share a bit about our own life as well. We were also lucky enough to share in some of their Eid celebrations, as it was a few days after the holiday. We got to taste their customary foods and desserts, and the food was amazing. All in all, very happy we booked this tour our KL experience would not have been the same without them!

title: Explore Kampung Baru with a Local
translated_review: This tour shows you they way generations of Malay lived and still live today!!! This Baru surrounded by a huge concrete jungle! Yet its serene, simple and unbelievably quiet!! Learned about why homes were built the way they are currently. Learned about families lifestyles. This Baru is SURROUNDED by tall sky scrappers yet it retains its culture and Malay simple style of living! The BEST was being invited into Fuads home. We chilled in his family home and learned about his personal lifestyle and about his family! We were lucky enough to meet his father, who graces us with a few songs while playing the guitar! We had some food in his home at his dining table as we chatted! Fuad is gracious, generous, funny, laid back down to earth, and humble! After we left his home, we stopped by his place of worship and learned about the Baru religion. After we stopped at a local streetfood vender and had some great satay!! My cell service was not working, so Fuad used his phone to order us a Grab ride back to our hotel. This is the type of guy he is!!! I highly recommend this tour if you want to immerse yourself into the real Malay culture!!

title: Gani Famous Pasembur
translated_review: 1st time to Gani Pasembur in Gurney Drive hawker centre on a Wednesday night.The hawker centre is busy and staffy as usual Almost all hawker centre stalls practice self serving. We need to order, pay, and serve our own food.The staff are very passionate. welcoming and greeting customer who approaching their stall.All ingredients are fried. I chose 2 Taufu, 2 fried beancurd slices, and 1 fried onion ball. Proceed for cutting and topping with cucumber turnip I ask for extra portion and some sauce, then make payment and bring it back to our seats to enjoy it. My plate cost RM9.The sauce is not the traditional potato Pasembur sauce. It is more towards the Indian rojak peanuts sauce it has some spiciness but more sweetness in it.The cucumber and turnips give some refreshing taste and bring down some greasiness. It is definitely the right decision to get extra portion of it.Although it is not the potato sauce Pasembur I prefer, but overall OK.
