title: Gani Famous Pasembur
translated_review: Update 8 12 2022 We want to try pasemboq which many have recommended Padang Kota Lama. This dining area is like a normal food court, there are several other dining stalls in one building. If you have already sat at any table, the waiter will come to take your order but be reminded that to order pasembur you have to queue at the shop. Recommended pasemboq at this food court are Gani Femes Pasembur and Husin Pasembur. We chose to try Gani Pasembur first this time. The pasemboq sauce is thick and has a lot of peanuts that are not completely crushed to give it a crunchy texture. The scallops are also still crispy even though they are exposed and after the sauce has been poured. Besides that, we also tried the grilled scallops. The stall is next to Gani Pasembur. The scallop dipping sauce enhances the taste of grilled scallops. We also ordered cendol for water. For me the cendol is delicious but it still can't match the cendol at the MITC Melaka mosque. Overall, there are various other food menus that can be served at this food court such as goreng2, laksa, kuey tiaw etc. The only main menu to try here is pasembur.

title: Gani Famous Pasembur
translated_review: Why this remains popular is a complete mystery to me. The commercialisation and mediocrity of the food quality cannot possibly justify the 3.8 star score. While the selection is extensive, many are lazy convenient choices typical of yongtaufu and honestly cannot be considered suitable for pasembur. I mean when did store bought processed crab sticks, fishball and hotdogs become part of pasembur? The taste was bland even for the fried stuff and some were not even fresh eg. sourish tofu .They really took their time lazily cutting up my selection and drenched it with the most shockingly red kuah ! It almost looked unreal and artificial colouring was definitely used given the intensity. The diabetic inducing sauce was sugary syrupy sweet with just a hint of spiciness. Absolutely no kick or aroma. The only redeeming factor is the generous portion of shredded jicama and cucumbers.My cucur udang, two tofu, two potatoes, boiled egg costs RM16. Would not say it s highway robbery but it comes pretty close. Would not eat here again. The pasembur on Lebuh Union nearby is by far more authentic, tastier and definitely a better deal.

Her cheerful colleague then entertained us to the delicious melons passion fruits which were superb in view of the super hot weather ! Don t forget to try out their popsicles. I love the mango, passion fruits yogurt flavour. It was super refreshing !We left with lots of purchase wonderful memories ! Will be back again ! Thank you so much Jasmine Team !

title: Hami EcoFarm
translated_review: Four days ago, I had a wonderful and educational visit to this Japanese melon farm! Many thanks to Chai Wan, who patiently explained the entire farming process from seedling cultivation and land preparation to harvesting! They also have golden passion fruit and a fishpond! The entrance fee is RM10. Plenty of cantaloupe, passion fruit, and cantaloupe popsicles are provided for everyone to sample! Definitely worth a visit! Advance booking is recommended.

title: Hami EcoFarm
translated_review: A few days ago, I visited Perak. My friend recommended a melon farm, but I was initially hesitant about visiting a remote area with a plastic factory. The person I spoke to on the phone wasn t very helpful, which added to my doubts. However, upon arrival, I was greeted with an unbelievably warm and charming smile from Ms. Bristi, a PhD candidate currently managing the farm. She explained the entire process in such a detailed and educational manner. The melons were incredibly sweet and aromatic beyond anything I had imagined.The combination of Ms. Bristi s expertise and the hospitality at Hamiecofarms made the tour exceptional. I highly recommend visiting, especially with kids they ll love it!

translated_review: Disappointing and Uncomfortable ExperienceOne of the darker sides of visiting Sabah. We had an unpleasant experience at this location where a group of young children seemed to monopolize the parking area. They aggressively approached us for money I gave to one boy, but suddenly more than five others surrounded us, all asking for money. They wouldn t let us move until we gave to each of them, making the situation very uncomfortable and concerning.Despite it being a paid parking area, we were still pressured into giving money to these kids. We were worried they might do something to our rental car, so we decided to leave the place entirely. It s truly disheartening and left a very negative impression.I strongly advise those using rental cars to avoid places like this. It s shocking that such behavior is allowed, and it raises serious questions about what the local authorities are doing. A real shame and a big failure in local management. Sabah deserves better

title: Handicraft Market
translated_review: The Filipino Market in Kota Kinabalu is a wonderful place to visit. It is divided into sections handicrafts, fruits, native snacks and fresh seafood. At night, a part of the market becomes a night food market. The people in the food market are actually nice but can be pushy. But no need to fear, just buy what you want. Do not buy what you don t want.The set meals at the night food market are affordable. 35 MYR for 2 people. You get 4 different dishes of shrimp, squid, fish and vegetables and 2 servings of rice. It is really good for its price.If you order fresh seafood to be cooked, then it is very expensive. You will be charged cost of seafood plus service fee. One dish can already cost you 70 MYR.So choose wisely. People are not dishonest, they can help you choose. Ask them how much everything costs before agreeing.The night food market can be smokey. It is muddy when in rains. If you are picky about places, this is not for you. Personally, i like its charm.

title: Handicraft Market
translated_review: The handicraft market in Kota Kinabalu can be underwhelming if you re seeking authentic, locally made crafts, as many of the items available are mass produced imports. However, it is part of the larger market complex that includes vibrant sections for food, vegetables, and fruits, offering a variety of local produce and delicacies that reflect Sabah s rich culture. If you have time, it s still worth a visit for the overall atmosphere and the chance to sample traditional foods or purchase fresh, exotic fruits.For those specifically interested in authentic handicrafts, consider visiting the Mari Mari Cultural Village or the Sabah Art Gallery, where you can find traditional crafts made by local artisans. Additionally, the Gaya Street Sunday Market is a great spot to explore for more unique, locally crafted souvenirs alongside a variety of other authentic products. These alternatives provide a better opportunity to support local artisans and experience genuine Sabahan culture.

title: Handicraft Market
translated_review: This market opening at 9am and closing at 8pm. Even it is look like a lot of thing for sale, but hardly to find what you want. Very few interesting items can found , but u have to go almost every stall through. Mostly same things. And what most disappointing, most of the souvenirs there import from neighbouring country, just with sign Sabah on it. That is why very expensive and not much can get discounted. Make sure you bargain the price almost double! Some are very friendly and give good price.

title: Handicraft Market
translated_review: This market was a lot of fun!! Especially being the only foreigner shopping around and checking out the local shops and vendors there. They have a huge selection of fruits, vegetables, pastries, dry goods, and a lot more. Big selection of rices and some funky looking pastas that I ve never seen before. The prices are definitely hard to beat. It s much much cheaper than going to any grocery stores in the area. The locals were very friendly and warm, always smiling and organizing their goods that are for sale.Go and check it out! It was a good experience walking through the market and interacting with the locals.

title: Handicraft Market
translated_review: We leisurely meandered through the KK Kota Kinabalu Handicraft Centre, also referred to as the Filipino Market or Anjung Kinabalu, on a Saturday. The market boasts a vast array of souvenirs, including Sabah pearls in various shapes and sizes. We even bought a T shirt for RM 40. There were stunning batiks for sale, along with fresh fruits, vegetables, dried snacks, and everything else you d expect at a market. Notably, along the front of the market, male tailors were busy at work with their sewing machines, adding a unique touch to the vibrant atmosphere. It was easy to visit, as our apartment was in The Shore, just across the road. The market operates daily from around 8 00 AM to 10 00 PM.

title: Historical, Heritage & Cultural Walks George Town
translated_review: Let s make it simple I had not a good idea about Penang prior to my visit and did not wanted to skip the highlights. The booked tour was more than I could have hoped for. To the reasons Tiger is extremely knowledgable and an excellent storyteller. You start the tour with Penang as a city, it s political relations to the Anrainer states and the various tricks the colonial powers manovered amount the political environment in SEA. Furthermore after a delicious local breadfeast you will learn about the Chinese, Indian and Malyan culture in Pengang and visit the respective districts. That is a short sentence for a long tour here, for the Chinese culture alone you will visit a temple, understand the mythology behind, learn about the various accents of the Chinese provinces and importance and composition of Chinese names and ancestral lineage. Same can be said about little India or about the Malyan culture and ways of Islam. Additionally you will learn about the Architekture, Feng Shui elements in the constructions or Chinese home or Indian Mythology. To keep it short if you have a desire to learn more about Penang book here with Tiger. Our tour lasted longer than expected for the one to reasons that nobody wanted to interrupt the flow of informations and secondary because Tiger had in between stepped in in a a local quarrel, solved the situation and called the police and had the professionalism to immidately switch back after to explain the chinese legal legislation. System back in the times. Definitly book here!

Not only did he do this through the walking tour and stories, but through cuisine and architecture. This tour was beyond value for money easy to do, always in a shaded or ventilated area and nothing is too far. Even if you re not a history buff, Tiger will engage you with his anecdotes. What a wonderful time! Thank you a million time over, Tiger!

title: Historical, Heritage & Cultural Walks George Town
translated_review: What an experience! Tiger was super friendly, knowledgeable and passionate about providing deep context into the history of Penang. Would highly recommend this tour to anyone who likes to learn a bit about a place with a local before touring by themselves really helps see the city differently.

title: Historical, Heritage & Cultural Walks George Town
translated_review: Wow! This tour was the best one I have ever attended and I have been on many throughout my travels! Tiger is such a wonderful person. Here come the reasons why He knows so much about history and his country He includes one by asking questions He prepared a rich breakfast,and explained the different sorts of food He was super talkative and I think that he truly loves what he is doing! He is funny and a great story teller He brought us to a temple and explained everything with patience and enthusiasm And what I also recognized and liked very much that he never rushed us at all. Result I highly recommend to everyone who is in Penang and wants to get to know the history of it and some more knowledge about Malay in general, do not hesitate to book this wonderful tour. Maybe at the end you even find new friends,like I did. Thank you so much Tiger. I will always remember you as a great, open minded and lovely person

title: Historical, Heritage & Cultural Walks George Town
translated_review: Wow, what an amazing experience I have a completely new understanding of Penang thanks to this tour. Tiger is simply a wealth of information about everything from history and architecture to food and living heritage and you ll get some delightful surprises along the way. My only regret is I m not in Penang long enough to go on his other tours!

title: Historical, Heritage & Cultural Walks George Town
translated_review: You can tell Tiger is super knowledgeable and passionate about Penang history and he wants to share that with you! You can learn hundreds of years Penang history in a few hours!

It wasn t easy, but with the guidance of the wonderful instructor, Chong, I felt supported every step of the way. He really helped bring out my creativity, and I ended up with a piece of self made art I m proud to display for the first time ever! It was such a fulfilling way to spend the afternoon, and I highly recommend giving it a try.

I think it s a great and original experience to get more into the Malaysian traditions.

title: Jadi Batek - Batik fashion gifts wholesaler retailer
translated_review: If you are looking for a fun and unique experience to do while traveling in Kuala Lumpur, this workshop is highly highly recommended. The instructors were knowledgeable, patient, and enthusiastic about teaching us the techniques of Batek

title: Jadi Batek - Batik fashion gifts wholesaler retailer
translated_review: It cost us rm80 each for our experience here, and we brought a tourist friend with us and we felt it was priced high but ok for the service and the environment. It was clean, the staff were very helpful and explained everything to us and off we went. We picked from their many stencils and traced in pencil the lines for our drawing, then they showed us how to use the wax to draw. We had to split our experience in two days due to our time constraints, and the staff were very accommodating. They helped go through our wax outlines again to make sure there were no gaps and leaks. The next day, we came back to paint with the dye. We were told that the dye is industrial dye, so there s no chance it ll wash out, which we were very assured by. Three days later. We came back to collect our work, nicely packaged and ready to go! We all chose t shirts you can pick a tote if you want and we love them!! Very pleased with the experience and will bring my future tourist friends here too!

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: A little homestead that is reasonable for small kids. The farm is an interactive experience because we can feed the ostriches and ride them. The best creature asylum of all time. The worker is extremely courteous. A very enjoyable experience that I will definitely repeat.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: A lovely petite ostrich farm. Expect to spend around 20 40 minutes here. The goat can be pretty pushy especially when they see snacks. They re not aggressive at all but for kids, animals can be daunting Overall, a lovely and unique experience.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: First of all, the Waze app will bring you to the gate that is closed. And they expect you to read a small sign that asked you to use Google map to enter the farm thru another way.When we reached, we were greeted by a couple where we were asked we can only parked our car outside the compound.Once we entered the farm we bought 3 packets of food to feed the Ostrich , surprisingly we got a shock when we found out they have also released the goats out in the wild. Hence, my children who are really young 7 12 years of age we re intimidated by the goats and one of them was only definitely taller than my kids. So they were rushing and going after the food like hungry animals. I was taken aback and I did try to ask the owner or keeper of the farm to get the goats back to where they belong, but the guy said it s still not time yet to asked the goats to go back in.There were around 4 5 ostriches on both sides of us. I felt we were being attacked. Hence, I have no choice but to throw all the feeds on the ground so that the animals will just leave us alone.Instead of showing my kids how are these ostriches being brought up and live, we end up leaving in around 10 15 mins of our stay.P.S. It started drizzling while this was happening. Hence, I felt it could be a God s sign that I should leave the place immediately with my family.First and the last time!

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: It was a nice experience to drive into this area from the town. The farm is situated within the compound of State Veterinary, also quite a bit to see here.The ostrich farm is about the size of 10 football fields. The place is green and serene. RM 10 for adult is considered cheap.We spent almost 2 hours here. We fed the goats and ostriches, we stand on the ostrich eggs to experience the strength of the egg ourself. We then watched other patrons riding the ostrich pity! . We then walked a bit to see an arrogant pony horse. Then watched ostrich race bit lame . Made U turn to exit, and visited the souvenir shop.Parking can be little difficult during peak as the space is very limited with no designated parking. The crew members are good, they are committed to what they do. Overall, it was a good experience.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: It s a sad place where everything is run down and shabby. We came here hoping for a fun experience but it s just a few ostriches and emus. They charge RM8 per person and give each a packet of dried corn kernels which you can use to feed the ostriches. When we were there, the goats and cows were back at their sheds so we didn t see any of these animals. After 10 minutes of feeding the ostriches we walked over to a shack where a friendly Indian man started telling us about the ostriches. He was also selling ostrich oil derived products like hair growth spray and all purpose cream for skin. The shack was so decrepit and had seen better days. I left wondering why we don t have higher quality standards for our domestic tourism? I didn t feel I got any value from the visit!Update Surprisingly, despite the sad state of affairs at the ostrich farm, the highlight of the trip was the ostrich oil that I bought. It works really well on scars and is quite moisturizing and you can see its effects after 3 4 days of application. They do sell their oil on Shopee and my sister did order a few more bottles of the ostrich oil about a month after.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

The size of the egg is as big as an adult face. You need to pay RM8 for entrance and get a free packet of dry corn to feed the ostriches.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

My cousin s hand hurt until blood as the ostrich see the food and it come suddenly and bit. Not a good experience for us. The enter fee is RM8 per person, is very expensive as you just stayinside there about 10 15 minutes. No parking also, put your car beside the road only.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: It's worth it to bring a family to learn about animals. There are ostriches, goats, geese. I really want to let these animals come close to us. There are packs of dried corn for RM1 each to feed all the animals. You can ride the ostrich again! Entrance fee is only RM10. Ready with a tour guide. 100 more worth than going to an expensive zoo and the animals are not taken care of even in a state of torture. It's very friendly and clean here. I've been here twice, I don't feel at a loss, I don't get bored at all.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

This area is clean and does not smell like excrement especially. Fresh air

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: The farm is just a short drive away from the city of KL. It is a family friendly farm, great for both kids and adult. The farm offers an interactive experience as we are allowed to feed the ostrich and ride on them. Certificate is provided once ride is completed.It is fascinating to witness how huge as ostrich egg can be, also there are also stalls selling dishes featuring the ostrich eggs.It is fun day, but advisable to bring portable mini hand fan just in case for the hot weather.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm
translated_review: The place is not clearly marked. Waze navigation missed the spot But we caught a glimpse of the only banner near the actual place and after asking around we arrived at the farm. You will have to drive a distance to reach the ostrich farm after spending quite some time at this underrated mini zoo Please do come here and the kids will have fun with a lot of animals. They can actually get close and feed them. Highly recommend. This can easily be transformed into a mini zoo commercially.

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jelita Ostrich Farm

title: Jeti Pak Awang River Cruise
translated_review: Well, this holiday to Kuala Terengganu felt even more special because I was able to try my first boat ride at the Pak Awang River Cruise Jetty. From the very beginning, the atmosphere at the jetty has given the feeling of calmness of the cool river breeze, wide view of the water, and neat and comfortable boats for the passengers. When the boat starts moving, the trip down the river really opens the eyes to the beauty of Kuala Terengganu from a different angle. We passed through some iconic locations such as the beautiful and sparkling Crystal Mosque from the riverside, truly a landmark of the city. The Islamic Civilization Park clearly shows replicas of famous Islamic buildings from around the world. Pulau Duyong Pulau Raja is a traditional village by the river full of calm atmosphere. Bridge Take a unique experience passing under this modern bridge while enjoying the view around the city. The most interesting thing is when passing through the bridge pillars decorated with colorful mural paintings. It is not only beautiful, but also a creative and artistic boat route sign. The whole trip feels relaxed, suitable for families, couples or tourists who want to see the other side of Kuala Terengganu. With a reasonable fee, this experience is really worth it. Additional tip If possible, choose evening or dusk. The setting of the sunset along the river makes this experience more romantic and relaxing. To be honest, our family is very satisfied. This is an activity that must be tried when coming to Kuala Terengganu not only can calm the mind, but also add to the wonderful memory of the holiday.

translated_review: A new experience when going to Kuala Terengganu. It is recommended to book early before coming. Even early in the week is good because if you walk in, you have to be crowded, you are tired of waiting for your turn. Trip in 45 minutes per hour. The suggested time is after Asar. It's not hot if the weather is beautiful, the view is also beautiful. But if you want to watch the sunrise at the Angkut Bridge, 6.30am is also fine. 1 boat can fit 12 people. Many people go and take 1 shock boat. Do not mix with others. But if there are not many people, he has to ride with someone else. An adult RM30, a child RM15. It's a bit expensive but it's worth it to get a boat ride. This place is close to the state museum. If you want it easy, go to the state museum then continue with river cruising. There are restaurants nearby. Toilets are also available for visitors. Auto close if it rains.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: I spent 3 days and two nights with host Mrs. Jubaidah and families at the beginning of May. They provided a warm and friendly environment for all their guests. The food was marvellous with something new to try at every meal. The catfish and river prawns were lovely as were the noodles and chicken .... It was lovely to be able to relax and get a feel for life in the village on the river. With our guides we saw a wonderful variety of birds at least 4 species of hornbill, an asian paradise flycatcher, kingfishers, eagles and many more. We saw an orang outang, proboscus monkeys and silver langur and many macaques. We also came across evidence of elephants. It was a great experience.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: If you want to truly authentic experience with the original people of the Kinabatangan River and support their community, you want to stay with the Bilit Village Homestay. It is a wonderful community based program supporting 12 families that participate equally. There is no benefit to staying with one family over another as they are all wonderful people and share in this business endeavor equally as they do in so many parts of their lives. We stayed with Hamdah who was like a sweet mother to us and her husband, Leam was our river guide. The homestay was basic, mattress on the floor with mosquito nets but spotless, amazing food locally sourced where possible. We only did one night but wish we had done two as the girls that also stayed their second night with us saw pygmy elephants which isn t an everyday occurrence. We had late afternoon and morning river boat cruises and Leam was incredibly observant and patient. We probably passed 5 6 other guided groups from the expensive river lodges but I m sure we saw more wildlife as Leam was so patient and keen to the animals, including a crocodile under some river brush that at last 3 boats passed! The silver leaf monkeys were curious enough to hop on the bow of our boat and the proboscis monkeys were amazing but the real treat was seeing an orangutan swinging through the trees with afternoon backlight! Our driver, Lee picked us up in Sandakan and drove us 3 hours with a stop at the Gomantong Caves on the way. Lee loved to practice his english and was so appreciative of the job he had as our driver...so wonderful to see such dedication and love for his job. He made our long ride to the village seem so trivial with stories and historical facts, answering all our questions the best he could. The whole experience with Bilit is a once in a lifetime experience. It costs a fraction of what any of the river lodges cost and includes transportation, meals and guide excursions plus more importantly the money is supporting a village and improving their infrastructure....which they need to support our business. Booking was easy by email, just google Bilit Village homestay and trust you ll have a wonderful time. Lovely people.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: Most of the reviews here recommend to Masruna House and i m agree with you all. Seriously i m enjoyed 3D2N trip at Masruna House. Unforgetable Local experience with Masruna family. The strategic location of the house facing the River Kinabatangan indeed a beautiful scenery. We see so many wildlife on our river cruise jungle tracking. The food was so delicious super fantastic! I was very lucky had tried the traditional clothing of the tribe their called Orang Sungai and Only available in Masruna House! You can directly booking with Mrs.Masruna by E mail masrunahomestay1234 gmail.com Call Whatsapp 016 587 4323

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: My boyfriend and I spent one night with the Minon family in Bilit. A very confortable stay great food! and all together a great experience. We went on two river cruises and one short jungle trek. It s all very professional, and Jumal was able to pick us up at Sepilok and drive us to the ariport the next day making the transportation easy and enjoyable .Special thanks to the orginaizer driver Ju, and our amazing guide Liam great meeting both of them. Would highly recommend doing this if you are in Sabah!

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: My boyfriend and I visited Bilit Village for two nights on the 31st of august until the 2th of september 2015. We had a great time! The village is placed right next to the Kinabatang river and has jungle and trekking traces placed right in the backyard. We went on three boat cruises, one early in the morning and two in the afternoon. We went on one jungle trek after dark and one during day time. Our guide knew a lot about local nature, plants and animal life. We saw lots of proboscis monkeys, and tropical birds. We even saw a group of pygmea elephants bathing in the lake. Such a great experience! We also saw a crocodile. Our grand finale was weaking up at 04.30 a.m. the last day to climp a hill behind the village and watch the sunset with a 360 degree view.Our host family was were helpful, they arranged transport to and from the village and welcomed us to their house. They cooked great malaysian food of fish from the lake and fruit from the village. The room was simple but clean. If you are looking for a less touristed way of experiencing village life next to the jungle, this is a great choice!

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: We loved our four day stay in Bilit with Marino and Halimah! We laughed a lot, shared stories, enjoyed cross cultural discussions, and reveled in the awesome wildlife river cruises two times each day. We saw orangutan, proboscis, hornbills, macaques, silver leaf monkeys, etc. You can book directly with Marino and Halimah by e mailing marino4903 gmail.com and ikma01 yahoo.com or by calling 019 883 5714 or 017 890 9451. I d give them some advance notice at least a week. We were treated to delicious food including pumpkin curry and banana cake, mmmm. We slept soundly on a comfortable mattress under a mosquito net and our room had a rotating fan. Their grandkids were visiting and we really enjoyed hanging out with them too. Remember, bucket showers and no air conditioning, but there is a western style toilet. Try to learn some Malay before you arrive.Staying with locals is the best experience. Bilit is beautiful and so were our hosts.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: We saw more wildlife than we had even hoped to see, loads of Pygmy elephants on both days, proboscis monkeys, long tail macaques, silver leaf monkeys, hornbills, crocodiles, even an orang utan, the list goes on...! The homestay itself was really lovely, the family were so friendly and welcoming and made every effort to entertain and accommodate us. Ding, our guide was great at spotting wildlife and also incredibly kind to us at the longhouse. He even took us fishing one evening for no extra cost, just because we had expressed an interest, and was very patient at showing us how it was done the local way. Cannot recommend this Homestay programme highly enough, it has been our highlight of Sabah!

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: We stayed with Masruna and her family in Bilit Village and it was the highlight of our trip! We drove from Kota Kinabalu to Bilit Village and was welcomed by Masruna s husband and nephew Rusman and Amirul just outside of the village who led us to their house. We saw orangutans, proboscis monkeys, all sorts of birds, crocodiles, snakes and more! The meals were delicious and we felt so welcomed and as though Masruna and her family really wanted us to enjoy our stay and see lots of animals while we were there we noticed some other families who were not all that interested! . Their English is really good and they enjoy making conversation. We definitely recommend staying with Masruna you won t regret it! A fantastic experience and a great way to see some incredible animals in the wild! Email masrunahomestay gmail.com

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: We stayed with our two kids 14, 11 for 2 nights, and we had a fabulous time. A big thank you to Siti, Ding, their extended family and other lovely people at the village for making us feel so welcome and bending over backwards to make our stay great. Thanks to Lim too for being a great guide. Our kids loved hanging out with the other kids. Loved the river cruises, the yummy food, the jungle walk to the foot nibbling fish lake and getting to share Hari Raya celebrations. Added on Gomantong cave on our way back to Sandakan and glad we did. Deng did a slow drive there and spotted little pigs, a big lizard and Macaque monkey on the way. Plus there was an orang utan mum and baby hanging out by the entrance to the cave. If you need air con, lifts and hot running water, this isn t the place for you. But for us, it was the best part of our holiday hands down.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: We wanted to stay with a local family more a more unique and true experience. The overall experience was really nice, friendly family and great guide. Food prepared by the grand mother and the mother was very tasty. Be prepared for very basic amenities, simple matress on the floor, and no real bathroom, just a bucket in the toilet as a shower. but that s how the people there live and so should you for a few days. We went 3 times on the family s boat so spot animals, we saw plenty and the highlight was the elephants which were by the riverside, one even swam the river just in front of our boat which was amazing. We were only 4 on the boat, me, my friend and another girl who also stayed in the family. This is great because you re a very small group compared to the packed boats that you see coming from the hotels nearby.

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: winner!

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: Wonderful people, fantastic hospitality, amazing wildlife!!!

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: Wonderufl time at Bilit homestay

title: Kampung Bilit Homestay
translated_review: Worth for a visit and great stay at murinos house!

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A good place for educational purpose, photoshoot, videography and memories. As a Sarawakian, we should be visiting this place at least once evey 2 year. I was at a disadvantage since I lived very far from Santubong. Luckily for me, I get to

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A good place to visit for those who wants to experience the different cultures traditional houses of Sarawak. There are mini activities for you to participate, local snacks making selling, costume rental for photography and a cultural show note the timing . Certain time of the year, this is a host venue for the annual rainforest musical festival other concerts, so you can plan accordingly if interested.Try to purchase the tickets ahead of time especially if it s a public holiday to avoid the queue but make sure you are certain of making the visit as the tickets are non refundable after purchase. Sarawakian prices differ so it s best to purchase upfront not online.1 star missing because there is lack of interaction in most houses, rumah bidayuh being the best with great informative presentation by the host. Also the cultural show should have some improvement than the same program moves with no change vs 3 decades ago

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A great place to experience the diverse cultures of Sarawak. Unique ethnic architecture, cultural performances, and fun optional activities like tasting local snacks and trying on traditional costumes with extra charge RM5 10

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A must visit if you are ever in Sarawak! We spent half a day here, and it s awesome learning about the tribes and their way of living. There s a restaurant located inside that you can have lunch too.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A must visit destination when in Kuching, Sarawak.Sarawak spans over 1,200 km from end to end, making it challenging to explore all its diverse regions. The Sarawak Cultural Village offers a unique opportunity to experience the rich heritage of the state in one place. It showcases the traditions, architecture, and way of life of the many ethnic groups that call Sarawak home bringing the essence of these distant communities together in an accessible and engaging way.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A must visit if you re in Kuching! The cultural dance show, traditional ethnic houses, and historical exhibits are really interesting. There are also activities like mini games and sago processing that you can join or watch.The restaurant food is also worth mentioning.. very delicious! You can choose from the a la carte menu or go for set packages that come with the entry ticket.It s about 45 minutes from the city centre. Getting there by Grab is easy, but getting a ride back in the late evening can be tricky, so plan ahead.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A place must visit when you are in Kuching. Today just come back from the village and heard a sad news, fire burn down two houses in the village.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A place to understand Sarawak diverse ethnic and culture back in the old days.A top spot for tourist and local, bring umbrella, portable fan, sunglasses and hat to beat the scorching sun and sometimes rain

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A very educational trip.We can learn about the sarawak ethnics, their traditional house, music instruments, etc.It s located close to Damai.We can walk through these ethnics houses about 1,5 hr and then we hurried to go to their main theatre to watch performances.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A wonderful experience. Feel the vibes of Malaysia s ancient indigenous peoples through recreations of activities and environments. The show features fun and authentic performances that engage the audience. Worth the price of admission.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: After entering, we can see a lot of houses from different culture. We can visit each house to see the architecture and traditional equipments that belong to each tribe. There is a big lake at the middle of the village. The location is quite far from the Kuching city, but is very close to Damai beach.Recommended for locals to go around and get to know more about local cultures. However, the price is very unfair to the foreigners tourists. The entry price for foreign adult is 150rm and foreign kids is 90rm. It is better to visit other cheaper cultural sites.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It is worth bringing children here. Walk in Sarawakian tickets are cheap. Ohsemmm show, it feels like the show is over. Amused. Come to Kch, don't forget to stop there.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Good place to see lots of culture and history that you won t see in books. Fun to hang out with your friends or family

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Had a wonderful experience at the Sarawak Cultural Village which included a nice walking tour of the various Sarawak indigenous tribal homes and culminating with a nice cultural show.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: I went to the Rainforest World Music Festival RWMF . It was very crowded with both local and foreign visitors. It is a very nice place to visit. And only 2 hours drive from Kuching city. The view is spectacular, and you will feel connected to nature once you visit there.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Improvement for carpark entrance signs of the place shld be more visible esp for visitors from overseas drivers.Entrance fee for foreigners is quite steep just for 5 or 6 longhouses 1 show.The drinks in the restaurant are affordable.I love the cultural dance show!

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It is a very good place about Sarawakian culture and the various ethnic groups inhabiting this beautiful state. I recommend you to reserve your tickets from the website beforehand, where you can find the admission lunch package RM 125 pax . Even though it s quite expensive and the access quite remote it s very hard to find Grab from there , it s a place worth visiting. There s also a cultural show every 11 AM and 4 PM.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It was a good overall experience. Love how they have live cooking in each tribe house making it more lively. The design of each tribe house is unique, I believe God has created good natural born architects for each tribe to be able to build such houses. The theatre show at 11.30am was entertaining, however, the speaker quality was not so good and I could not hear the narration well enough to understand. And during seating time before the start of the show, please make sure you provide ample lighting to avoid trip hazard. I sprained my ankle while going down the steps because it was too dark.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It s a must visit! Sarawak Cultural Village offers such a beautiful experience you get to explore the unique traditions of seven major local ethnic groups and see firsthand how they build their traditional houses. They also sell delicious local desserts, adding to the authentic atmosphere.I especially enjoyed my conversation with Cecilia at the Rumah Cina. She was incredibly warm, welcoming, and took the time to explain the plants and cultural details so passionately. She truly deserves a raise for her dedication and hospitality!

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It s best to rent a vehicle to get there, as the village is about 40 45 minutes drive from Kuching city and public transport options are limited. There are live cultural performances at 11 00 AM and 4 00 PM, so plan your visit around those

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: It s definitely a tour worth doing to not only see but also taste a cultural city. Each house welcomes you with unique and freshly backed traditional treats.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: If you go to Kuching, you must stop here..arriving here has already covered Sarawak..the ticket purchased includes the Cultural Show

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Loved my visit! The cultural show was amazing and the highlight of the trip. The tribal houses were interesting to explore, and overall it was well worth the entrance fee.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: OA1nbd

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Okay. But it's not worth paying RM90. It should be RM30 only. I went inside, no one explained the location except in the Iban house and the Malay house. The aunty at the Chinese house was very rude when I tried to ask her to explain

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: One of the more popular tourist attraction in KCH. It s about a 40 mins drive from KCH waterfront and there s two show timing at 1130 and 430 so arrange your arrival near this time.We arrived around 0930 and it was more than enough time to tour and hang around the 7 houses before entering the theatre at 1100. The houses were very educational and some of them sell local snacks too which was surprisingly good.Show was not bad and the theatre was air con. Pro tip is instead of arranging for tour to get you here which can be quite costly, rent a car and get the ticket yourself to save some money.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: A very meaningful experience. Healing with Mount Santubong in the background. Seeing the multi-ethnic culture on Earth.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Quiet set of houses representing Sarawak s traditional living quarters. Could do with more signage and explanation to make it more meaningful. Each site is staffed by 1 3 minders who also demonstrate traditional cooking methods. Can buy

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Run Down, and lack of maintenance.I visited this place 20 years ago, it was so great and I was so impressed. When I return this time, I was very disappointed. They collected so expensive entrance fees and yet didn t maintain the place well.Sarawak is the state rich with cultures, and all the performers are so great. If the management didnt seriously consider to re development this place, soonest or later this place will not sustain.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: So, there s a new concrete building next to the houses. The show performance is awesome, but it feels like the same old routine. We definitely need more trees or plants around there.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: some of houses a bit torn and old, not sturdy structure, for visitor pls be careful, pls wear shoes, sunglasses. umbrella and comfortable it could be very2 hot humidity that area is quite high but windy area, beautiful view surrounding with mountains and jungles green, a lot facilities provided, such as washroom, water station, staff very friendly and helpful, smile and welcomes u,

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: The cultural show lasts about 1 hour without boring and keeps the guests engaged all through the show. Its a must visit . The different houses are also interesting. There is a restaurant with traditional food available.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: The last time I was here 20 odd years ago and it still holds the charm. Love the traditional dance Tarian Alu Alu just need to get into rhythm and let go of the thoughts on the fact that your ankle might get clamped off The place holds 6 main cultures in Sarawak Chinese, Malay Sarawak, Ibans, Bidayuh, Melanau and Orang Ulu ie Penans, Kayan, Kelabit. The blow pipe hunter is from the Penan tribe which I feel is such a skill! Love the cultural performance show and using only the traditional musical instruments. A must to visit when you are in Kuching. Though there are two tiers of fee Locals and International.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: The show was excellent and the houses were fantastic. However at the time of this review, the largest and most beautiful structure Bidayuh longhouse just burned to the ground about an hour ago, along with the massive grand hall where real human skulls were kept. We just spoke to the village chief in that building a few hours before and he was great. We are all truly saddened by the loss, but at least we ll have the memories. Note, early reports say the blaze started in the great hall where a fire is always burning, smoking the skulls. Apparently it s bad luck not to keep them in the smoke. Strange, as the building has been there for decades with no issues. I wonder what the investigations will find. Can t be arson, everyone there was lovely and happy....

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: The theatre experience was great. The houses and performances were wonderful. Delicious snacks were available at each stop. Price is quite steep at RM90 for Malaysian and more for foreigners. Removed one star because there was no queue management at ticketing or theatre entrance so it was annoying with cutting queue and what not.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: The whole experience was fun. Superb performances by the dancers and musician. Everyone was friendly and nice. You can also try out different types of kuih that they sell in different houses. Hopefully they can improve the maintenance but overall it was great

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: This is a very special ethnic village where you can see many traditional Malaysian architectures. Here you can watch a traditional Malaysian music and dance performance. The manager here is very kind. Our group went straight from the airport to here, it was quite late, preparing to close. The manager here happened to meet us and when he knew we had come a long way, he immediately took the initiative to invite us to watch the rest of the show without having to buy tickets. What a kind man

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: This place was a good 45 minutes drive away from Kuching city. Worth it if you like learning about different cultures. They had many different longhouse on display. Which house has little exhibits in there and occasionally people cooking and you can buy the snacks they are cooking . The walk between the different houses is quite long for those that mobility is an issue, especially on a hot day. And of course fr some, climbing the narrow steps are quite challenging.Do come for the cultural show as it was quite good. It is daily at 1130 and 4pm. And it last for 45 mins. Carpark is available if you have a rented car. Entry prices are way too steep even for Malaysians . Download the app as it has the map on it.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Very interesting and informative place.This should be maintained well for future generations.It will be more interesting if you have a tour guide. As the the explanation make the visitation more interesting.The performance was so good and impressive. Will visit it again next time.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Very interesting and value for money. You will be able to see all the tribal cultures in Sarawak. A very interesting and entertaining CulturalShow.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Visited Kampung Budaya in Kuching such a cool way to explore Sarawak s rich cultures! Traditional houses, live performances, and hands on activities made it fun and educational. I m sorry I forgot to take pictures of some of the houses only managed to snap a few. Definitely worth a visit!

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Went here during the Harvest and Folklore Festival for free entry. Quite a large space filled with in depth cultural items such as architectures, musical instruments, traditional attires, traditional tools, performances etc from different indigenous ethnic groups located here in sarawak. Furthermore, it encompasses musical instruments from other cultures due to the Sarawak Rainforest Music Festival.We went there in the afternoon and the sun was unforgiving, highly recommend to get some water and an umbrella ready to walk across sites when there are no covers.Overall a pretty good experience!

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Went there for Rainforest World Musical Festival 2025. It is a very nice place which u can explore all the cultures and uniqueness of each races in Sarawak.

title: Kampung Budaya Sarawak
translated_review: Wonderful place to learn about some of the tribes and races that live in Sarawak. Realistic model replica of the different house on a smaller scale. Guides from different native tribes gives good explanations. The cultural show was good and

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: A replica of an ancient Malay village. Wooden houses, complete with simple interior exterior d cor.There are also boats that were once used in that era.They also organize cultural shows on certain days, please consult with them to find out the schedule.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: A very good environment and also this place provides very good service in tourists guide.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Actually....they have a supporting crew...soft spoken and not too angry to teach us about terengganu.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: There is a lot of historical information. From ships and boats to the construction of relatives' houses. Wide and there are interesting activities. Suitable for family information.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Alhamdulillah, very very satisfied to come here. Coincidentally, on the first day, there is a Musing Ketang holiday event..many interesting places, a real picture of the architecture of the old Malay palaces, also traditional game activities, carving art, archery, batik painting, martial arts performances and so on..tell me that we will come again the next day. Thank you to Mr. Hazri, take us on a tour and explain in detail about the Islamic civilization in Terengganu, the meaning behind the architecture of the old historical houses.. our Malay nation was so great in the past. Thank you also to Ms. Hawa, who helped us a lot when trying on traditional clothes. Please take pictures. And also to the other staff, very friendly and also friendly.. thank you all.. God willing, we will come again.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Alhamdulillah...it is the best Terengganu Cultural Village, the best Cultural Tour Activity in conjunction with the best KBT Geset...Thank you for the best treatment for DUP PTSS Students.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Almost three weeks after Hari raya on a week day the place was close. I had truly enjoyed the kutching conter part and was excited to visit this one. I drove from kelantan to find it unexpectedly closed. The staff were all there but only for meal and gathering. Nothing happening at all. Other visit its were like me disappointed, i managed to walk around the site and, i have to admit that it looked unappealing. It consists on just a few old malay houses. A very disappointing experience

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Soothing music and a spacious area but a lot needs to be repaired

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: One of the interesting places in Terengganu...provides a lot of impact and useful information not only for students but also for teachers....students have fun trying various traditional games....the officer on duty also gives good and informative information... smktelokmas

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Astonishing, with the old house vibrant, creating a nostalgic era of 1800, and unique shop lot where they use the old house as a venue, like a blend of modern world and the past. And shout out to the amiexa coffee shop, really great local coffea, different from the others but the taste is great. Hopefully can come here again as the progress of renovation of the muzium still on going.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Lots of real scale house builds that can be entered. However, the historic boat exhibition is not well maintained.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: there are many old palaces near this village. fun, you can enter every other palace to feel the atmosphere of the old days. every palace has different types of home decorations. sell handicrafts, drinks, etc. wide worth coming. the entrance ticket is definitely rm5 per person.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Beautiful museum, Rumah Terengganu style. Had a great time walking around. Just wish we had more time to explore the whole museum. There s a lot of stairs, good workout so you can enjoy kopi nisang guiltfree later. There are small booths of kopi and souvenirs, and vending machines available. All the food kiosks were closed during my visit there.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Best. Went because of the Musing Ketang holiday event 2024. A lot of knowledge was shared from the ignorant to the knowledgeable. A lot of history is shared. Interesting story behind the old house in the village. Can we do more events like this, can we bring boys to show our Terengganu traditional Malay architectural game? Thank you

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Beautiful and clean... Lots of history to learn and lots of interesting things to do

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Come here during the Ramadhan night barakah lamp festival celebration. Satisfied, the children also love it. It is hoped that it will become an annual activity because it is an attraction for local visitors and also from outside Terengganu.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Don t forget to buy their craft and visit the Nelayan House to try the dish.Got to buy ticket before enter.This place just beside Terengganu State Museum.There s a show during holidays and souvenir sold just under the house.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Exciting experience. Super friendly staffs! Insta worthy ambient! Please dont forget to include this place in your trip.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Fun activities enriched with traditional history, music, art, culture, and more. However, I have a few suggestions 1. Install air conditioning in each house. While I understand the concerns regarding electricity costs, it would be extremely helpful during the summer, especially for families with many children, to reduce the risk of heat stroke and provide more comfort.2. Provide more details on each displayed item and the history behind them. I noticed that most of the items, instruments, traditional clothes, etc., inside the houses are only labeled without further historical context. It would be more engaging if technology could be used to offer detailed explanations. These improvements would add great value to the experience.3. I wish there were clearer directions on where to start or what activities are available. The directions were unclear, and I wouldn t have known about the variety of activities if I hadn t asked the staff.Overall, the experience was fun, the staff were super friendly and the events were enjoyable. I hope to see some improvements to the facilities on my next visit.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Gonna repeat my trip to Kampung Budaya Terengganu.my kids love it so much. It s a great place to experience the unique charm and traditions of Terengganu. It serves as a showcase of Terengganu s rich cultural heritage, featuring traditional Malay houses, cultural performances, craft demonstrations, and various traditional activities.. our definition of healing place

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Guide Tour dan staff sini sangat friendly! And really can learn a lot of thing from here. Recommended!!

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Highly recommended to go when there is an event Holiday Holiday Musing Ketang traditional game is really fun. The house is well-maintained and very inspired by Malay culture. Thanks to the best and friendly staff. Ready to explain well about architecture and history. Very knowledgeable. You deserved my compliment Hazri, Hawa, my uncle painted batik, my sister played girls wearing glasses, and my uncle did archery, good luck!!

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: I had a great experience thereNew knowledge unlockedDon t worry about the feeRM 5 adultRM 2 children

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: I love the design of the museum.I hope one day I will be there again.Thank you for the experience, Terengganu.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: I really love this place, so authentic... We went to make friends with the officer there named amirul, he is very friendly, kind, and we even went and he was recommended, which is interesting.. He also told about every village house that is here and its history... He should be given a promotion or an award for giving the best service...

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Its free entrance during the time we are here. I heard they will impose payment soon. We can see many type of traditional house and a big ship. There are various type of traditional and cultural activities such as wood making, batik, anyaman tikar mengkuang. There are booth available to buy some drink during your walk. Parking is the same at museum area. I also try achery activities. Love the experience.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: So the dream arose to have a house made of wood, very beautiful architecture.. there are some places that need to be tidied up. Hope the new, present and future generations are always interested in learning about the old history. Keep recovering what you have.. The best people under the house

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The streets in this area look at cultural villages and traditional houses. Now you pay only Rm5 for adults. Children are free. There are cultural performances at certain times around 3pm. There is a demonstration of making traditional cakes such as lok lik, durian flowers. There are also traditional food sales. Make sure you enter every new house to experience the culture we have. Uncle made the lok lik ready for you to hold the battered wooden mortar. There is also a demonstration of fried crackers in the sand. Delicious. There is a boat port at the end of the river facing the Pulau Sekati bridge. Beautiful. You can see the old boats, bird huts. Before returning, you can try archery. Children must love it.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The cultural village preserves the well-maintained art and cultural heritage houses. There are several houses that are hundreds of years old here. Good for children's learning to get to know our culture. The explanation from the staff is very good. Can try to play topless. Very interesting for a half-day visit here. The ticket price is very reasonable, only rm5 for adults.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This Cultural Village is next to the Terengganu Museum. This place is wide, very wide.. it's really tiring to walk. Make sure to bring water. More or less like in Melaka, only the house is less than Melaka. the area is very poorly maintained, especially the back. The aquarium is closed.. The fishing area behind is quiet. The houses that are there are awake.. you can go in and see the old architecture of the past.. old palaces. Just a cheap ticket.. you can enter.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The Cultural Village is huge. Next to the Terengganu Museum. If you want to go after going to the museum, you can just be a little tired because the museum is big. If you want to walk comfortably, you have to wear sneakers. You can enter the exhibition houses but don't forget to take off your shoes. Btw, the exhibition house is the real house that people put back near the worker's culture village that is the story. When I went that day I could see a martial arts show and be able to shoot archery. They also sell water nearby so if you are thirsty you can buy it. Souvenirs are sold near the bottom of the exhibition house. If you don't want to walk, you can ride like an electric motorbike but for a fee

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Get to know the heritage architecture of the Malay Nation in the past. Experience the beauty, finesse and precision of construction by great skilled craftsmen

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Good service, clear explanation by the officer on duty about old history

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This Cultural Village is very interesting. Very awake and clean. The staff is also a very good guide to try traditional games and tell a little bit about the history of the house. Everyone must come here if they go to Terengganu.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This museum is very big, beautiful and interesting. The building structure is very special, make sure if you go to Kuala Terengganu to stop by this unique museum. You won't regret it

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The biggest museum deserves it! It's fun to walk around in this. The shrine and other facilities are also comfortable

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: nice place..nov 2021 many activities are held in this Terengganu Cultural Village..time of Nov still free entrance..various shapes of traditional houses are there..there are different sales in each house..you can taste the traditional Lok Lik cakes and taste the delicious etok etok cooking..not sure whether it will continue or the Nov 2021 activities only..you can bring a hand fan if you can't stand the heat..

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: OA1nbd

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: A great knowledge experience when you visit this cultural village. His employees are all good. If you come to Terengganu, you must go to this Terengganu culture village

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: First time here. It's really wow.. the building is classic.. and the museum is big. Because we didn't have enough time, we just took pictures outside the museum. That has already been viewed. In front of the museum there is an exhibition of old cars. Can he find out the models used by the state government. If you bring children, it will be fun. There is an entry fee to enter the museum. But I'm not sure how much the price is. You can check directly on the website or call directly.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The new tourism product which is the Terengganu Cultural Village at the State Museum Complex, Losong here started opening today to the public. Terengganu Chief Minister, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar said, the cultural village which has various attractions with the concept of a living museum brings together various arts, culture and heritage from all eight districts in the state.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: really love it! the house is very well maintained. The staff is very nice, there are friendly people who can answer if you have any questions. used to come before, but closed. sustenance can reach here. with a price of RM10, it's seriously worth it!!!

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: I really like this place. Terengganu Cultural Village is a place where various cultures are preserved. I was able to see various traditional houses such as Rumah Bujang Berkembar and Rumah Rimas Dutch. I also saw various traditional boats such as Perahu Payang and Perahu Bidor. The art design of the boat is very meticulous like the shape of an eagle on the boat. It is very beautiful. I can also play traditional games here. I saw my friends playing cocky and cocky. I feel happy because I went on a school trip here but I'm a little sad because I want to go back to come to another place.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: We had a great time at the Terengganu Cultural Village.. If you go to Terengganu, don't miss the opportunity to stop here. The houses in the village are very unique, beautiful and full of cultural value. We got a lot of knowledge and information about Malay history, manners and architecture of the ancient people from the tourism officer Amirul who was on duty and helped our tour guide throughout the tour in the village. Can also record memories in the surrounding area. A very valuable experience.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: I like to see the architecture, it looks like a mosquito.. it looks big, I don't remember how much the entrance ticket is. Historical goods, ok there are a lot of them.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: this place is nice but lacks the charm of a long distance needs a bike service or something suitable to be more tourist friendly.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Very spacious place. Last year I went to the museum but couldn't go here because of the renovation. This year there is a fortune to come to KT again, so we will go here. There are people demonstrating in several places.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: An interesting place for those who enjoy a relaxing leisurely visit. The museum is located next to Kampung Budaya, 2 In 1. Ticket prices are reasonable. Expensive sights and experiences can be created here.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: An interesting place to visit for those visiting Terengganu. The ticket price is also cheap now, you can afford it. Excellent staff service for before, during and after the incident. The explanation given is very clear. Thank you from us who came with 1 bus from Yan, Kedah.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This place is very best.. many activities.. Other houses, other activities.. The palace house is really beautiful in architecture.. The children enjoy coming here.. Visiting while learning.. The place is also beautiful.. If you come to Terengganu, don't forget to stop here..

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This place is next to the Terengganu museum. Tickets can be bought next to the gate before entering. It's cheap. RM5 adults, RM2 children, oku free. Every house has activities. You can see and join. You can go up to the top of the house to see the old architecture. Beautiful, neat and sturdy. For the exterior, wheelchair access is possible, just to go up to the top of the house is not allowed. Because all the stairs and high. But it's okay to look at it from the outside, I'm satisfied.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Terengganu Cultural Village is must go place, you can understand different local village building some play some activities

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The best tour ever end of year 2022. With Group of awesome people from bumi Hang Tuah Melaka having historical time in state of Darul Iman Terengganu . Learnt about many types of houses, busana traditional attire must try on, types of perahu, gasing and traditional music instruments. Definitely will go once again with my family. Our tour liase with handsome men and gorgeous ladies with historical knowledge of every part of the village. Thanks to all team TCV for making our tour very fruitful and memorable. Congratulations!!!

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: The place looks majestic, some area of improvement1 beehive are getting bigger near the main door, kindly remove it before it stung visitor2 need some good mapping on the flow, i am lost what to expect while im in the muzeum3 more video graphics will add some good explanation.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This cultural village centre is next to the impressive state museum. It has various traditional houses on display as well as many styles of boats and ships. The pathways run throughout the well established gardens and along the river.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This place didn t seem to have much going on at all. Most buildings were closed or almost empty. There is barely any interesting information about the buildings. Maybe it is better when there is an event going on but when I visited the place was empty and not really worth visiting at all.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: This place is incredibly underrated! If you re reading reviews and debating whether to visit, take this as your sign to go you won t regret it. It s an engaging and educational experience that s perfect for both kids and adults, offering a deep dive into our culture and history. What sets it apart is the hands on exhibits, allowing everyone to interact and truly immerse themselves. The staff are welcoming, knowledgeable, and genuinely passionate about what they do. Overall, it s an unforgettable experience that s well worth your time!

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Very very good experience Teri Makasi nice people, nice please, easy to laugh play and meet people, they have game and architecture, thanks to the people I meet there I had a very nice day learning about Terengganu culture

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: What a lovely place. It s not only a great open air Museum, but also a lovely place to learn about culture, traditions and architectural styles. The staff is very friendly and we had a great time.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: Wonderful outdoor architectural displays to learn about the history of Terengganu s boating culture and history of the state and people. Many interesting aspects of the music, crafts and food that s unique to the region on display. Worth a half day visit and wander at your leisure.

title: Kampung Budaya Terengganu
translated_review: well done kampung culture team. We from Kuala Lumpur were very impressed with the friendly service provided, complete with solid information. Various activities include, traditional gannu martial arts show, traditional cake making, batik canting, the price of hand painted batik shirts sold here is very affordable, mengkuang weaving and so on. Each house is perfectly maintained..complete with home decorations and kitchen items such as crockery. Around the clean, well-maintained landscape of green trees. Clean toilet facility. Good job!!!

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: A good place to disconnect from the city. No fancy facilities but the nature experience makes up for it.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: My kids enjoyed feeding the chickens and learning how villagers harvest vegetables. Felt like an educational trip.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: Quiet, natural environment. Host provided traditional cakes for us very delicious. Thank you for the hospitality.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
