title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: Staying at Kampung Dagat Homestay felt like living in a real village community. The host family was very warm, and the river view in the morning was so calming. Definitely a hidden gem.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: Such a peaceful place. Loved the boat ride arranged by the host and the traditional breakfast. Simple but meaningful experience.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: Quiet place, fresh air, comfortable room. It's just a little far from the town, but it's suitable for healing.

title: Kampung Dagat Homestay
translated_review: The village is one of the last place in Sabah that reminds us on the beauty of reciprocity with nature

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A beautiful and charming experience. We also visited the fireflies the second night at a different spot with motorised boats BUT KAMPUNG on the row boat was our favorite experience. It s so quiet and magical. Top tips get there 40 min early, one of you get into the q to buy tickets and the rest of the party q up on the rows of seats. They way once the tickets are bought you are in the line to get onto the boats. We paid the 50RM for the entire boat for the 2 of us. 4 on a boat is a tight squeeze. Read up on the fireflies first the boatman are not tour guides. Take mozzie spray.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A magical experience. It s as beautiful as we expected it to be! It was likg being in a Disney movie with all the fireflies surrounding the boat and the trees. I highly recommend this place to anyone travelling in the area!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A must see when visiting Kuala LumpurA Malaysian friend drove us out to see the fireflies.We stopped at Restoran Makanan Laut Jeti for seafood dinner by the river first it s about 10km away .The fireflies are amazing. The boats are hand paddled, so no noisy motors. It was restful drifting along the river, stopping at different mangroves to see the sparkling fireflies.Boats seat a maximum of 4 people.We all wore life jackets. Staff helped us in and out of the boat.Well worth the trip out of KL it was around 1 hour by car.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A quote from the guide that I can t forget Fireflies are very sensitive to the nature s delicate balance. If the temperature changes, they die. If the water is polluted, they die. If the tree nest is disturbed, they die.Suffice to say, if you don t see fireflies anymore, than the earth is dying

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A trip to Kuala Selangor for the Fireflies park is a must if you re going with kids as they can enjoy seeing the Fireflies in the darkness, Eagle feeding and Blue tears experience as well. Even the boat ride will be a fun experience for both adults and kids. Watch out for the weather conditions before you book tickets as it can play spoil sport. Have fun.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A trip to Kuala Selangor would be incomplete without exploring the uniqueness and miracles of the fireflies found in the midst of mangrove trees in this small village. Take a traditional sampan small boat into the darkness and you might find yourself amazingly enthralled by the thousands of shinning fireflies. So breathtaking, you won t want to miss it!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: A truly magical experience! The fireflies at Firefly Park Kampung Kuantan were absolutely mesmerizing like stars twinkling all around us. The boat ride was peaceful and the guides were friendly and knowledgeable. A perfect spot for nature lovers and families. Highly recommended if you re visiting the area!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: a very good place for you to educate your children about nature.one of the best place to see firefly.do not come during full moon because maybe you won t get to see the firefly.RM60 boat. each boat 4 pax. during 20 minutes.ticket counter open around 6.00 pm. activity start around 7.30 pm or 8.00 pm.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Absolutely magical experience. It was the sort of thing a person dreams about. The boat towers were so quiet so didn t disturb the firefly s at all. I really recommend this place to anyone travelling in the area. But make sure you wear bug spray !! And a lot of it.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Absolutrly lovely view but it would not be a good place to go by ur own probably better with someone who u love. Otherwise u will feel lonely like I did Try not to take photoes of fireflies, but try to record a video instead. Works much better. As for myself could not take videos as I could not find button for video..... aet ur camera for recording before hop onto a boat. Cheers

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Although the place isn t really wheelchair feasible, the guard was kind enough to let me drive into the area to send my handicapped uncle. Price is quite expensive but if you share, it can be okay. Plus, the view is magical, especially if u

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Amazing! Amazing! Amazing! Don t go with a tour! Don t go to the two other camps! This is the original and locally operated camp. Much less tourists and more authentic. You get on a boat for Max 4 people. The whole boat costs only RM 50. And have I mentioned 4 people on a boat? The other camps fit 20 30 people in a speedboat while this one is super authentic with rowing, so super quiet to the fireflies. Any yes, when it s not training, you will see plenty of fireflies. In fact, we went there after it was raining the whole day but stopped in the night. We saw them and it was a memorable experience!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Awesome. You ll feel like in fairy tales

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Basic infrastructure needs to be upgrade. For nature lover it is a great place to see fireflies because its non seasonal species.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Take the family for a walk to watch the fireflies at the Selangor river estuary

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: The payment is reasonable, but the fan where you wait for the turn to board the boat is dusty.. It's been like 2 years without washing it.. Sorry, please take action... Because the wind doesn't feel good and it's hot...

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Entrance fee per canoe RM60 4 5 people. Buy tickets at the counter. Can stop at the mosque next to the location. An interesting experience compared to Kuala Selangor because you can see closer and hold fireflies.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Fee RM60 for 1 sampan, sampan capacity for 4 people. Wear a life jacket as well, the jacket is sturdy, we are sure you will be safe, InshaAllah. The staff is also very friendly.. The best Kelip kelip Kampung Kuantan team. A very valuable and exciting experience for us as a family. The children are very happy with this new experience. Come early 7.30pm, and preferably when there is no moon, so that the view is more beautiful.. Please visit here..

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: The fee to ride the boat is RM60 per boat. You can share with other people to meet the capacity of the boat. You have to behave in the boat, don't be rude. Because in the dark, anything can happen. As an improvement, the management can demonstrate how to wear a life jacket correctly. Because the jacket needs to be the right size, tie a string at the crotch. Most people only have a cover and a clip on the front part only, it's dangerous. If you fall, the size is big, you can tear off your jacket. The service of people who paddle the boat is also the best! A lot of shared knowledge about fireflies and the history of the river. For RM60 boat cost, and 20-30 minutes boat ride, I think it's worth the new experience.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Beautiful experience. You need to get a ticket for a 4 person boat that cost 53 RMB. The wooden boat trip took around 30 minutes and it was unforgettable hundreds of fireflies bringing entities trees to life, it almost looked fake. Highly recommended

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Beautifully simple and naturally breathtaking experience. The fireflies were astonishing, However, there are NO GRABS NO TAXIS NO BUSES running so you HAVE to hire a driver or you may be stranded and at the mercy of the locals. I thought there would be a few entrepreneurial locals running a few cars but there was one and about eight people who all had no ride home!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Best experience with your love one..Ride the Do Re Mi sampan in the dark, then the rowers will take you to the area of ​​trees that become a twinkling house..beautiful twinkling like street lights

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Best experience. Clean place, good facilities, good service, easy access, only the uncle who paddles the sampan should wear a safety jacket for everyone's safety, plus it's best if the tour guide talks about fireflies, you can add knowledge to the visitors who come. because it's expensive to pay but it's quiet on the sampan. personally took the initiative to ask so that it would not be quiet all the way down the river. Whatever is best

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Best time to visit is during night time. Beautiful scenery of Fireflies along the river.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Best!!! Recommended for the young as the sampan is quite dangerous for the elderly and hyper active children

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Best. But when I left that day the moon was bright. So there are not many flashes. Sampan ticket price Rm60 for 4 people. I went with my husband. Luckily, there was a couple who wanted to ride the sampan with only two people. So, let's just pay the couple. Btw the ticket counter opens at 7:45, I had time to pray first at the mosque next door.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Besttt!!! It's a lot of fun to come here. A new experience for our family. Being able to ride a boat to see the fireflies at close range. We arrived early before dusk. Maghrib prayer at the mosque next to him, there is indeed a gate to go to the mosque. After the prayer, go ahead and buy a ticket, so you can quickly get on the sampan. If it's late, there's a long line. Open at 7.30pm to 10.30pm, Rm60 per boat can take up to 4 people!! Cash only tickets!! Good luck bringing cash. The canoe paddlers are very good at explaining. Just found out that flickering is not the same as fireflies. Flashes will decrease if the wind is strong. We have a lot of sustenance these days. Totally recommended to everyone to go. Saturday, February 18, 2023.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Very beautiful. It just flickers not as much as in 2014. It's a little creepy because it's dark and quiet at night. Haha

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Come as early as they open! The fireflies are most active at dusk.They charge per boat so it s best to come in a group of four.Not recommend to take photos of the fireflies as the camera s flash will scare them.The boatman is really skillful and knows a lot about the fireflies as they re locals being a part time boatman as an exercise The ride lasts around 15 20 minutes.The ambience are amazing and serene. Very healing.This is my second time here

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
username: Mohammad Amirul Assyraf Bin Mohammad Noor
translated_review: Cost per sampan is RM 50 which it can accommodate for four person. You are recommended to bring own mosquito repellent, damn expensive when you buy here RM25. Please come and queue early as the counter will be opened at 7.45 pm night .

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: just come and buy a ticket at the cheaper machine. at machine 1 sampan only rm45, enter ic number and pay using ewallet. happy parking is also easy. I came subtu at 845pm, I didn't even queue. there is a life jacket, so don't worry.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Come here on Saturday night, there are a lot of people..RM60 1 sampan, 4 people max, let's help the villagers.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Depending on the condition of the environment, timing and humidity, the fireflies will never disappoint.Best time to visit is when it s just after rain with high humidity and no moonlight. You ll then be able to see the trees light up just like Christmas tree

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: During weekdaysThere s not much que.Rm60 boat 4pax seating Raining Not open Windy lesser firefliesI went on the correct timing...8pm above is the right timing to see more fireflies ..I went duringNot windy not raining , perfect timing..Bring kids will be much better experience..

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Fees per boat is RM60 ringgit and can occupy 4 adults or 2 adults 2 kids.Great experience to watch firefly while cruising along the river. Peaceful and quiet.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Fireflies experience 8 10. But facilities and services are definitely 3 10. You paid rm50 boat for four people, for just 20 minutes ride, with no insects repellent provided.If there s another fireflies place around, nobody would ve come here. No interactive information of some sort. No open cafeteria. No toilet paper in restrooms. No video room as mentioned by the sign near the ticketing counter. No free insects repellent should be able to provide the cheapest one at least for the price you pay . This place is world class, people from all over the world come to see OUR fireflies, but when you arrive here you don t feel like it. Men who row the sampan, they should be able to take 5 minutes to give info about the fireflies while rowing, about how special they are. But nope, just keep rowing and playing with phone or burps loudly while rowing. Disappointed with the services provided, but well, who cares right? As long as the locals can make money with minimum energy spent, and people can actually see the fireflies, everyone is happy I guess. Disappointed.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Good authentic experience with low boats that allow you to get up close to the fireflies and trees. Payment system is automated so nice and quick now and queue was moving fast when I went.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Good experience for the family members to watch the fireflies.Very enjoyable sampan cruise along the river.The staff are friendly and courteous to the visitors.Safety jackets are compulsory to be worn during the cruise.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Good place for children for visit.RM60 1 boat 4 ppl about 20 min ride.Be patient expect the long queue, 7.50pm enter entrance still have to for an hour for the ride

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Good place to educated your children. Friendly staff, well kept environment. Tapi banyak nyamuk la... Tp repelent ade jual kat enterance

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Great environment.peaceful and clean air. Firefly strive major attraction attract tourist worldwide.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Great experience, we took a package tour with Klook that includes monkey feeding and dinner which was great .Must have a bug repellent spray..

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Great place to experience fireflies

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Here you can ride on sampan and enjoy the scene of fireflies dancing in the night nearby those trees which is its food source.. the price is RM50 sampan which can ride up to 4person. The experience is great and not to missed if you like beauty of nature.. best time to visit when the night without moon and high tide..

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Here, you can take a sampan ride and take in the sight of fireflies flashing in the darkness next to the trees that serve as their food supply. The best time to visit is during a moonless night and a high tide. This site is amazing. Fireflies are so rare that they can be seen in large cities. I advise you to bring your children here so they can experience nature.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Highly recommend for a visit especially if the weather is dry and you are in Kuala Selangor until late evening night. The facilities are newly renovated under partnership of the municipal and local boatman. The local boatman had served there for a long time. This firefly sanctuary is one of the most populated sanctuary by the firefly thanks to the special species of tree found in the rivers.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: hmm can go for a try, is a little surprise for family or short trip, just an little area for walk

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: hygiene awareness very poor! Life jacket very bad smell. Advise to wash and dry it at least once a week .

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I heard some negative feedback that place is very dirty. My life jacket was a little smelly because of previous guys applied some anti mosquito spray, but it was not too much and not bothering me. I recommend you to buy a anti mosquito spray before coming there.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I honestly didn t use any of its facilities like toilets. I only remember that there were information boards which gave us sth to look at during the long wait for the boats. I think there was a souvenir shop too. Not sure about other facilities but let me tell you that the fireflies are worth the visit! They really do look like fairy lights and it s amazing how they can synchronize the lighting. Ticket price might seem expensive but let s do it for the fireflies!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I love this unique experience, so quite so peace rolling the boat on the stream while witnessing the firefly, river, mangrove, night sky and trees. Highly recommend to you that looking for unique experiences. My boy love it too!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I loved this special experience the night sky, the gentle breeze, and the twinkling fireflies. It was so quiet and peaceful, with only the faint sound of the oars in the water.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I never would have learned about this destination if not for a Korean friend. Absolutely stunning! We were at the queue at 7pm and had to wait until sundown. Was able to do the activity at around 8.30pm and it was so good. Definitely worth it. Guides said that it was better if we did 9pm but sadly, our itinerary cant accommodate a later viewing. The trees looked like Christmas trees. That was how many fireflies were there. Our guide provided anti mosquito spray but it wpuldnt hurt if you bring your own and of course, wear enough clothes to cover as much skin as you can.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I visited as part of a tour from KL. You ll need to plan your visit on a dry, dark night preferably when there is no moon. Its an amazing experience! Defo recommend to see it once in a lifetime

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: I visited the firefly park. I was told that beside this place the other places were only in New Zealand and Brazil. Saw many tourists mostly from Japan and Korea and few from Russia. I was lucky that my guide was an old man who had been doing the job since 1973. Very knowledgeable and thus I learned few interesting facts about fireflies, pretty amazing facts. I was told that to have good view one should visit during the full moon. And the view would be much better if it was raining earlier in the afternoon. Ticket is RM53 per boat that sits 4 adults. It the river is cleaner I would give 4 or 5 stars rating. I recommend a trip here nonetheless.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: If you are seeing firefly first time in your life then this place is okey to visit once else no need, for us it was 10 min boating in dark for four person for RM50 was quite expensive and not that fun.But really appreciate the effort and maintenance they are investing here.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: If you love nature, this is definitely a place you must go. This 30 minutes night tour will give you a unique experience of watching fireflies lightshow while cruising along the river in the traditional sampan. Each sampan can accomodate up to 4 people plus 1 rower which also serve as your tour guide.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: Interesting night time outdoor activity to do with the family. It s about 1 hour drive from KL. Admission fee is RM50.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It is actually wonderful! There s almost no queue and the boats are for 4 people. I would suggest to go when there s no moon so you can see better and when is not raining cause the fireflies could not show up. But if it s raining a lot they might. You re also gonna have the chance to touch them and it s very cool! They re also a particular kind of fireflies that lights on and off all togheter. It is very beautiful!

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It is approximately an hour away from the twin tower.Amazing fireflies.Tips come one hour late after evening time to avoid the queue after the sunset.You wil not be able to take the boat before sunset.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It is nice, you will see some fireflies on the trees. Make sure you are wearing mosquito repellent before going to this place.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It was a magical experience. The fireflies around the trees looks like a Christmas tree. If you plan to come here, make sure that it is not during full moon. No moon, better! You have to pay RM50 per boat which could accommodate 4 person per boat.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It was very mind blowing experience for me. There were lots and lots of fireflies on trees. They were flashing bright. I felt like I was in a fairytale wondering what would ancient people think about it all if they did not have an idea about fireflies at all. It must have felt like magic. It is still a magic.Only four people are allowed on a boat and it costs 51 RM per boat. The trip is about 20 min long. They don t take you far away from the dock. Like 50metres to the left, back and 50 metres to the right.

title: Kampung Kuantan Fireflies Park
translated_review: It s a bit overrated. You need not be disappointed if you miss it. However, if you have a lot of time available and curious to find out what the hype online is all about like us You won t be upset that you visited, but can t rule out coming back disappointed.Few tips 1. Get yourself mosquito repellent liquids prior to visiting it is also sold there for RM 20 to 30 not so good, but does the job.2. Dress easy this is not a boat or a ferry ride.. it s a catamaran ride. Be prepared for a bit of stretching and crouching.3. Carry a DSLR instead of relying on your phone. It basically boating in the night. You need something that can handle extreme low light photography.4. The life vests are available in plenty but smelly carry a body spray or perfume otherwise you re only thinking of the odour sweat smells and not enjoying the experience You will for sure thank me for this one when you re wearing the vest. You can hit that like in advance 5. Most important Cards not accepted, just ensure you have RM 50 available per boat of 4 adults6. Get there early, queue up outside the ticket counter. If not you ll end up in a long queue later.7. If you re in a group, split your team Few can queue at the ticketing counter and the rest can queue up at the boarding area. So you don t end up queueing twice.The drive from KL is enjoyable, a mix of highways and fields crops.Enjoy your trip!!!

title: Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah
translated_review: Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah in Kuching, Sarawak, has earned its reputation as one of the city s renowned and highly recommended sellers of kek lapis, and it happens to be a favorite in my family as well. On this trip, I had the pleasure of introducing my friends to this cherished establishment.Our stay at the Hilton Hotel required us to cross the Sarawak River via a small boat, or sampan. Conveniently, the pier was just a stone s throw away from the hotel. The short five minute journey came at the minimal cost of RM1 per person, making it an enjoyable experience in itself.After disembarking on the other side of the river, we embarked on a brief five minute walk to reach Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah. Arriving late in the evening around 9 30 PM, we found that most of the cakes were already sold out.Our group decided to sample some of the cakes and planned to place our orders the next day. The prices here were considerably more affordable than those at the waterfront, and the taste was equally delightful, if not better. The butter based kek lapis was priced slightly higher at RM25 per piece, while the margarine based kek lapis cost RM15 per piece. For convenience, 5 to 6 kek lapis could be neatly packed into a small box that fit perfectly in a luggage bag. I ended up ordering two boxes.What truly stood out was their exceptional customer service. Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah offered delivery directly to our hotel, with no additional charge. We only had to pick up our orders at the hotel lobby. For larger orders exceeding 15 pieces, it s advisable to place the order a day in advance to ensure the cakes are freshly baked and promptly delivered.My personal favorite has always been the masam manis kek lapis, and I found it just as delightful as ever. Without a doubt, I ll be ordering from Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah again in the future, and I would highly recommend it to anyone seeking a taste of Sarawak s famous kek lapis.

title: Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah
translated_review: Sorry, I want to give a sincere review for the seller and also the purchase that comes to this store. I bought a layer cake at the store on Friday and then packed it in a box to bring back to KL on Sunday so I expect the layer cake especially the premium one to last but my guess was wrong. The 2 premium layer cakes that I bought, biscoff cheese and red velvet, have been attacked by fungus, even after storing them in the refrigerator. It's a bit sad because I couldn't taste the layer cake but I had to throw it away because the package was moldy and smelly too. Please the seller in this shop can take more careful steps before selling the layered cakes by making sure that the layered cakes have expired or other precautions so that it is easy for customers. For those who buy, this shop's layer cake looks good to me when I taste the tester but I'm not sure where it went wrong, I hope the buyer can also take the step of checking properly before taking it and paying. Sincerely from me.

title: Kek Lapis Dayang Salhah
translated_review: Probably one of the best layer cake or kuih lapis shop in Kuching. This is their main store and many visitors come here to buy these, even locals. The prices here are lower than what is sold in the city. The shop is located a little inside from the main road, so will need to walk down a short path. Footwear not allowed inside. They have a huge selection of different varieties and flavors and on the sampling table, there are about 20 different flavors to try before you purchase. There are simpler ones and more lavish ones at higher prices. The lowest price for a block is RM 10 which is still much cheaper than in the city. Some of the more premium ones can get up to RM 25 per block and are stored in the fridge. If one buys over RM 100 per person, during my visit, they gave a RM 10 block as a free gift. Packing is done very well for shipment and travel. They have cardboard boxes. It was quite busy during my visit.

title: Kek Lapis Warisan Sdn Bhd
translated_review: I had the opportunity to try the Sarawak layer cake from this Warisan Layer Cake booth while attending a competition and I was thrilled with the experience. The taste of layer cake offered is very delicious and enticing, with a variety of unique and satisfying flavor options. Out of the many options available, the two flavors that caught my eye the most were the Hearty Pari and Belacan flavored layer cake, a slightly different texture and flavor combination. Both have a rich taste, balanced between sweet and fruity, as well as a soft but dense texture. The original and authentic taste of the original Sarawak layer cake. The service and hospitality provided at this booth is friendly and very helpful for me to choose the layer cake that suits my taste. Their booths are strategically opened at the competition location, making the process of trying and purchasing layer cakes very easy without having to walk far. I really appreciate the provision of many testers and unlimited visitors are free to try different types of cakes as long as the booth is open. This gives the opportunity to choose with confidence without doubt. What adds to the comfort of this experience is the friendliness of their sales coordinator. They are very helpful, give flavor suggestions according to the customer's taste, and never tire of answering questions with a smile. This shows a high commitment in providing quality customer service. Overall, I am very satisfied with the quality of the cake, friendly service, and customer-friendly approach by the team at this booth. Their Sarawak layered cakes are not only suitable to be enjoyed on their own, but are also suitable as gifts or souvenirs from Borneo. I will definitely buy again when I come to Sarawak again

title: Keropok Lekor Tokku
translated_review: I have been buying this keropok lekor from another famous shop for years. This time I was introduced to this shop by a colleague working around here. A pretty simple village style shop and they sell dried fish crackers as well. The fried lekor was especially delicious, very suitable to my taste compared to the other shops I ve been buying from. translated_review: Every time I come to Kuala Terengganu, especially in Kampung Losong, I definitely choose local fish-based food. This time, I stopped at Kedai Tokku Leko, which sells lekor and sata crackers. The fried lekor crackers are full of fish flavor, and when drizzled with chili sauce, it tastes even better. Scale 3.8 5 However, for me, the most special food here is the fish fillet. It tastes really good, with the perfect balance of fat and spicy. This is the tastiest fish sata I have ever tried. Scale 4.5 5 Whenever I visit Kuala Terengganu, especially Kampung Losong, I always opt for local seafood based dishes. This time, I went to Kedai Tokku Leko, which sells keropok lekor and sata. The fried keropok lekor had a rich fish flavor, and when dipped in chili sauce, it became even more delicious. Rating 3.8 5However, for me, the true highlight here is the fish sata. The taste is absolutely delightful, with a perfect balance of creaminess and spiciness. This is, without a doubt, the best sata fish I have ever had. Rating 4.5 5

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: i have to admit that when i first found out there would be no mobile reception and TV i was worried on how to keep a 2 and 6 year old entertained! but i decided that i needed to breakaway from that connectivity and reconnect without these technology devices. upon reaching the area, we crossed a suspension hanging bridge. the kids found that amusing. then when they descended from the bridge, they ran around like wild chickens. when we approached the fig tree i was actually impressed how compact yet complete it was. we decided to do half and half for food cook and also enjoy the local dishes which could be ordered. we went to the river for a dip, fed the fish in the pond, played with the local musical instruments, did a slight trek in vain to find a buffalo don t make promises you can t keep to kids so i learnt . we found the village buffaloes bathing in mud and owh gosh the kids were ecstatic. at night, we had an early dinner. Helenna who prepared our food and Aryl our guide stayed on to play and chat with us and the kids. by 9pm, we called it a night. the area is so calming and it really did give me a sense of peace. would i return?yes. how would i vacay there again?with friends! Imagine having a private space all to yourself and friends, BBQing and just enjoying the night. Now there are only 2 rooms, but you can camps comfortable in the shaded area. I would also have done Quad Biking but didn t have enough time for that. breakaway and get away. here at the kiulu farmstay

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: We booked a two day, one night stay here through Borneo eco tours. On arriving we were greeted by enthused and pleasant staff. Wet were given an excellent lunch prepared by one of the local woman from the village. In the afternoon our tour guide Saiden, took us on a 3 hour jungle trek which we thoroughly enjoyed. He explained the life and culture of the villagers and the edible and poisonous plants around the jungle. He also showed us the rice process, rubber tapping and many other local cultural scribes which we also participated in. All the food was prepared and served to us by the local village and wad truly scrumptious especially the hot banana and Saidens home brewed rice wine...This is a place to forget about modern living, relax and enjoy the natural beauty.

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: We came here in February. The place is located across the river, and this is a single accom hut in the farm so you re basically the only ones there which is why it s so cool! When we get there they had just finished preparing us lunch. The caretakers were awesomely welcoming and friendly. The hut is one of a kind, open spaced kitchen with two bedrooms plus a toilet. The kampung meals were absolutely delicious I could still remember it till this day. At night it can get pretty eerie as there were no one else there in the viccinity but we had a Mr. Wexton who camped outside during our stay so he was always there to make sure we were OK. The river in front of the hut superb! It s not rocky like in Kolumpong or Tegudon and best thing is the river is all yours for the entire stay. We enjoyed our stay here it is one of a kind experience. No TV, wifi is weak, and there are bugs flying around especially at night so you may want to keep your sleeves long and bring a bug repellent. Get a full board package so you don t have to cook your own meals plus you re helping the community to earn some cash.

title: Kiulu Farmstay

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: We showed up without being expected and they manage to host a visit for us in a very diligent way ..this is the nice part Because of the non expected visit no lunch was ready but that was king of our fault They organize a few activities rice grinding , pipe blowing and we went on a short tour to the owner village. Nice but nothing extraordinary over 30 minutes. I had asked before how we will be charged and were told you will see upon arrival at the village. Warm welcome by the family with fruits, music and dance and photos. Then the owner asked st which hotel we stayed and looked at my wife s bracelet and was preparing the move to charge us big time, all this with a big smile of course 125 ringgits for each person !! we were 3. We paid as we did not want to create any concern and even though we appreciate we have a higher standard of living in Europe, this was just outrageous for Borneo plus we had to pay for our own transportation . This guy is a crook , do not go there and better spend your money somewhere else. I am sure all is business is run the same way. Good thing we had better experience with other Borneo people who did not only see us as wallets on legs. So long.

title: Kiulu Farmstay

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: We stayed there for 1 night sometime in June and absolutely loved it. My family, especially the kids had a lovely time staying there. Mr Saidi was exceptionally welcoming and even went above and beyond when he told stories to the kids to keep them busy while we were packing. The welcoming beverages was sangat sangat Sedap! My wife especially really loved the sambal that was prepared for us. Very delicious mix with my sister s nasi goreng terjun. Even though the he wifi was down but it gave us opportunity to really disconnect and just enjoy the moment. Few days after, Mr Saidi contacted me to update about the suspension bridge that has been repaired at the place after I expressed my concern about the bridge s condition. Mr Saidi, you are my hero! 10 10 would visit again.

title: Kiulu Farmstay
translated_review: Wow, wow and wow.. This off beaten destination is worth the travel for one who wants to experience a back to basis kind of holiday. I took the 2D1N package from Borneo Eco Tours and it was worth every dollar. Simple lodge, lovely guide, friendly locals, interesting activities, folktale stories, and many more. As soon as you arrive to this destination, its time to keep your modern day mobile devices away.. not out of choice but there isn t any signal.. All the meals were cooked by the locals and it was delicious.. My favorite outdoor activity was the river tubing and ATV ride.. simply awesome.. It was certainly a memorable experience..

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: Eve of Kaamatan KDCA had super busy slow moving traffic in either direction. Safe drop off on the roadside as cars were respectful of pedestrians. Free entrance. A festival atmosphere as u pass d KDCA arch with a blast of Emcee on ongoing competition, soft drinks food vendors. Walk thru a path with the majority of BBQ fried steamed cooked food. Yes....ur nice attire, out goes ur freshly bath body, now a mixed food scented aroma. Oooops face mask on due to shoulder to shoulder crowd....hmmm..luckily orderly, respectful moving crowd. Lots of stalls selling BBQ pork or burger or stewed or fried wit all same price of rm20 serving. Soft Beer hard liquor stalls with sit in 2 enjoy r available strategic locations. .. Yes, it a safe crowd environment....Sadly no public traditional dance nor performance..Grab either way as both sides of road r parked, double park...even central divider enjoy ve a gd harvest celebration

After the attack I was Brough into a local restaurant for some Hainanese chicken, however after forty five minutes of waiting, was only served raw turnips. Not my number one recommendation for your travel layover.

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: It s been real fun visiting the cultural village! It s near to the city centre, cheaper than the mari mari village, plus amazing crew and tour guide! It s definitely worth the visit, and the tour guide is a must to make the visit more meaningful. The tour guide s super friendly, being a local himself, he s very knowledgable and most importantly, he s patient with all our questions! So I personally think that the tour guide is a MUST if you want to know more stories of each ethnicity. Will recommend this place, for sure!P S more fun when u come during the harvest festival! Tho for me, when I visit during the non peak season, it s as if we booked the whole place and the tour guide and staffs are performing JUST FOR US!

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: Kaamatan 20221. Food booths have two parts for halal and non-halal. Booths krafts have many to choose from3. Booths of local entrepreneurs with their own products4. A traditional house that is not lonely and you are greeted with a bang5. Bamboo t-shirt booths select 26. Maxis, RTM, Maggi, Hitz FM, Kupi Kupi FM and Petronas booths. These booths are not good at being quiet, there are activities they do. Inside the hall is more interesting. Congrats to Juara Sugandoi, Unduk Ngadau Sandakan and everyone involved. You are all the best. Next Mr Kaamatan

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: Koisaan Cultural Village KCV at KDCA is a fantastic place to immerse yourself in the rich cultural heritage of the Kadazan Dusun people. The guided tours are highly informative, showcasing traditional homes, dances, and crafts. The staff are knowledgeable and passionate, making the experience both educational and enjoyable. The cultural performances are vibrant and engaging, giving visitors a genuine taste of the local traditions. The village is well maintained, and the hospitality is outstanding. Highly recommended for anyone interested in learning about Sabah s indigenous cultures and enjoying a unique, enriching experience.

title: Koisaan Cultural Village

title: Koisaan Cultural Village

title: Koisaan Cultural Village

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: My experience on the KDCA tour with my family was truly unforgettable, thanks to our in house guide Miss Priscilla and Mr. Paulinus, who helped with all the demonstrations. They warmly welcomed us into the world of the Kadazan Dusun people, sharing stories that made their culture come alive. The highlight was witnessing the Magagung performance, a traditional gong ensemble that filled the air with lively rhythms.Walking through the cultural village, we were amazed by the traditional houses and the intricate details that reflected the locals way of life.Overall, the KDCA tour was not just a learning experience but also a personal journey of rediscovering our roots. It s such an underrated place of interest. I highly recommend this tour to anyone who wants to immerse themselves in the rich culture of Sabah. Kotouhadan!!!

title: Koisaan Cultural Village

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: We recently rented three units during my daughter s wedding there, namely Hamin Tambunan, Ranau, and Kuala Penyu.We were accused of stealing eight 8 blankets and four 4 towels and were forced by the staff to pay a penalty of RM210.Those towels and blankets were not even in the house in the first place, especially in the double decker beds in Hamin Tambunan, there were none at all. PLEASE REFER TO THESE UPLOADED PICTURES. CAN YOU SEE ANY BLANKET TOWELS??? We have witnesses who can confirm the absence of those items in Hamin Tambunan from Day 1. However, the staffs kept insisting that we pay and claimed they had checked the CCTV, which they said showed that only we were there during that period. However, they have failed to prove us with a CCTV recorded video, showing that we stole those items. None.I will never pay for something that was not our fault in the first place, even though my wife has already paid the penalty just to avoid escalating the matter further. I did not agree actually.To anyone planning to stay at any of these homestays, be cautious of this scam !!Thank you very much.

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: We visited KK during their Kaamatan Harvest Festival and there was a huge night market and activities through the centre over 29th to 31st May. I believe there are other ceremonies and cultural activities throughout the month of May too. Spectacular. I just love being among the locals and being part of their communities. Pork burgers, grilled oysters and stirred fried wild boars !! Gorgeous!!

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: We went during the Harvest Festival on the 30th at nighttime ard 6pm. It was chock full of people suuuperr hot and stuffy . They barred visitors from eating or drinking within premises as they shud haha so we hurried along to the huts for a quick look around before heading back out.The alcoholic drinks in kdca during Kaamatan is q pricy as you need to spend around RM120 on average per person. The food stalls there are also priced a minimum of RM10 onwards but that s because rent is like 3k for 7 days of vending so it s justified la.Tbh it s like any other bazaars or tamus hahaha, we din rly see much traditional performances barring one Magunatip floating bamboo dance performance. Ah, the beauty pageant Unduk Ngadau contest was also held on the 31st inside the air conditioned hall.

title: Koisaan Cultural Village

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: Went during the Kaamatan Festival. It was packed with people and cars are parked dangerously outside the main road which is not safe for the pedestrians especially for children and elderly . Anyway, here s what I liked the most, the handmade handicrafts, the traditional cultural longhouse RM1.00 per entrance , the homemade delicacies like lihing, sikat, fish and fruit pickles ranging from RM10.00 and above no free sample food testing okay , the local vendors agro culture products sadly, I didn t see much of it, since the name of Kaamatan means harvest clearly mentioned but I enjoyed the Goat Milk Honey Ice Cream though yummy . . For a first timer visit to KDCA during Harvest Festival, it will be a cultural shock, where you can see lots of pork and alcohol stalls here and there plus the locals get drunk till morning I wondered how the nearby neighborhood can sleep at night with the nightclub noises Thanks God it was a sunny day. For your information, the entrance fee not on festival season is RM25.00 for local and Foreigner is RM35.00. Here are my snapshots on the Kaamatan event itself .

title: Koisaan Cultural Village
translated_review: Went there on the morning of 19 April.At 10plus in the morning, the sun was killing us.The cultural village tour was fruitful and we got nice experiences in it.We bought the ticket at the location. we think we were the first visitor to the compound on that day.We visited the various model houses of the natives.But the memorable peak of visiting this place will be the station tasting Lihing glutinous rice wine , eating sago grubs butod , learning to start fire and cook the butod, use the blowpipes, jumping onto the traditional trampoline and watching the traditional music being played on gongs and kenong.Our guide, a young lady wished I could have asked for her name was very pleasant and knowledgeable. We asked a lot of questions beyond what was seen onsite.The murut house is exciting as we have to cross the swinging bridge, to be met by the native from Murut on the other end.The sago grubs, or Butod is a MUST TO TRY!!Yes, initially the thought of eating a worm can really test ones nerves. But after overcoming it, the butod eating is actually pleasant and delectable.There is nothing unfamiliar with the taste, except for the fear of eating a worm.The butod personally for me tasted like fried egg. Especially the white part of the egg white which was fried with a bit of brown bits on it. Some said it tasted like fresh uncooked sweet corn.Because the flavour is familiar, I am compelled to say that I am definitely going to have more butod in restaurants around KK that serves them.I do recommend tourists to visit this place, even though the weather is not friendly enough. Because their hospitality is superb!Wishing KDCA all the best and will expand to have more native housing and share more of the native cultures and history with us. Will visit you again definitely!!??

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: If Google tells you the ticket costs RM200, don t panic. It s actually closer to RM25. Some private charters may charge more, but the standard tour is affordable and easy to join. The boats are clean, the life jackets are in good condition, and the whole trip has a choose your own adventure feel. Some ads mention dolphin sightings, but don t count on it they re seasonal at best, maybe even mythical. No one brought them up on our visit.What is on offer is a surprisingly rich view of river life. You ll pass under the town s iconic bridge while someone explains the different types of handmade boats. Then you ll stop at a kelong a floating fish farm part zoo, part classroom where you might hold a horseshoe crab or gently touch a pufferfish. Kids get excited. Adults act cool. But everyone learns something, whether they admit it or not.The highlight is the eagles and they don t disappoint. They are trained and arrive when the boats do. Like clockwork, the sky fills with 20 to 30 pairs of wings. Rumour has it you get to feed them, but we didn t on our trip. Still, they fly close enough for great photos and even better memories.Most staff speak Malay and Mandarin. English varies depending on the guide. If your guide is Khairul, you re in luck he s passionate, knowledgeable, and deeply respectful of the environment. The kind of person who makes you care about mangrove trees more than you thought possible.Local schoolchildren often help out with customer service. Some are enthusiastic, others clearly wish they were somewhere else. On the one hand, it s impressive how capable they are. On the other, you hope they re being treated well and not skipping school just to help tourists hold sea creatures. Either way, it gives a glimpse into the town s effort to keep eco tourism alive.It gets busy during peak times expect long queues and less of a personal feel. Visit early or during off peak hours if you prefer something quieter. Tours last around an hour to 90 minutes. Timings are flexible or just loosely followed. Some slots are fixed, but others seem to leave when enough people arrive. It s charming in a slightly chaotic, agak agak kind of way.All in all, this isn t a polished theme park and it doesn t try to be. It s the whole kampung coming together to share what it knows, what it loves, and what it s trying hard to protect. And if you re lucky, you ll leave with more than just pictures of pufferfish you ll leave with a new perspective.

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: On 6.6.2025, our family embarked on a memorable river boat tour in Kuala Sepetang, Taiping that offered an enriching blend of nature, wildlife, and local culture. Tickets were reasonably priced at RM25 per person. We were the first group to set off that morning at 10 00 AM, joined by three other families about 12 people in total.The tour began with a warm welcome from the locals, who were enthusiastic and eager to share their knowledge. Our guide, a friendly and knowledgeable young Chinese girl, accompanied by an experienced boatman, made the journey even more engaging with fascinating stories about Kuala Sepetang s history and environment.Our first stop was a floating fish farm, where we got up close with a variety of marine life, including crabs, puffer fish, and several other sea creatures. We had the chance to feed them and snap some close up photos, making it a fun and educational activity for both kids and adults.From there, the boat took us along a peaceful stretch of mangrove forest. Gliding through the calm waters, we soaked in the beauty of the mangrove ecosystem an important natural habitat rich in wildlife and tranquillity.The final part of the tour was particularly exciting. As the boatman gave a few loud honks in the middle of the river, dozens of wild eagles appeared and began circling above us. Watching them glide and dive overhead was a spectacular sight perfect for photography and an unforgettable end to the journey.In summary, the river tour was an unforgettable experience that delighted the whole family. Between the close encounters with marine life, the scenic cruise through mangroves, and the incredible sight of wild eagles responding to the boat s honking, it s a must do activity for anyone visiting Kuala Sepetang. Highly recommended!

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: Our tour guide was En.Khairul, who is very knowledgeable about the local history and ecosystem of Kuala Sepetang. His tour is different from the other commercial tour operators in the sense that 1 his tour group size is smaller so you get a more personalised experience, and 2 he is more sensitive to the needs of the local flora and fauna, and will not encourage any activities that will harm it. His tour was also slower in pace, so we didn t feel rushed and were able to take our time exploring each stop. And he brought us pretty close to the fireflies, which was amazing and something we noticed the commercial tour boats were not able to do. Truly a memorable trip with En.Khairul.

If you arrive early in the morning, it feels like riding a foggy Cameron. Fresh seafood from pengkalan, fresh belacan salted fish for me is delicious from tan g D . But have to enter the intersection after Makjah Mee Udang. Everything is made in Malay. If after that, most non-Muslims do it. If you want boating experience, you have it. title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: Stumbled upon this private tour service on Google Map while searching for activities to do in Taiping for a family trip. In contact personally with Mr. Khairul Salleh. Since it was a private tour service, time was flexible which is a plus for a crowd with kids, trying to get ready on time!Mr. Khairul Salleh is very friendly yet professional, and very knowledgeable in regards to Kuala Sepetang and its neighbourhood.We took 2 trip packages daytime trip around Kuala Sepetang nighttime trip for the fireflies.Daytime Kuala Sepetang trip 90min boat trip along the river. We were shown around looking at the fishing industry, the mangrove forest the coal factory. We landed at the Shell Beach where the kids managed to collect various seashells washed ashore by the local cockle shellfish industries byproducts. Then we stopped at the fish farm looking at tilapias, horseshoe crabs, pufferfish mudskippers. Info sharing session by Mr. Khairul Salleh was very informative interactive. I was very happy that my siblings the kids were very happy getting involved throughout the trip. We ended the trip watching eagles feeding on the river. Magnificent! Oh, almost forgot the mangrove sapling release into the river with hope of everlasting green ecosystem for the future to enjoy cherish!Nighttime firefly trip Another memorable trip. One hour session watching fireflies amongst the mangrove trees flashing lights synchronously along the river. We were all in awe by Mr. Khairul Salleh s vast knowledge of the fireflies the mangrove habitat, and reminded us how crucial important keeping earth clean green is to the sustainability of the ecosystem.All in all, we really enjoyed it definitely would recommend it! Keep it up, En. Khairul!!!

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: The locals are so dedicated to provide the best eco tourism services, such as River Cruise, Eagle Feeding, visit to Fish Farm, shuttle to Kuala Sangga and Firefly tour to name a few of the main offerings. There are many friendly tour operator providing single item tour to full package. We are happy with the tour packages by this small fishing town. Easily a day to two to complete the visitation to Kuala Sepetang.Note Parking is by the roadside but be warned not to park at corners as tour buses will definitely making turning at the already narrow street.

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: The tour with Mr Khairul is something I will remember for a long time. He lovingly prepared us breakfast before the trip. Being a city gal, I benefited tremendously from the immersive learning experience with Mr Khairul and discovered alot of things about the local community, animals and environment of Kuala Sepetang. His easygoing personality made the trip pleasant and sempoi . He does not encourage the eagle feeding and he ll tell you why which tells you that he is the REAL deal. Do yourselves a favor and book him!

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: This is by far the best river tour in Kuala Sepetang. Khairul has so much information and knowledge to share. We really appreciated that he was so conscious of our impact on the environment and making sure we were there to observe, not interfere.The mangroves are a crucial part of not only this town s ecosystem, but the whole world.Thank you Khairul!

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: Took up a tour that included a visit for a fish farm, mangrove and eagle feeding by today on 26 Dec 2024. The ticket per person is RM25. If you have a group of 6, you can get a cheaper rate. We chose the 5pm slot and we were grouped with others in a shelter speed boat. They will provide you with a safety jacket and a bottle of small size mineral water upon getting on the boat. The tour guide was a young girl who spoke good mandarin and a bit of humour. she explained the history of Kuala sepetang and the places that we went. The first stop was at the fish farm where we were introduced with some crabs, puffer fish and some other fishes. We have a chance to take a photo with those fishes and even feed them. The second stop was passing the mangrove and then the third stop at the middle of the river to feed the eagle. Lots of eagles and seagulls were flying all over the sky and river. Good spot to take photos. Overall the experience is good and I definitely will recommend this tour.

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: Very educational boating tour along a river and highly recommended for family trips with kids as Mr Khairul patiently takes time to explain every detail about the places visited and the wildlife in the area. There are several stopovers made and the trip is not rushed as it is a private tour. You get to learn about the different types of fish farming, cockles farming, as well as the various types of boats used in catching different types of seafood. You also get a chance to view different types of coastal birds in the area. There is a stopover at a kelong where you get to feed the fish and also get a chance to hold puffer fish and horse shoe crabs. There is also a cool demo of archer fish hunting their prey . There is even a mini ceremony for everyone to release mangrove seedlings in the river. The trip wraps up with watching the feeding of brahminy kites. You might want to prepare your cameras to capture the action of these kites swooping down over the river. Overall a truly wholesome experience and money well spent. We went over to Rasa Sayang seafood restaurant for lunch not part of the trip as it is just a short walk from the jetty. Their food was great and very affordable. So a wonderful way to conclude your family execursion at Kuala Sepetang

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

We had a very exciting and pleasant tour of Kuala Sepatang. Khairul talked about the various fishing boats and activities, History of Port Weld , the various types of birds we saw along the way and Mangrove conservation. We also visited a fish farm. The highlight of the tour was the eagle feeding. We recommend a booking with Khairul, and you can customize your tour or take one of the packages.

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism

title: Kuala Sepetang Eco Tourism
translated_review: We took the sunset kelip kelip bintang fireflies and constellation tour and had a wonderful time. The fireflies were a spectacular sight not to be missed by any nature lover. Knowledgable, friendly, and fun we learned so much about the mangrove habitat, fireflies, birds, river ecosystem, stars, local geography history, as well as the local community livelihood and economy. We will definetely come back for other tours with En Khairul. Highly recommended!

title: Kuan Wellness Ecopark
translated_review: This attraction focuses on edible bird s nest farming. Visitors are introduced to the various processes of farming and extraction of swiftlet nest and is very educational especially for the young children. There is also a petting zoo where visitors can see and feed domesticated animals. This attraction is located not far from the town of Tj. Sepat

title: Kuan Wellness Ecopark
translated_review: This is a small farm with an entry fee of RM5 per person. The animals are kept in cages, which may not appeal to all visitors. Exploring the entire farm takes around 10 minutes. There is also a bird s nest farm with a small gallery for visitors to explore. Additionally, the farm has a caf and restroom facilities. Given the limited attractions in Tanjung Sepat, this can be a convenient stop for those looking for a brief visit.

title: Kuan Wellness Ecopark
translated_review: This is an interesting eco park of 5 acres, engaged in swiftlet farming and bird nest processing. Beside the large car park, there is a row of six antique cars with the year of make and photographs of before and after refurbishment.At the bird nest processing centre, you can watch through a large glass wall, the workers busily cleaning the bird nests. The centre looks like a laboratory with the workers all dressed in blue uniform. At this centre, you can find bird nest, honey and some health food for sale. Behind this is a caf that serves food with bird nest. A few steps away from the caf is another sales centre dealing with mainly organic cereals.On the ground floor of the swiftlet house, you can watch a video presentation of everything about bird nest and a mock up of the swiftlet house. You can see real bird nest attached at the ceiling but the swiftlets are fake. The entrance fee is RM3. Beside the swiftlet house is the mini zoo. The entrance fee is RM5 3 10 16 .

title: Kuih Bakar Kuala Ina
translated_review: 2023 08 26 Kuih Bakar Kuala Ina and is not recommended.Arrived at the place around 11.00am from Kuala Lumpur and the shop was a little tricky to find situated at the rear of a house a few metres from the road. Main access is just a small gate beside a toilet! Was served by a young lady, no greetings and with sour face muka ketat . Asked about the kuih bakar and the price. Ordered four pieces of kuih bakar and handed her a note of RM100. I was so unlucky that I didn t have any other small notes. With unfriendly tone and making face, she told me that they didn t have a small change unless I come at noon. She also talked to her boss lady with a cynical way on me. Reminds me of the scent of a gingerbread.Texture is more like cake, and costs RM3.50 each. It tastes EXTRA good when it s hot and freshly baked. Highly recommend eating a bite when it s warm still hot. Pairs perfectly well with some kopi or teh. The outer part of the kuih is crispy, and the insides are moist and soft. Highly recommend heating up in an air fryer or toaster oven before eating.Their business is inside a house and we saw that the rooms were basically like a small factory baking our these kuihs. What a cool little kampung operation small biz.They accept e wallet QR payments and cash only. We were told that the kuih bakar can be kept for up to 1 week too. Best to refrigerate if not eating immediately.

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: 100 recommended Kayak Tour. The place is like a small paradise and absolutely enjoyable touring through the mangroves. We learned a lot about the nature of Langkawi. Beside this we had a nice dinner directly on the floating restaurant. Our guide was also not focused on the time, which means the tours goes longer than expected and we could enjoy more. Thanks for this nice experience!

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: 100 recommended. The tour is organized, our guide Muz was an extremely kind and knowledgeable man, and the place is just incredible. On top of that, differently from most other tours around the area, they have an explicit policy of not feeding the animals so that the tourists can take pictures, which causes enormous problems for the environment in the long run. All other people that were with me in the tour also appreciated that.Thank you for the amazing experience!

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: A great guided tour by Sham. Accidently, it ended up being private tour as others canceled. It was a great experience to kayak between mangroves and see it from within.It was a nice surprise to see different fishes which live in the mangroves and hear explanation of them.As others mentioned communication was fast and clear which gave us a lot of confidence. Sadly most of the bat cave was closed, but still we got explanation about them, which was really nice.

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: A MUST DO IN LANGKAWI. Could not recommend this tour enough. Sham was born to do this his passion and love for the mangrove, the animals and trees species and the conservation of it all was so inspiring and funny. What a lovely guy. We saw 2 types of eagles, monkeys, bats, crabs, fish, snakes, otters, water monitors, kingfishers and more. A must for nature and animal lovers. We were a bit sceptical about the kayaking due to my partners back problems we have struggled on an excursion in Mexico in the past but we were accommodated by using a back support which we were so grateful for. Thank you Sham for the most amazing day

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: An Unforgettable Eco Adventure in Langkawi!Langkawi Eco Adventures exceeded all my expectations and delivered a truly immersive nature experience.We did the mangrove kayak tour, and it was absolutely incredible. Paddling through the winding waterways, surrounded by towering limestone cliffs and rich biodiversity, was a peaceful yet awe inspiring experience. Our guide, Sham, pointed out various wildlife including eagles, mudskippers, and even a few viper snakes. He shared insights on the local ecosystem and the importance of mangroves.Safety was prioritized, and all equipment was clean and well maintained and comfortable.Lunch was good too! Thank you so much for the most amazing time.

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: The guide helped us very well and took the time to tell the story behind everything. He also has a good sense of humor, which made it extra fun. Food was tasty with a nice view. I would like to thank the guide again for his friendly tour. Greetings Fahrettin Sinem

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: Despite a small hiccup upon arrival, everything was quickly resolved thanks to the prompt and helpful support via WhatsApp truly excellent customer service!As for the tour itself, it fully met our expectations. The guide Sham took the time to explain everything clearly without dragging things out unnecessarily. Going through the mangroves by kayak was a real highlight we were able to reach areas that boats simply can t access, which made the experience feel truly special. Our group of 8 people was the perfect size and made for a relaxed and enjoyable atmosphere.Lunch was well organized, with chicken fries, rice, and a delicious Thai soup. Water was also provided, which came in handy even though much of the tour is shaded by the mangrove trees. You can leave your personal belongings at the starting point if you don t want to take everything with you though dry bags and waterproof boxes are available on the kayaks.In short a fantastic guide, a well structured and enjoyable tour, and an unforgettable experience in the heart of nature. Highly recommended!

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: Excellent tour! Our guide, Sham, was knowledgeable, nice, and funny. We saw many animals bats, spiders, snakes, lizards, monkeys, and eagles. The food was also very good I appreciated that I could have a vegetarian option. We enjoyed ourselves and learned many things.

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: Fantastic Mangrove Tour with Sham in Langkawi!We had an incredible experience on the mangrove tour in Langkawi with Sham as our guide. From start to finish, he was knowledgeable, engaging, and made the entire trip both educational and fun. His deep understanding of the local ecosystem, wildlife, and conservation efforts really stood out.Sham took us through the stunning Kilim Geoforest Park, pointing out unique flora and fauna while sharing fascinating stories about the mangroves. He ensured we got the best views and we even spotted wildlife like eagles and monkeysHis passion for nature and sustainability was evident, and he made sure we respected the environment while enjoying its beauty. The tour was well organized, and Sham s friendly personality made it even more enjoyable.If you re in Langkawi and looking for a memorable and eco friendly tour, I highly recommend booking with Sham. It was one of the highlights of our trip!

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: Great tour with informative and explanations from the enthusiastic guide about plants and animal life. Price and quality ratio is therefore excellent. The bat cave is well explained and beautiful. Good instruction on how to handle the

title: Langkawi Eco Ventures (Mangrove Kayak Tour)
translated_review: Had a fantastic time with Langkawi Eco Ventures and cannot recommend them enough! Our guide Sham was all in all everything you want in a guide super fun, engaging, knowledgeable and well prepared. Overall did not know what to expect when booking the experience but came away feeling fulfilled by a good morning of learning!We started the day around 10am there were clear instructions on how to meet the guide . We gathered and first took a boat to a floating platform which turned out to be a restaurant. There we already got the opportunity to get up close and personal with some of the marine life in the geoforest park, including horseshoe crabs and stingrays. After that we grabbed our gear, hats and water prepared by the guide, and explored the mangroves on kayak over a good 2 3 hours. The time absolutely flew by and while it may sound like a long time, we had ample rest stops where Sham shared his experience and answered our questions about the mangroves. By the end of the morning we had seen different types of marine life, learnt about the local monkeys, and saw several eagles! Around 1 30pm we had the opportunity to share lunch with the new friends made and even got lucky and snuck a visit to the Bat Cave nearby before we ended! The guide, boatmen and other members of the group were really friendly and outgoing.Would really recommend this company to go with, if you re looking to do something to learn more about the natural environment in Langkawi and get some exercise in the meantime! translated_review: Superb! Brilliant day with Sham who was so entertaining, enthusiastic and knowledgeable!!Booking process was really simple. Sham explained that whilst he has worked in the industry for over 20 years he has only recently set up this company. Guest experience is very important to him therefore his policy is to keep group sizes small max 10 .We then took a speed boat into the Eco Forrest. The first stop was the bat cave which was fascinating. Sham took us through the cave and we saw the bats up close. Beware the trouble making monkeys .After, we got back in the speed boat and headed to the floating restaurant where we picked up our Kayaks. Sham provided us with water and dry bags. The Kayaks were great quality included a back rest which was really welcome.We then Kayaked through the Mangroves for around 2 hours. We saw walking fish, lizards, grabs, a centipede, a viper snake, eagles and otters! Sham was so knowledgeable about all the wildlife and explained everything brilliantly!After a tasty lunch we were taken back via speedboat to the car park.I could not recommend Sham enough. The tour lasted 5 hours from 10am until 3pm and only cost around 35 pp which was amazing value.Strongly recommend! Thanks Sham!

There are also several stalls selling snacks such as lokching and corn. To enter, there is a charge of RM3 per car. There is also a platform for rent near the river suitable for a picnic. Overall, lata kashmir is highly reccomend to stop by, if you want to visit Kelantan. title: Learn Authentic Malaysian Vegan Cooking
translated_review: A delicious way to spend an afternoon! Zurana has a clear passion for vegan cooking and a lot of experience with making a variety of dishes from scratch. Her kitchen equipment and ingredients alone are a testimony to the enthusiasm for exploring cooking. Zurana s interest in cooking is contagious, and invites you to ask more questions about recipes, ingredients, and processes of making different vegan dishes she already won my heart by having her own homemade vegan mayonnaise and ketchup . We chose to make burgers, and made three types, each of which ws delicious. Depending how hungry you are, you might even eave with dinner lunch for the next day! I really appreciated how Zurana was attentive to the amounts of food we prepare, and making sure nothing gets wasted in the kitchen. An added bonus of the experience is that she has three wonderful cats!

translated_review: Had the opportunity to visit Mah Meri Cultural Village yesterday with my wife and children. Overall, this place is good to get to know and learn about the culture of the Mah Meri indigenous people through the exhibits in the museum and also interactively with the staff there. Traditional dance sessions, making decorative materials from nipah leaves and also making sculptures are among the interesting activities here. Suitable for adults and children. May this place be maintained and maintained to be visited by current and future generations. Kudos for the managemnet and staffs...

translated_review: I am Youtuber Sumit Sightseer youtube channel, Entry ticket 25 Ringgit

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: I thought I could at least enjoy an experience in this place since one has to pay an entrance fee of RM30 for local tourists and RM40 for overseas tourists.The welcome drink was just iced cordial near the front desk. Each guest was given a weaved headgear to wear. There was not much of activities except watching one of the orang asli carving from a tree trunk which was the only highlight . If you are expecting a dance or anything of that sort, there was none. Either there were too few tourists or it was too early we were there at 11.30am There was no guide to give you a personal feel of the lifestyle of the orang asli. You are practically on your own walking and entering the galleries which you need to use your handphone torchlight to read the history, as the lighting was very dim .There were two ladies waiting near the cafe to teach tourists some simple weaving origami to take home.To make it a little more worthwhile, I had my guests play 5 stones at the cafe, and shoot seeds with catapults.It was at least educational and to see the unique and intricate carvings.

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: I walked into this cultural centre hoping to learn about Malaysia s orang asli but I came away learning more.Mr Rashid, his dedication and passion to this place and their people deserves our utmost respect, showed us around.He painstakingly explained Mah Meri people s history and their practices.To see this place, it s preferred that you book in advance as they have limited guides.Mah Meri are proud and happy to receive visitors with a welcoming ceremony , blessing and even a crown.They will prepare according to the informed numbers and requests.It s a small place but, only thru a guide s explanation will you be able to learn their history properly. It s interesting that they already know and was practicing what we are only just finding out and starting to teach our young ones now.There are only about 4200 of them. They need our support to remain as part of Malaysia s colorful history.

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: Interesting place to know orang asli named Mah Meri or people that live in jungle. Not that far from KL, and can continue the trip to Morib.

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: It was a very pleasant and casual 2 hour experience. We had the opportunity to understand and learn more about Mah Meri s cultures by chatting with a lady, who is the person in charge of the cultural village.She is very well versed and communicated well in English with us. She singlehandedly trained the female employees, exposed them with the responsibility of maintaining and promoting their identity, in the cultural village. So that the world knows such amazing cultures and beliefs a minor has.There are notable spots where maintenance is needed, such as air conditioning and pavements, and the whole place is currently run by their own, which means they are not subsidized or sponsored by the Malaysian government. Hence, any help from the public is needed. Let s help them together!

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: Lives this place . Mr Rashid and his team absolutely killed it. His passion and dedication to this Mah Meri history and people are so commendable.But , to go, please book in advance.This is because they are very proud and happy to receive guests. They will prepare a guide , welcoming ceremony with blessing for their valuable guests and even a crown.Please do not be rude and ask what you want of them. But see their history with open hearts. Their way of life is something that we are only just learning and teaching our kids. Isn t that amazing ?

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: Mah Meri Cultural Village is an excellent destination for those interested in learning about the unique culture and traditions of the Mah Meri people. The village offers a glimpse into the daily lives of the community, with guided tours showcasing their intricate wood carvings, traditional dances, and costumes. Visitors can also purchase authentic handicrafts and enjoy delicious local cuisine. Overall, it s a fascinating and enriching cultural experience.

title: Mah Meri Cultural Village
translated_review: Mah Meri Cultural Village is very informative and yet so little is known of them. The pokok besi aka iron tree used in carving is getting scarce, and development may also threaten supply of nipah and mengkuang leaves used in traditional weaving. While there also check out Hatter Castle and other buildings dating back 1920s at Sime Darby Plantation.

The kuah kacang is deliciously thick and flavourful! One of their signature dishes. The laksa, also a signature dish, is authentic and tasty. I like how the owner, who is a ceramist, and his team hand produce the plates and bowls used in this cafe. The pottery workshop is just next door and classes are also available. The owners passion for ceramic craft and his love for creating good food is apparent here. A serious recommendation to eat here! Well done. Service here is fast and the staff are very polite and friendly. The wait times are around 15 mins...not long and the food is very affordable.
