title: Maklang Cafe by Ilham Ceramic Studio
translated_review: If the measure of a good meal is defined by flavours and ambience, then Mak Lang hits all the spots. Hosted and served by the multi talented Radzi he is the master cook and also the potter whose ceramic studio made the tableware that the food is served in. To savour authentic and simple Northern Malay fare in handcrafted all natural ceramic tablewares is truly an experience of its own. Upon entering the quaint space, I can t help but feel like I m having a meal in the kitchen of a family friend.

title: Maklang Cafe by Ilham Ceramic Studio
translated_review: This cute cafe is located in Penarak, Kuah, Langkawi. Need to enter a little village road opposite TS Duty Free Chocolate Shop. Parking is spacious and easy to get. As soon as you step into the cafe, you are immediately stunned to see the interior decoration which is so enchanting. Small but not cramped. Simple but soothing. Comfortable and not hot even in hot weather. The interior is also very clean because customers have to take off their shoes to enter. The service and greetings from the owner make customers feel so welcome to eat here. He is also ready to help customers choose a suitable dish from the menu sheet provided to customers. The reviews about the dishes offered here show that he is very knowledgeable about each dish on the menu. This is very helpful for tourists from abroad who may not know the local menu such as laksa or prawn cucur. We ordered a plate of Mak Lang's prawn dish, tamarind apple juice and iced tea. Potato chip snacks are also available while waiting for the main course to arrive. Cucur prawns are served hot and the sauce is also more than enough for a meal for 2 people. The taste of cucur prawns is also as good as it tastes, quite spicy and salty. Nothing over the top in terms of taste. The peanut sauce is also tasty and not too spicy. In terms of drinks, asam boi apple juice really feels concentrated. Pure and not too sweet. The best water mixer. There are also various payment methods available. Cash or cashless. QR Pay, ApplePay, or cards are all possible. Really makes it easy for customers. In conclusion, if you want to try a variety of local northern Malaysian dishes such as laksa, mee curry or prawn stew, come here. The service from the staff is very welcoming, and the atmosphere of the interior and exterior of the cafe is also very enchanting.

title: Malaysia Batik Canting Workshop
translated_review: A cute outdoors activity hosted by the team at Teja studio. It did start off a bit slow, as we were left to our own devices with little to no explanation and just given the cloth with some line art to trace. Although it was understandable since it was really busy when we got there. After the tracing, we had to call out for their attention, but it got better from there. After getting their attention, they taught us about the history and different types of batik with some examples and showed us how to use the equipment with tips and tricks too. Overall a very fun group experience, would recommend it. Though it looks like you get to do batik on different types of cloth and sizes if you book directly with them.

title: Malaysia Heritage Studios
translated_review: Malaysia Heritage Studio is an exceptional destination that beautifully celebrates Malaysia s rich cultural tapestry. It offers a unique experience, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in the diverse heritage of the nation. From traditional crafts and intricate batik designs to mesmerizing cultural performances, the studio serves as a living museum that showcases Malaysia s vibrant traditions.The ambiance is warm and welcoming, making it a perfect place for both locals and tourists to explore the roots of Malaysia s multicultural identity. The knowledgeable staff enhances the experience with engaging stories and detailed explanations, providing insights into the history and significance of each exhibit.Moreover, the studio often organizes interactive workshops where visitors can learn skills like batik painting or traditional dance, making the visit both educational and memorable. Whether you re a history enthusiast or someone looking for a meaningful cultural experience, Malaysia Heritage Studio is a must visit destination that truly embodies the heart and soul of Malaysia.

title: Malaysia Heritage Studios
translated_review: Malaysia Heritage Studios is a very interesting and worthwhile destination to visit, especially for those who want to get to know the culture and heritage of each state in Malaysia. This place features a variety of traditional houses from each state, complete with information adapted for school children. This makes their learning experience more interesting and interactive. Each traditional house is accompanied by an easy-to-understand history and background, making it an ideal destination for educational tourism. In addition to the house exhibition, there are also interesting activities and performances that visitors can enjoy. This helps liven up the atmosphere and allows visitors to learn more about the local culture in an interactive way. However, one of the improvements that the management can make is to replace the statues of international celebrities such as Angelina Jolie, Brad Pitt, and Mr. Bean with figures of Malaysian legends. For example, P. Ramlee, Saloma, Dato Siti Nurhaliza, and Michelle Yeoh. This can not only introduce Malaysian cultural and artistic icons to local and international visitors, but also make us proud of our own country's heritage. Overall, Malaysia Heritage Studios is a place that should be visited by all walks of life, especially for those who want to learn more about Malaysian heritage in an interactive and educational atmosphere.

The traditional house is very beautiful, but it's a pity that only the outside architecture can be seen, the inside is already dark and there are a lot of lighting decorations. My opinion is that it is more suitable in the house for the special features of each state such as state traditional clothes, state related info. When we left, the collection of clothes was placed in the Perlis house only. It is encouraged to bring a standby umbrella if it is too hot or raining because the area is open. Avoid wearing high-heeled shoes because this area is large. Wear comfortable shoes. The suggestion is to place the vending machine in the middle of the house or on the hill. A bit thirsty also a lot of walking, the cafe is at the bottom before the exit. There is also a toilet. Anyway, congratulations on the improvement.

Malaysia Heritage Studios MHS is a unique destination located in Ayer Keroh, Melaka. Known as the first magical heritage park in the country, this studio combines elements of art, history, and culture in one interesting interactive space. title: Malaysia Heritage Studios
translated_review: We visited today and there were only 5 houses open to public while other houses are closed for maintenance.The ticket price is RM 5 for adults durint weekdays and RM 10 in weekends. Free for children below 12 years old.It takes about an hour to visit the whole area.If you want to attend the culture event, then you have to go there only in weekends at 11 am and 4 pm.From Wikipedia Mini Malaysia ASEAN Cultural Park is a cultural park where impressive replicas of traditional Malaysian and ASEAN homes can be seen. Similar to the Karyaneka Handicraft Centre in KL, each house contains furnishings, fixtures and works of art depicting the culture of each state or country.Located in Ayer Keroh, the Mini Malaysia ASEAN Cultural Park showcases the country s 13 states represented with houses that actually look alike to the untrained eye save for the Borneo house . However inside each abode is where the differences can be seen with life sized wax dummies dressed in respective traditional costumes as well as local handicrafts. At the Mini Malaysia Complex there is also a model of an Orang Asli village.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village

A special shout out to our guide Azzalea who has been patient with our questions, and totally knowledgeable! For those using e hailing services, remember to pre book your return trip. Definitely a must visit for any traveler to Kota Kinabalu.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Had the pleasure of visiting Mari Mari which translates as Come Come Village after a warm invitation, even though we arrived later than expected. We were greeted with genuine hospitality by our guide, Danish, who was both professional and entertaining. The cultural education and immersive experience were incredibly impressive even as a local, I learned and appreciated so much. The performances and food were absolutely superb, far exceeding expectations. Definitely a 5 star experience for me. I ll absolutely be back again!

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: I can t tell more about how truly enchanting the experience was. Every moment felt magical and exceeded my expectations. The atmosphere was delightful, and the attention to detail was remarkable. It was genuinely worth every second spent, leaving me with memories that I will cherish for a long time. The entire experience was not just enjoyable but also incredibly enriching, and I would wholeheartedly recommend it to anyone looking for something special.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: I gave 2 stars because it was the management at fault, not the entertainers . Dancers and tribe house representatives gave their best.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: I would agree that this is the Best Tourist Attraction Non Nature Man Made , as stated on their main website!Opted for the morning 10am slot.The tourists are generally divided into smaller groups, or into groups per families led by a guide each group.Staffs were extremely cordial and welcoming. Our guide was Layren, who was fluent in English and Bahasa Melayu and spoke little Mandarin.The location is shaded by the tall forest trees, so there was no direct sun despite the timing of the tour and we found ourselves surrounded by enchanting nature, with birds occasionally calling out from the trees and some long wild lizards creep at corners of the walkway. We were safe from being bitten by any mosquitoes throughout the tour.Would, however, recommend to don light, cooling clothing as it s still quite humid and a little smoky in some areas.The tour was enlightening and fun, as we got the chance to immerse ourselves into the lives of the 5 tribes, their distinct cultures, homes and even taste their snacks at their long houses. Guests were welcomed to join in on activities such as their traditional trampoline called the Lansaran, give it a go with the Sumpit , a blowpipe weapon, take home a souvenir in the form of their artform tattoo , which looks similar to Henna ink and enjoy the cultural dance. Guests are also welcomed to the stage to join in on the bamboo dance.The buffet lunch spread is delightful and the drinks served are refreshing.Our small family went together in one group, consisting of 2 senior citizens and 2 toddlers. The steps are gentle and cater to the toddlers and senior citizens. Entering the long houses, there are options for the traditional bamboo steps, for the immersive experience or the gentle steps. Everyone of us enjoyed the experience at Mari Mari Cultural Village! Definitely recommended!

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: If I were to recommend ONE venue in Malaysia which everyone should visit, that would be Mari Mari Cultural Village. Kind, helpful and friendly staff who will help you during the visit. Both adults and children will learn about history, self and community identities, survival as well as communication skills. I am so impressed and awed by what I learned from the 3 hours guided tour. Our guide, Mr Zul, did an excellent effort guiding us through different houses and educating us on history in an entertaining way. One of the houses has a built in traditional trampoline like floor ! I am also impressed by the dances and musical performances. Beautiful smiles everywhere, and one can see they are just happy, kind and proud doing what they do. There is also a sense of peace and tranquility in the environment which I love. Anyway, my deepest respect for everyone who works to keep the Village alive. I really do suggest everyone to go visit and support the place. The knowledge and experience are just precious.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Mari mari cultural village is a popular tourist attraction in Sabah Malaysia. It s a village that showcases the different ethnic groups in Sabah Malaysia. We avail the tour meals.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Mari Mari Cultural Village is a unique place that allows you to immerse yourself in the rich culture of Sabah s five main tribes the Kadazan Dusun, Rungus, Lundayeh, Bajau and Murut. Each of these tribes has its own unique history, traditions and lifestyle, which you can learn about during an interactive tour of the village. Visitors can see traditional houses, learn how to make fire with bamboo, learn about tribal tattoos and try local delicacies.Transport and accessibilityGetting to the village is relatively easy using the Grab app, which allows you to quickly book a ride from Kota Kinabalu. However, getting back can be more difficult finding a driver in the area can often require a long wait. So it s worth planning your visit in advance or considering an organised tour that includes round trip transport.Is it worth visiting?Despite the minor inconvenience of the return journey, Mari Mari Cultural Village is a place worth visiting. It s not just a museum, but a living space where you can experience authentic Borneo culture and get a feel for the old days. If you are interested in the history and traditions of indigenous tribes, this place will surely provide an unforgettable experience.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Mari Mari Cultural Village offers a well organised and thoughtful introduction to the traditional cultures of Sabah. The visit gives a good sense of how different indigenous groups lived, with the chance to see traditional homes, try local food and drinks, and learn about customs that are still respected today.Our guide for the English speaking group, Zul, was calm, clear and informative. He guided us through the experience with patience and knowledge, creating a comfortable atmosphere without rushing or overloading us.The whole Mari Mari team deserves credit for their effort everything was well prepared and ran smoothly, and it s clear how much care and pride they put into their work. From the performances to the details in each house, you can see a real dedication to keeping these traditions alive and sharing them in a respectful way.A valuable experience for anyone interested in local culture, presented with sincerity and professionalism.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Mari Mari was an absolutely unforgettable experience! The views and huts were absolutely beautiful. Learning all about Sabah s history and different tribes and groups was not only an amazing educational time, but it was also incredibly interactive. Our guide, Ben, did a wonderful job sharing stories and history as well as his sharing his own childhood stories which i loved hearing about. Everyone there was so kind and welcoming and I m really going to miss it there. title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: My visit to Mari Mari Cultural Village was one of the highlights of my trip to Kota Kinabalu. This place offers a beautifully curated and interactive glimpse into the diverse indigenous cultures of Sabah, all set within lush rainforest surroundings.From the moment we stepped into the village, we were welcomed warmly by our friendly and knowledgeable guide. The tour took us through several traditional houses representing different tribes the Dusun, Rungus, Lundayeh, Bajau, and Murut. Each home was brought to life with demonstrations and storytelling, from fire starting and tattoo ink preparation to blowpipe techniques and traditional rice wine processes.I especially appreciated the opportunity to try Kuih Penjaram, a sweet local delicacy made with rice and pandan soft, fragrant, and delicious! We also learned about native crafts and even sampled food cooked in bamboo. The Murut longhouse segment was fascinating rich with history, culture, and even a bit of dramatic flair.The surroundings were serene, with a peaceful stream running through the village adding to the charm. This wasn t just a passive museum style experience it was hands on, authentic, and respectful of the indigenous heritage.Highly recommended for anyone wanting to deepen their understanding of Sabah s people and traditions. Allocate at least half a day, bring water, and wear comfortable shoes you ll be walking through the forest and up bamboo stairs. It s worth every step!

Well ofcourse after witnessing the beauty of Mt. Kinabalu and the mountains. Now i know about Sabah and the people because of the experience i had at this village. This is a must go place while u at Sabah. It s really worth the price. The price is include the buffet at the end of session. U need to walk along the way..and sad to be inform, it s not the place for the handicap. U cannot use the wheelchair at this village. Please be alert.

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: We had a truly wonderful experience at Mari Mari Cultural Village in Sabah! It was both educational and immersive, offering us a fascinating glimpse into the diverse cultures and rich history of the indigenous tribes of Borneo. Every station was well thought out and gave us a deeper appreciation of the traditional lifestyles, customs, and crafts.A special shoutout to our guide, Nisa, who made the tour even more enjoyable. She was incredibly knowledgeable, professional, and spoke excellent English, which made everything easy to understand and engaging. Her passion for the culture really shone through in her storytelling and explanations.Highly recommended for anyone visiting Sabah and wanting to experience the heart and soul of local heritage. I think it had better gifts etc than the shop that you end at.We did the afternoon session and I wish I had booked the morning session. The morning session would time well to use the included buffet for your lunch.Definitely worth a visit!

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: We visited the Mari Mari Cultural village and great value for the tour, can very much recommend. I wasn t expecting much food wise as a vegan but there was a decent selection of food in the buffet suitable for vegans and everything suitable had a vegetarian label as well as ingredients written out so I felt at ease and didn t have to ask anyone if I could eat anything! The local food provided in the demonstrations was also accidentally vegan except for the bamboo cooked chicken rice, but the guide asked our group if anyone was vegetarian and to my surprise they had a chicken free option! Was very tasty and made my day they really thought about offering options which is not often on these kinds of tours. Would recommend to anyone interested to learn more about Borneo s ethnic minorities

title: Mari Mari Cultural Village
translated_review: Yesterday's visit to the Mari Mari Cultural Village turned out to be an unforgettable journey through time. Surrounded by the echoes of the past, I was deeply moved by how our ancestors adapted and survived with such limited resources, their wisdom is nothing short of remarkable. Carl, our passionate Dusun guide, shared his culture with such depth and clarity that everything I d once read in textbooks finally came alive before my eyes. It was a humbling and enriching experience that reminded me how much we can learn from those who came before us. The feeling of the kind of knowledge from textbooks from high school finally became real. Thank you to everyone there who explained everything so well that every aspect of the culture felt alive.

title: Maria Kek Lapis Sarawak
translated_review: Original Cheese Kek Lapis RM10.00Most of us would know of this Indonesian Cake known as Kueh Lapis but in Kuching, its known as Kek Lapis . This is another Kuching Speciality. This is the type of Cake that really takes alot of patience to make. Just looking at the intricate layers upon layers and you can tell that alot of work is gone into the making.Kek Lapis in Kuching are usually sold in a Thick Long Slab and mostly range from RM8.00 to RM15.00 and up. And from most of the Kek Lapis shops I visited, the prices are more or less the same throughout.There are many Kek Lapis Shops around and I chose this one specifically because they have 2 Adorable Cats within! They re so Adorable they re really Enjoying Themselves, Sleeping and Manja all over the place.The Staffs here at Maria are also very Friendly with a Smile and I chose their Classic Kek Lapis which is something called Original Cheese Of course there are many more flavours to choose from. There are so many Beautiful Variations of Kek Lapis, it almost feels like you re touring an Art Gallery. They even have a Free Sample station so you can have a try which Flavour you prefer before you make your decision.For this Original Cheese Flavour, the thing that I noticed first was that it had a very Strong Cheesecake like Smell. The Actual Cake don t actually have any Cheese Taste though, which was a slight disappointment, but the Butter taste is Very Prominent. Its not the Super Buttery type of Taste, but you can definitely feel its presence here. Personally, I also thought it tasted just nice and not too sweet.After tasting this, now I understand why this is called Kek Lapis rather than Kueh Lapis because this totally doesn t taste like how you d imagine it to taste. It really does taste more like a Cake than a Kueh. Texture is Soft, Sticky and Spongy, and I thought it tend to stick to my Throat often. This is perfect if washed down with some Tea or Coffee.RM10 for this size of Kek Lapis I feel is worth it just because of how much work it takes to make this.Personally, I don t know if this is really the Best Kek Lapis ever, but the Combination of the Friendly Staffs, and the 2 Super Adorable Cats is a Yes from me for Maria. I can recommend to come here if ya ll want to give Kek Lapis a try.Thanks for Reading. Come join my Facebook Food Blog A Solo Singaporean s Guide to JB Foods to view the Full Review with Photos.

title: Masala Medley Cooking Class

title: Masala Medley Cooking Class
translated_review: I attended the Masala Medley Cooking class on May 3rd 2025. At first, I was just interested in learning how to make indian s cruisine but after tasting everything she taught us to make, I can honestly say I would make the other recipes again. She demonstarted her techniques and how to make the dish, then set us off on our own to try the recipes while always attentively being close by if we ever needed help. The level of confidence that this class gave me with being able to make these dishes again was priceless. You can teach technique, but it s hard to teach confidence. Renee s somehow did it though. So thank you Renee for the confidence you gave me and for this fun and positive learning experience. To anyone who s thinking of taking this class in the future, I highly recommend it!!!!!!

title: Masala Medley Cooking Class
translated_review: We had a cooking class with Renee at Masala Medley and it was absolutely brilliant.We are currently travelling Asia and we ve done a cooking class in every country we have been too, and this one is by far the best even though it s brand new. The class itself is in a freshly renovated property that has a large seating area where you sit down, learn about all the spices, why and how to cut and mix the ingredients. You then move to the cooking area that s spacious and great decor. Once you ve cooked your dishes you head to another cozy and warm dining area to enjoy your food. The whole place is very rustic, warm, stylish, inviting and very clean. The ingredient pots, entire decor gives the whole place an authentic family feel to the place.Renee is a fantastic teacher and her Mum is great too. They were very informative about Indian spices and ingredients and how to use them, the recipes are family recipes which adds a great personal touch.If you are in Kuala Lumpur and looking for a cooking class we couldn t recommend it higher.

title: Mira Cake House (Kek Basah Sarawak)
translated_review: Mira Cake House Cawangan Keramat. Who misses Kek Lapis Sarawak Mira can have it here.... Various types of kek lapis can be bought at the Headquarters in Sarawak.. Usually Kek Lapis Sarawak will be served on special occasions. They are often baked for religious or cultural celebrations such as Eid ul Fitr, Christmas, Deepavali, Gawai, birthdays and weddings. People in Malaysia practice an open house tradition during any festival day, and the modern layered cakes way be served almost everywhere in Sarawak. to have derived from a form of European spit cake, which was made by the wives of Dutch administrators in Batavia during the colonial period and served during evening tea. It is believed that this type of spiced layer cake was introduced to Sarawak in the 1970s and 1980s by the Betawis from Jakarta. The Sarawak people then added new ingredients, flavors and colors that resulted in a new version of the layer cake being introduced and named as Sarawak layer cake. In addition, modern Sarawak layered cakes were inspired by Western cake making in the early 1980s, later modern designs to the traditional layer cake, along with new flavors were introduced.

title: Mira Cake House (Kek Basah Sarawak)
translated_review: Mira Cake House in Taman Keramat KL is the best if you want to find Sarawak layer cakes. Just enter the store, you will see all kinds of colorful layered cake options and it looks very interesting! It tastes really good, soft, fat enough and not too sweet. The service is okay, the staff are friendly and don't hesitate to explain the available flavors. It is suitable if you want to make your own food while hanging out in the evening or if you want to buy it as a gift for family and friends. In short, if you're craving Sarawak layer cake, Mira Cake House is definitely worth a try!

title: Mira Cake House (Kek Basah Sarawak)
translated_review: Mira Cake House is one of the most popular spots in Kuching for buying Sarawak kek lapis layer cake , and it definitely lives up to its reputation. The shop itself is bright, colorful, and welcoming, with rows of beautifully patterned cakes neatly displayed. Walking in, you immediately feel tempted to try everything because of how attractive the designs and colors are.The variety here is excellent. They have all the classic flavors such as butter, chocolate, pandan, and cheese, but also many creative ones that you don t often find elsewhere. What I really liked is that they allow you to sample different flavors before deciding which ones to buy. This makes it fun and also ensures you get exactly what you enjoy.Taste wise, the cakes are moist, rich, and flavorful, with just the right level of sweetness. They are packed nicely in travel friendly boxes, which makes them perfect for souvenirs. Prices are reasonable for the quality and craftsmanship, especially considering how labor intensive making kek lapis is.The staff are friendly, patient, and always ready to explain the differences between the many flavors. Service feels organized even when the shop gets busy.Overall, Mira Cake House is a must visit if you are in Kuching. Whether you want to bring home gifts, surprise friends, or just enjoy authentic Sarawak layer cake, this place will not disappoint

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Decided to at least do a cultural tour before we fly off in the evening and was not disappointed. The guides at Monsopiad heritage were very informative and friendly. We learnt a lot about their history and customs, got to see the actual residence of the great Monsopiad with the skulls, and the activities were fun too. To list some, there s food tasting, starting a fire with bamboo husks, blow darts, and also a dance performance. The whole visit takes a bit more than 2 hours.It is fairly simple to get to and from this place. We took a Grab from downtown KK, the drive is somewhere between 20 30mins, depending on traffic conditions. This is very convenient for those who decide to visit on a whim and do not want to join a tour group. Plus the communication from Monsopiad Heritage village is prompt via WhatsApp or their social media. So just check with them if you have any queries

We enjoyed all the performances, games and traditional food alcohol brewed by the villagers .You can buy tickets online or at the entrance. Rm75 Malaysians, rm85 non Malaysians We rented a car and drove ourselves to the destination, there is free parking. Do take note of the time of the tour at 11am 2pm only. Do bring along mosquito repellent or buy in store at the souvenir shop. We spent 3 hours here and its was worth every second.Please support this community as their funding from the government was cut and now solely rely on tourism.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Highly recommended! The staff were incredibly friendly and helpful, making our experience truly enjoyable. Our tour guide, Jus, was exceptionally patient and friendly, providing us with a wealth of information about Kadazan culture. It was a fantastic experience, and I can t recommend it enough!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I had 2 issues with the village. One is that it seemed to me that the hosts and performers don t do it from the genuine willingness to preserve their culture but for the tourists. Some staff seemed even not to enjoy it.. Secondly, there is an introduction missing as well as explanations of the activities we are invited to witness or take part at. To sum up great place to see and do but not very educative.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I had a great experience at Monsopiad heritage village today. I had Chela as a tour guide and she was extremely considerate during the entire tour and done whatever she could to make me feel like a precious guest. I really felt the customer experience was taken to another level because if her and the entire team s hospitality. I had enough time to see everything at my own pace without being rushed, I could ask questions, try the traditional food, play games, had a nice lunch outside the entry fee , even tried the traditional cigarettes, waw how the fire is made with bamboo, and a great performance where I could join and dance with the group. Not to mention I could also chose a souvenir a keyring of my choice part of the entry ticket 65 RM for foreigners . A more quite place than Mari Mari and less touristic, where you can go anytime during the opening hours to visit, but the shows are fixed at 11am and 2pm. Definitely worth it a visit!More reviews and details about Sabah and Borneo on my instagram account, ID iuliaalexandraciobanu

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I had loads of fun and learned a lot. The only thing that was not that great was that my guide was very unprepared. She would just repeat what was written and could not speak English well.. so I could not ask questions and as I was focused and interested I would have enjoyed doing it.There were some things that I have been wanting to try a type of flute that looks like an organ and one that you play with your nose and betel leafs but she said they were toys and that it was out... So I don t know if it was just her who didn t know how to play and where were the things were or if really these are things only for display. Because others were not display at all!! Like the tobacco which is super natural, and the worms.Also the show was great, but after they invited everyone to dance they sent us back super quickly, while I was enjoying interacting... They were kind of bored during the presentations....So there s great potential! But also room for improvement!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I really like the natural and sacred traditional surroundings. feels like ancient and ancestors spirituality all in one. Sacred stone to house skulls..it is part of the tour.The tour guide is very friendly and informative. We learned a lot of things about suku asli Sabah from here.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I took a Grab car here from KK and am so glad I did. For 65 MYR international tourist rate I was able to show up on a weekday around 10am and be assigned a guide to take me around the village for an hour and a bit. I get the feeling that on weekends or during peak season that booking in advance is advisable though.My guide spoke English and also doubled as a cameraman he excelled at both . He also ensured I got a front row seat to the 11am performance and encouraged me to join in the dancing when asked. I couldn t have asked for a better guide!Trying traditional Sabah food and drinks was a real highlight, as was learning how to use a blowpipe to shoot balloons, and trying my hand at the traditional dances.If you are interested in learning about the Kadazandusun people and want to visit a cultural village with a more personal touch, then this is an unbeatable experience!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I was just there yesterday came to KK spontaneously and initially read about cultural villages. I love my experience here, I was alone and my guides were very friendly kudos to Mimi, Dennis, and Kimi for the interactive tour and casual conversations! I was not able to book online and took a shot at walking in, huge plus that it s also reachable via Grab both back and forth. Everything was personal the food tasting, the traditions I was encouraged to practice to experience how the tribes did it, the tribal games, and the show which I also participated! From the moment I entered the village up to I left it, they were with me all the way! 3 If you re visiting, ticket is super worth it! just bring a mosquito repellant D

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: It was an enriching experience that brought me closer to my indigenous roots whilst having fun too with some traditional games our guide, Kimi, was great!! He brought stories and heritage to life with his passionIf you re in Sabah, do visit

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: It was nice experience! From knowing the famous headhunter s stories to tasting some food items and wine. My guide also showed us some herbs which was nice.U will be brought to this part where you can see the collection of skulls being hanged inside the home.The dance performance happens twice a day. It was nice appreciate the audience involvement

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: It was very interesting learning about the culture. Everything was fun to look and learn about. The tour guide was really nice and super chill. Definitely recommend.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: I ve been here twice once in 2013 with my eldest child and again this year with my younger one. Both times were awesome! Although on this visit, the site has slightly changed but the experience remained incredible. Our guide, Kimi, really was the best. He explained everything well, he was also very witty and attentive. Both my children and I have fond memories of this heritage village. I highly recommend! Terima Kasih, Kimi!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Just a small place compared to Mari Mari. Staff are super attentive and you can rent the costume for photos. Hopefully they maintain or decrease the entrance fee for Malaysian. It s RM55 kinda a lot. Location is good not that far

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Location is good and took about 20 minutes from KK town.It is very easy to get there by maxim or grabcar.If you walk in they provided including tour guide,free food tasting,and free drinks at cafe.After that you will enjoy mini games and traditional dance.For lunch package you need to book one week before and contact them through their Facebook official page for inquiries.I hope they can improve the bridge soon.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Love this place. You can learn about kadazandusun culture and the great headhunter warrior knowned as Monsopiad. There are kadazandusun traditional games that you are able to try, traditional food and drinks and cultural show. Great place to visit. Recommended!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Loved It! We enjoyed the natural and historic setting, the tour from Monsopiad s tribe ancestors, all of the activities, and the terrific show.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Made the right decision to visit this place. We truly enjoyed our experience there. Our guide, Jahan was friendly, knowledgeable and he made us feel welcomed. Thank you so much Jahan and team.I really recommend this place if you want to learn about local history in Sabah. Ticket price is affordable so come and bring your kids!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Monsopiad Heritage Village s commitment to presenting local history greatly pleased me. There was excellent information on the guided tour. It was visually stunning as well as educational. Our tour guide, Mr. Radzi, showed enthusiasm for imparting knowledge, and the interactive exhibits made the tour enjoyable. It s a perfect place for a family outing.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: My overall experience has been really great and enjoyable. The staff there is super friendly and I feels very welcomed. I love how the tour guide explained every little details about their cultures and histories.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Nice place to visit. With wonderful tour guide to explaining the culture.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: OA1nbd

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: One of, if not my favorite experience in Sabah.The local guides were extremely friendly and welcoming. We learned a lot about the local traditions, tasted the traditional food freshly prepared and rice wines. They have the Sago live worms too if you re somehow into that.They was a lovely live dance performance in the theater audiences are invited to join them on stage too Super duper fun!There was also an area where we could shoot blow darts, learn to start a fire the traditional way not easy! And fire slingshots.Definitely a MUST go if you re in KK not far drive from the city . RM55 for locals, worth it!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Our experience in the village was unbeatable. We took part in a lot of immersive activities, going through several sections where we did different things and learned about the history behind each of them. The attention we received was excellent. We were accompanied by a very attentive young woman who treated us wonderfully throughout the two hours. The dancers and musicians also performed just for us, and we were only two people. The whole experience was perfect. I highly recommend it.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Our first cultural experience here and really enjoyed the tour with our guide Kevin. Very easygoing and informative. Truly a place not to miss especially when really learning about the kadazan dusun.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: PLEASE DO COME HERE. EVERYTHING WAS EXCELLENT. THE PLACE, THE STAFF, EVERYTHING WAS SUPERB! RM55 for adults no student price ya. student still RM55 but it was amazing! you can just walk in there, no need to pre book. the place was easy to find. got ample parking space also!you get free food tasting, learn the history, trying out their traditional culture, played games and got one keychain as complimentary gift. PLEASE DO GO AND ENJOY.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Really worth visiting I was between Monsopiad and Mari Mari and am really glad I went for this one. Authentic, personalized experience Kevin was an awesome tour guide. Great traditional gong show dances to top it off apologies to the team for my awful moves!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Service 5 5 Price 3 5 rm55 adult local Area 4 5.Activity 4 5 Service is really good. From the beginning to the end. The staff there helped a lot with my son. Hope the owner can invest to attract more visitors. The place is interesting, it just needs more activity and maintenance. It's a pity to see the staff. Multitask. Tips make sure to bring insect repellent before arriving. Lots of mosquitoes.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Situated approx. 20 minutes by car from central KK, this Kadazan village tribe is organizing tours to learn about their customs, food and of course the famous headhunter Monsopiad himself with 42 skulls to prove it.I was lucky enough to have the place all for myself and I really enjoyed the interaction between the villagers and me during games and such.The price might seem a lot, RM85, but for me atleast, it was well worth it. Card payment accepted. Beer available for purchase. Booking via Whatsapp highly recommended.Truly a hidden gem. Even the taxi drivers haven t heard of this place and yet, it is more than worth the efford and money.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: The entry fee is very reasonable compared to the other more famous cultural village. It may be smaller, but has that personal touch. This is a privately owned business and isn t funded by the tourism ministry. Expect things a bit run down, but the service and friendliness of the staff is top notch. The location of the village is an actual village of the famous head hunter Monsopiad. The house of skull is worth a visit, if not for the visual, the story behind it. A must do in KK.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: This is a must visit if you are a fan of obscure cultural sites. A little on the dark side, as it is centered on a head hunting warrior from 300 years ago, but that is not the only focus of your visit.We had a great time here learning about the local tribes of Sabah, trying their foods and drinks, and playing their games. Blowdarts were my personal favorite.Our tour guide was super friendly and knowledgeable about local tribal history.Bring cash, as the only other payment option is bank transfer, and there is a small gift shop and cafe. You also have the chance to buy the food and spirits you sample.We spent just about 2 hours here. This would be a tad longer normally, but we arrived a bit late for the cultural shows.It is located a little off the beaten path, but we had no problem finding a Grab car to take us back to our hotel, and felt it was well worth the trip.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: This place is definitely a must visit if you re visiting Sabah. You get to learn quite a lot regarding the history and culture of Sabah and its people. The workers there are friendly, super accommodating and just splendid! Definitely recommended!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: Ticket price comes with a private tour by passionate and knowledgeable guides, cultural performance, tribe games, and local delicacies including liquor. However the place has many mosquitoes especially after rain so repellant is a must. Shoutout to Kimi for making the trip extra ordinary.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: very informative and entertaining with a versatile program ... the venue is not very big and luckily, for my taste, not totally flooded by endless columns of busses and rivers of tourists ... that would destroy the place within minutes! thank you, that you run this place with so much enthusiasm and love for your guests.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We did the tour yesterday with our in house guide, Mimi. She s very talented and know the culture very well. Great experience but we hope the management could do something about renovating the place. It s a bit run down especially the skull house floor. Anyway, lovely staffs with welcoming vibes.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had a fantastic day at Monsopiad Cultural village. Our guide Kimi was so knowledgeable and informative and made for a wonderful experience. We learnt so much about tribal life and traditions and involved us in all sorts activities. Such a great day

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had a great afternoon at Monsopiad cultural village. Easy to get to with Grab. Way less crowded than other cultural villages so we had a much more personalised experience. Our guide Kimi was awesome, very knowledgeable and taught us so much about a way of life that was a little bit gruesome and has pretty much disappeared from our not so distant past. I would thoroughly recommend this experience to everyone. For other westerns just make sure to take some mozzie repellent, it wasn t too bad but I could have done without that distraction. My wife took the photos so I have none to post but all the other reviews are right on the money.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had a great experience when our visit to this place. Started at 9am. We were welcome with their great staffs from the entrance.We booked our slot online and registered at the counter. We were assigned with this one great guy named Andy. Andy is great giving us information related to the place.Our first visit was the house of skulls. We do respect the rule where we cannot take any pictures while we are in the house.Next we visited local food booths before heading to the games area. I chose this place because the coverage is a bit smaller compared to Mari Mari. My parents will not be able to cover Mari Mari. Thats why i chose this place. The show was great and we do like it.Foods were given at the cafe for free and u may choose other foods too and need to pay some amounts.Souvenirs shop is located next to the main counter.Overall, me and my parents are very happy. Thanks Andy and Jep. We hope u will do well in Melaka in the future. U did great in whatever field that u are in.

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had a great morning at Monsopiad village all the staff were very helpful and friendly, particularly MJ ? who guided us around and spoke great english This place is so interactive, we got to try food, drinks, blow darts, slingshots, fire lighting and traditional dancing. We had lots of fun and highly reccomend a visit!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had an amazing experience here! The tour guide was very informative and had many interactive and hands on experiences such as learning to make fire, using blow gun and trying sago grubs. There was also a cultural performance where we were able to join and learn how to do some steps. Overall we loved everything and would definitely recommend to anyone that wants to learn more about the Kadazan dusun culture!

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had an amazing time here. This village is a must do if you re looking for somewhere not overcrowded with tourists. Special thanks to our guide Kimi! He is a really amazing guide and photographer and we had a really good time with him exploring the village. From watching cultural shows to dancing, trying different food, drinks, herbs and playing various games and challenges this was really great and we learned a lot about Kadazan and Murut culture. The staff is also very welcoming and nice. Thank you again Kimi

title: Monsopiad Heritage Village
translated_review: We had an amazing time in Monsopiad Heritage Village. Thanks to all the crew and our lovely tourguide, Ms Claire. Very informative and engaging indeed.Our kids had fun dancing with the tribe, appreciating its headhunting culture and rituals, taste test their delicacies of bambangan fruit wild ginger and hands on with the sago worm butod traditional blowgun sumpit .A must visit in KK.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: 10 10 experience would recommend to everyone! We chose pakage C and enjoyed the amazing hike with beautiful waterfalls. The guides were very knowledgeable and extremely friendly.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: 8km hiking.. Visited all 7 waterfalls. Wonderful experience. Sadly facilities was in bad shape when I was there. The last waterfall is in murky condition. But that doesn t stop me from enjoying the nature itself.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A bit greedy, not exactly customer focused. I messaged them to ask for suggestions about how a solo traveller would get there from KK. He offered to organise me a return trip for 250 MR. I told him Grab taxi charges 50 MR each way.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A cluster of gorgeous waterfalls, 7 to be exact. Trails are divided according to difficulty level and hours. I took trail B that covered 3 waterfalls, relatively easy hike. No leeches or pests.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A great place to be one with nature. The guides and staff are really friendly. U can buy food that u can take to the waterfall or light breakfast for yourself. Do make sure to have enough water, and sunscreen too.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A great place to relax, sightsee, and reconnect with nature. Guides are friendly and helpful too, do contact them if you are not sure which package to choose. The best part is it s quite easy to reach, not too far from the city.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A hidden gem place of Sabah, Malaysia. If anyone loves adventure, waterfall, or a short trek, they must visit this place. It s almost 50 km away from kota Kinabalu city. Grab Taxi self driven car is available. But best to take a self driven car from kota Kinabalu.Must need to pre book through their website. But if you are foreign visitor, you can book through their given contact number on the website, as the payment option is bank payment.They have 4 packages. One can choose according to their choice, strength and health condition. We have taken Package B which includes 3 waterfalls. I mostly loved the second waterfall named Piolungan Waterfall.With the package, a guide cost is not included. Must need to take guide. Our guide was super friendly, caring and funny. His name was kungkum. Best guide, I have ever seen. Gossip with the guide, they will share lot of information about the trek, village, waterfall and local stories Tips to visit there 1. Wear quick dry clothes, if you have plans to swim in the waterfall. Indeed, you can t resist swimming after seeing the beautiful waterfall. Once you put your foot in the cold water of the waterfall, you can t say no to swimming.Note They have changing facilities and washrooms for both male and female. Not a posh one, not a low condition. Basic facilities.2. Bring hat, sunscream, raincoat for sun and rain protection.3. Must wear a trekking shoe or sandal4. Bring some light food if you want to eat food during the trek5. Bring waterproof gadget cover if you want to capture some close pictures with the waterfalls.6. Skip to go in weekend days, if you want to enjoy trek and capture your good picture.5. Last not least keep your trash with you.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A nice place with a good view for hiking and many waterfall to look at and swim

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A very beautiful scenery of waterfall ...To come here you need to book through their website, no walk in visitor. There have 3 package available...package A you will meet only 1 waterfall, need to walk 3 km roundtrip suitable for beginner hiker ... Package B, you will be served 3 waterfalls, the trail take about 4 km roundtrip , moderate hiking level. And for package C you will meet 7 waterfalls through 8 km trail, it is a challenging trail.All those trail, you need to have a guide for every 5 person. Though the package A very simple and easy trail you need to pay for a guide too. As we are beginner so we only dare to take package A for starting, for survey the trail before we decided to take other package next time . Package A very easy but don t underestimate it. Please wear suitable shoes, as the trail can be slippery some time. From main point to the waterfall, you can walk leisurely, the path is only downhill all the way. but, to return back to the starting point, you need a lot of stamina because you have to walk uphill along the route.. for package A, pay for rm35 for a guide apply for 1 5 hiker

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: a very good place to do long distance hiking

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Adventerous and challenging trails. Tiredness will fade away once you reach the waterfalls

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: A very beautiful and clean waterfall for those who haven't been yet, don't miss the opportunity to go hiking and see for yourself.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: All the waterfalls are nice for those who are not intended to go for hiking would advise to go for Package A.... you may enjoy the fresh air and relax at the side

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Amazing experience hiking with my husband and our friendly guide Pastor Andrias. We took package C 8km , started walking just before 8am and finished before 1pm with a beautiful, refreshing swim stop at Murug Turug although it was just 2 of us and guide, and I think we walked quickly and didn t take many other breaks . Absolutely unbeatable rainforest waterfall and mountain views 80 in the shade which was a relief for those worried about heat and sunburn! . You can buy snacks and drinks at the start point. Quite challenging hiking and scrambling up rocks at times as well as a few places to wade through streams, but ropes provided where needed and should be a good level if you re relatively fit and healthy! Really good value for money, and we had a great experience. Thankyou!

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Amazing experience. Went for Trail B in a big group of 17. The guides were extremely helpful friendly. Will definitely come back again for another hike!

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Amazing short trek. Package B. Waterfalls are pristine. Some have fishes! Dont forget to bring clothes for a dip. And trash bags for cleaning.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Take package A.trail to the waterfall x la ssh.road is wide car path.toilet shower room is provided free and clean.guide ok,good.price of package RM30 please let me know earlier x enter with guide and certificate.hikers please prepare hat as the route is not exposed to the scorching sun

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Awesome hike through sabah s jungle. The waterfall is really refreshing and clean!! Tips use the kampung adidas

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Beautiful waterfall and great hiking experience. Remember wear socks as you changed to their provided shoes. The shoe provided good nonslip but will cut the feet.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Beautiful waterfall must visit when u travel to KK.I took package B, went to all three waterfalls. And all are amazing. It s bigger and taller than what I thought.Due to heavy rain 1 day before I visit, so I didn t manage to swim at the waterfall very strong current . So play at your own risk.My tour guide very friendly and patient. He is also my cameraman. Worth trip to visit. Hidden gem in Kk

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Beautiful waterfalls! We took package B, which covers 3 waterfalls, since we were saving energy to hike Mount Kinabalu the next day. Next time I return, I will take package C, which covers all 7 waterfalls. The trails are we went through were moderately challenging. We were lucky since we were the only people at the waterfalls at that time so we took our own sweet time to enjoy it. Please ensure to maintain the cleanliness and bring back your own trash! Also most of the guides are friendly local teenagers who can converse in English really well so please tip them generously it will certainly make their day.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Beautiful waterfalls! You will walk past some fruits plantation and local settlements. Overall recommended trip!

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Been here in September 2019. 3 packages to choose from package A is a 8 km hike with 7 waterfalls B is a 5 km hike with 3 waterfalls and C is a 3 km hike with 1 waterfall. Closing time for package A is at 9am as it will take a few hours

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: been here twice, I ll never be bored of this place. The waterfalls and trails are very clean. They provide adidas kampung rubber shoes , life jacket float rentals. You can also order maggi cup at the main waterfall buy fish food to feed the fishes. The guides are super informative friendly. Would recommend 10 10!

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: The best and the view of the waterfall are all beautiful

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: best hiking experince. 7 waterfall hopping

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Best hiking trail and waterfall view

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: You can get a purchase certificate and there are shoes to rent. A friendly, kind and sporty guide. Suitable for those who like e-stream. It is recommended to go early in the morning, don't worry it's not hot and there are many beautiful and mesmerizing scenes. Good luck, best, most importantly, enjoy

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Enjoy the hike with beautiful waterfalls...

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Everything is awesome, just the toilet out of order

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Extreme jungle tracking experience with beautiful nature view. Heaven of Waterfall .Helpful and experienced guides.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: For does of you who love adventurous and extrame trail, Package C is the one that recommended for you guy s. The 8km trail not only test your physical capabilities but also mentally too. All along the way you will be amazed by 7 waterfalls that Murug Turug Eco Resort have to offers.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Friendly guides. Recommended using rubber shoes adidas kampung for hikes.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Good experience. The guide is super friendly and talkative. Beautiful waterfalls with exciting hiking trail

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Good for VERY FIT Hikers. Guide friendly but at times can be really overwhelmed. Some part of the trails are difficult for beginners so better some training before start.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Good place to enjoy light trekking on the way to Mt. Kinabalu.There is several courses available and we chose the shortest course A around 3 hours for round trip , in which we experienced lots of up and down so make sure you have enough energy to bear these.At the end of the path, you reach at waterfall where you can swim and cool down your body.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Great experience with friends.. love the waterfall.. so calm and clean.. the trail is quite challenging for those who is not fit and low of stamina.. overall the trip was awesome and worth it..

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Great experience. Adventurous trails, helpful local guides and beautiful waterfall.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Great place. Relatively short walk to the water fall. Looks like there are plans to improve the main trail. Waterfall itself is great. Litter free. Guides are nor really needed but appreciated, informative and helpful. Swimming is great and refreshing. Lots of fish to see. Kids failed to catch any! Thanks Aria for a great time.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Heaven of waterfall! You guys not gonna regret it. Must visit place if you re more into adventure and hiking yeahh you must go here. Highly recommended

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: I went with the package B 4km trail , and it was way more adrenaline than I expected!! Hiked up and down for 3 waterfalls, sharing the same enthusiasm with the tour guide was kinda fun. I recommend going on weekdays rather than weekends to avoid crowds ps. they do rent shoe, go for it as you will be stepping on mud water

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: If you come to Sabah this is a must visit place. They have three packages to choose at affordable price. If you come with kids you can either choose the Pakej A 3km to a waterfall or Pakej B 4km with view of 3 waterfalls.No worry, the trail is not that difficult but i would advice you to wear a proper hiking shoes.. please avoid slippers or sandals as some part of the path is sippery..The waterfall view was great.. prepare to dive in.. best!!

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: I m not exactly an active person, and I did this with no prior practice but managed to finish the track for 4km with 3 waterfalls. The going part was fun and wasn t very tiring, but the coming back was soooo tiring, hahaha, cause it s

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: It is a big place. Very wide and far. For experienced hikers would be a nice place ro go to. There are a total of 7 waterfalls. They provide 3 packages and depends on which package you take that you will get to visit the waterfalls. Packages A, B and C. I took package C that brings me to all the waterfall but i was not an experienced hiker so was too tired to go to all the waterfalls.They also provided a ride back near the end for the hikers who were to tired to hike back to the starting point. Have to pay rm30 one way.Don t forget to bring your own food. Waters there numerous water spring that you can drink from.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Just got back with a bunch of my friends taking on the Ultra Pakage C 8km Hike. At the entrance there are shops available for soft drinks and foods, there is even a hiking shoe at the administration. There are camp sites and only one available homestay near here too. The staff is friendly and experienced. The grueling hike starts off with 3 km walk down the hill to the first waterfall, this was the easy part before climbing the steep hill to the tip of Malaskalas here it starts to test your endurance, traversing the forest before reaching your goal the Murug Turug. Literally not for newbies but it is for experienced adventurers.

title: Murug Turug Eco Tourism
translated_review: Murug Turug Waterfalls, located in Minangkob, Tamparuli, is a hidden gem for hiking enthusiasts. Opt for Trail C, and you ll be rewarded with an exhilarating trek that features seven stunning waterfalls. The trail is quite challenging, with quite steep ascents, rocky paths, and occasional river crossings, making it perfect for those seeking a bit of adventure.Each of the seven waterfalls along the route offers a unique spectacle, from gentle cascades to thunderous torrent. The lush surroundings and pristine nature create a serene and captivating atmosphere. The highlight is the 5th waterfall, which boasts a beautiful and majestic flow, perfect for a well deserved rest and photo opportunity.Overall, Murug Turug Waterfalls provides an unforgettable hiking experience, combining physical challenge with natural beauty. Ensure you re well prepared with sturdy footwear, ample water, and snacks to fully enjoy this remarkable trail.

translated_review: Went with a group of friends for the package C and package B Package C was advertised as 8km in actuality it was 12km, and Package B is about 4km or less but to be honest, it wasn t that hard. title: myBatik (by appointment)
translated_review: I had the wonderful opportunity to attend a batik craft cooperation event, and it was truly an eye opening and enriching experience. From learning the history of batik to trying the actual wax resist dyeing technique, every step felt deeply rooted in tradition and creativity.Working alongside skilled local artisans gave me a new appreciation for the effort, detail, and passion that goes into every piece. It was more than just an art workshop it was a connection to heritage, community, and sustainable craftsmanship.What stood out most was the spirit of cooperation how local communities, social enterprises, and participants came together to preserve and promote this beautiful art form while also creating economic opportunities.I left the event not only with a beautiful, hand dyed piece I made myself, but also with a renewed respect for Malaysia s cultural richness. Highly recommended for anyone who values creativity with purpose.

There are two styles that you can choose from block stamping or free hand known as chanting .Great for newbies like me or more experienced patrons, Emilia provides you with everything that you need, including encouragement, patience and friendly conversation.I tried my hand at both block stamping and free hand, and the experience has been one of the highlights of my trip to Malaysia. I recommend it without reservation.Note payment for the courses can only be made in advance via bank EFT transfer.

title: myBatik (by appointment)
translated_review: MyBatik is not just a place to purchase beautiful Batik pieces it s an experience steeped in passion and expertise, thanks to the delightful Emilia. From the moment I stepped into Emilia s workshop, Emilia s warm personality shone through, making me feel like an old friend rather than just another customer.What sets MyBatik apart is Emilia s genuine enthusiasm for Batik and the arts. Our conversation flowed effortlessly from discussing the intricate designs of Batik to sharing travel stories and anecdotes about family and parenting. Emilia s depth of knowledge about Batik was evident, but what truly stood out was her eagerness to share her passion with others.It s refreshing to support a business where the owner s love for their craft is palpable. With Emilia, you re not just buying a piece of Batik you re investing in an art form that holds a special place in her heart. Her generosity in sharing her expertise adds an extra layer of value to each purchase.In a world where commercialism often overshadows authenticity, MyBatik is a breath of fresh air. Emilia s dedication to her craft and her customers is evident in every aspect of her business. Whether you re a seasoned Batik enthusiast or a newcomer to the art form, a visit to MyBatik is sure to leave you inspired and enriched.

title: MyBatik Art Gallery
translated_review: I had the wonderful opportunity to attend a batik craft cooperation event, and it was truly an eye opening and enriching experience. From learning the history of batik to trying the actual wax resist dyeing technique, every step felt deeply rooted in tradition and creativity.Working alongside skilled local artisans gave me a new appreciation for the effort, detail, and passion that goes into every piece. It was more than just an art workshop it was a connection to heritage, community, and sustainable craftsmanship.What stood out most was the spirit of cooperation how local communities, social enterprises, and participants came together to preserve and promote this beautiful art form while also creating economic opportunities.I left the event not only with a beautiful, hand dyed piece I made myself, but also with a renewed respect for Malaysia s cultural richness. Highly recommended for anyone who values creativity with purpose.

translated_review: I booked my cooking class last minute with Khalid via email and he responded to my request within 15 minutes of my enquiry. I had one to one coaching for the afternoon.We made six dishes Nasi Lemak Coconut flavored rice , Char Kway Teow Fried Flat Rice Noodkes , Egg Plant Sambal, Malay Chicken Curry and Banana Fritters.Khalid was very knowledgable and did not just show me how to make the dishes but discussed the mix heritage food culture in Malaysia for a good insight into the use of the main flavors, fragrances, condiments and garnishing used in each dish.The course ran from 3pm until 6 45pm. I prepared each dish and as we cooked Khalid discussed how to taste and know that the dish was developing well. We sat down to eat each of the six dishes. My only regret was I don t have the biggest appetite. Khalid did however pack up the Banana Fritters which my partner devoured gratefully in the hotel room.It was a super afternoon and I cannot recommend Mypintu Plate and Khalid enough after the course Khalid gave me guidance on further reading and equipment that I can get to prepare the dishes that we prepared and even sent me photos and videos of me at work in the kitchen.Thank you Khalid and Mypintu Plate

title: MyPintu Plate
translated_review: Khalid is a lovely man but I want to be honest about the cooking class for others reading this review. My husband and I have done many cooking classes all over Asia and this is the first one we found really disappointing.To highlight the main issues the class is meant to be 3 hours. It actually lasted 4 hours, and the first 1.5 hours were just sat around a table with a weak ceiling fan with a lot of the time being Khalid showing us pictures from recipe books. Khalid prepared all the ingredients in advance amount, chopping etc . At times we were asked to identify ingredients, or do simple things like tie a knot in a pandan leaf which was not very engaging. the ingredients were shared between the whole class and dished out by khalid so even though we had our own pans to make rendang curry, we were essentially just stirring the pan as khalid added the ingredients to the pan for us. We did have a little control over adding more water if needed, and we requested extra sambal because the sambal was made by Khalid one portion for everyone to share there was not enough for everyone which meant people were asked to take some off of their plates to give to others, I ve never seen something like this before It was the same with the char kuay teow but worse as this is a quick 4 minute cook so waiting for him to dish out each person s ingredients meant things got burned eg. Garlic and the taste was not good. Khalid also took food from everyone s char kuay teow to taste but put it all on one plate for himself anyway so it wasn t that there was any individual feedback and he was the one who had added the ingredients and he also concluded there wasn t much taste. Not sure what the point of this was except that he didn t have his own portion of food to eat? there was no air con in the kitchen where we were cooking, there was a fan but we couldn t have it on because the flame in the stoves were then affected meaning it was uncomfortably hot in the small kitchen. We were given room temperature water maybe an hour into the session the session didn t seem well planned out, it was often jerky and felt quite chaoticKhalid seemed well intentioned and took some photos during the class and sent some recipes after also but I personally wouldn t recommend this class even though it is affordable

title: MyPintu Plate
translated_review: The man was lovely but you spend two hours sat around his dining table listening to him talk about the dishes and ingredients before you even start any cooking. It took an hour for him to put a pot of rice on.You don t spend any time prepping ingredients, instead you watch him do it.When you do eventually start cooking, he adds all the pre cut and prepared ingredients into your pan for you, all you do is mix it all up at the end.It s fine if you re completely new to cooking and want a lot of detail and information about the ingredients and process, but if you want a proper hands on cooking class, where you cook alongside an instructor from the start, then I d give this a miss.It s really not for anyone looking to actually improve their cooking or add anything to their repertoire.When I voiced if we were going actually be doing any cooking, I was told I was impatient and that it was important to listen to each step. I don t disagree it s important to listen, but it doesn t take two hours to explain each step of cooking a new dish. It s absolutely possible to explain the recipe as we re cooking it individually.

title: Natural Batik Village
translated_review: A Cultural Gem in Kuantan Hands On Batik Experience at Natural Batik VillageI visited Natural Batik Village located in Balok, Kuantan, Pahang, and it was a truly enriching and enjoyable experience!The location is spacious and wheelchair accessible, making it suitable for visitors of all ages and mobility levels. I had the chance to observe how Pahang style batik block printing is traditionally made. The staff were friendly and welcoming they explained the batik making process in detail, which made the experience even more meaningful.After that, I explored the retail section where they sell a wide variety of batik fabrics, as well as batik clothing for men, women, and children, in many beautiful designs and patterns. Pahang batik has its own unique style, distinctly different from the batik found in Kelantan or Terengganu, which makes it special and worth discovering.One of the highlights was the DIY batik painting area outside, where visitors can colour their own batik block art and bring it home. This is a fun and creative activity, perfect for families or school groups.Overall, Natural Batik Village is a must visit destination in Kuantan for anyone interested in Malaysian culture, traditional arts, or just looking for a meaningful souvenir.

title: Natural Batik Village

Overall an interesting and pleasant visit.

title: Natural Batik Village

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: AMAZING! In my opinion l, one of the best ways to get to know a country is to eat the food or cook and thus get to eat but also learn about local ingredients, new styles of cooking and combing food. Nazlina was so sweet and taught us a lot in just a few hours! We did the morning tour booked late night the day before but would recommend booking at least a day or a few before which lasted from 8am until 1pm. It was just me and my two friends plus another couple and it was nice to only be 5 people and really get to cook and learn with Nazlina s full attention. The tour started with a market tour to buy all ingredients for our 3 dishes and it felt more like a food tour because we tried sooo many different things from each stand! The locals were super friendly, the vibe was laid back and relaxed and extremely clean. After the tour we went to Nazlina s kitchen and after a short overview of the dishes we were going to cook, prepared everything. We made everything from scratch and it felt amazing in the end to see the colorful dishes and taste the rich spices that we had prepared ourselves!! Nazlina guided us throughout the process, there was no rush, and everyone was very involved. What a wonderful experience, thank you so so much!

We didn t get to learn much beyond the recipes once we started cooking. But still we had a great time.
