title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Ms. Nazlina is a spectacular teacher with great cooking skills. Going throught the market to pick out the ingredients you are about to cook with is an amazing experience. It was great to see her bring together ingredients based on

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: My partner and myself took a class with her. It was a very informative and fun experience. We had several tasty snacks going through the market. We loved her outside kitchen setup and the recipes. We cooked a vegan variant and it was no problem

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: My third trip to Penang and a another great day cooking with Nazlina. The market tour was great, Nazlina s knowledge of food is second to none , i was introduced to herbs and other ingredients i had never heard of let alone tasted. Who knew that the local ginger is so much more fragrant than the regular ginger we all know. Nazlina also takes you on a tour of the streets around the market showing you intersting spots and giving information about food and local culture. There is plenty of opportunity to ask questions.Nazlina has moved to new premises, easily accessible with easy access for all. There are various cooking stations so just like before everyone can have a go at chopping ,slicing grinding and cooking. On this occasion we cooked outside under cover which was great on a hot day.Nazlina is a brilliant teacher and is very intuitive so will tailor tasks to your ability. A kind and patient teacher ,Nazlina guided me through the process of making a spice paste for the chicken rendang using a traditional grinding stone and roller batu giling . It really is cooking from scratch, it s authentic and lots of fun.Once all the dishes were cooked we got to sit and eat. We sat at a big table where we could chat and enjoy the fruits of our labour. On this occasion we made fish asam laksa , chicken rendang and a sweet called kuih kosui.For me a trip to Penang is not complete without a cooking session with Nazlina. I will definitely come back to Penang and i will definitely book another cooking lesson with Nazlina. When you encounter someone so passionate about the food and culture of this amazing place its hard not to be infused with excitement.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: My wife and I enjoyed the market visit and cooking lessons a lot. Nazlima and her assistant gave heaps of useful information during our walk and we tasted many products from local vendors. Each couple in our group made all the dishes themselves, a real hands on experience. Recommended.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: My wife and I had a great day in the market and kitchen with Nanzalina. She was friendly and knowledgeable while taking us around time and allowing us to experience the city.Look forward to making the Beef Rendang at home!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina is a fountain of culinary knowledge with a great sense of humour to match! . I came with two friends, having first taken this class 10 years ago, and enjoying it so much that had to return with my friends it was easily our favourite part of the trip. Every stop of the market tour and walking trip was peppered with lots of tips, knowledge and history and Nazlina made sure to ask abour dietary preferences as we shopped for our lunch and even went so far as to procure some nutmeg jam from a friend when we asked where we could get it . Being able to cook an entire feast wiyh multiple local dishes from scratch, and then sit to enjoy it together was great and have left with lots of techniques and tips that I will be trying again. Look forward to coming back very soon thank you Nazlina!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina is not only a passionate and knowledgeable cook but a gracious host as well. Her market tour was filled with stories about the city and culture. Her advice and insights never ended as we cooked beef rendang, roti canai, and four angle bean salad. Many thanks to Nazlina and her team for an incredible experience and delicious meal, well worth the work!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina picked us a group of 3 up near Pulau Tikus market where we shopped for fresh ingredients. She clearly has worked hard to make relationships with the sellers, and everyone seemed pleased to see her. Afterwards, she gave us a quick tour of her neighborhood, and then we headed to her cooking studio to prep all of the fresh herbs and produce for our meal. The food was incredible, and Nazlina is an expert in both palate and culinary knowledge. We can t recommend her cooking course enough!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina took a booking at short notice for New Year s Eve afternoon. We felt very lucky to have her teaching to our small family group. We went to an afternoon market and sourced fresh ingredients for our desired menu she asks you what you want to make, so it s worth having thought about this before the market and cooking class . We had returned from a hike directly to the class without eating and she bought us some food and recently made spring rolls by her and a previous class so we wouldn t be hungry. Throughout the cooking she instructs the preparation and tells stories of her past which made for a lovely experience for all of us. She imparts lots of knowledge and tips around all the food and cooking also. You can make almost anything I m sure from any local cuisine she knows it all. We made vegetable spring rolls, Laksa chicken and seafood chicken , Sambal and sticky rice witch coconut topping as no ripe mangoes were available for mango sticky rice at the market. The sticky rice with coconut topping was incredible! I can t wait to make it for guests! She can cater for vegan vegetarian non seafood also but it helps to know this in advance for her and planning as her classes can be bigger in numbers than 4. The cooking class is in her large garden beneath a roof outdoors and is a lovely setting. Her price is competitive and very reasonable considering all of the above and the duration of the whole experience. Thank you Nazlina for the wonderful experience which I m sure all 4 of us will remember forever.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s class was absolutely fantastic. She was willing to accommodate a late booking the night prior and also was willing to adapt the menu for dietary restrictions for my partner. Her love for the community was evident during the market tour and she pointed out ingredients, stalls and history that we never would have been exposed to otherwise. The class itself was fast pace and wonderfully fun with it all culminating in a sit down experience with nazlina to enjoy the work. Would highly recommend to anyone trying to experience a bit of Penang s food culture first hand!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s cooking class was a wonderful experience!We started by visiting several local markets, meeting the people behind the ingredients, and enjoying a delicious breakfast together. I got to choose which dishes I wanted to cook, and back at her lovely garden kitchen, she guided me through every step with a smile and a lot of patience.Highly recommended if you re curious to recreate at home the amazing recipes you ve discovered on your trip. I ll definitely come back next time I m in Penang!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s cooking class was amazing! It started with a really helpful tour of the market and surrounding area. She recommended great bakeries, coffee shops and food vendors to go to. Then we travelled to the cooking school! Nazlina is a great teacher and has so much knowledge about Malaysian cooking. We learnt so much! Thank you Nazlina, would recommend to anyone!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s market tour and cooking class was the perfect introduction to Malaysian cooking. The market tour gave us so much information we d never get going to the market on our own. Her outdoor kitchen space is well equipped and very comfortable for a group. Nazlina was very generous with her information and time. She s a great instructor and the food we made and ate with her was was some of the best we had in Penang.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlinas class was very interesting.We booked the class two days prior for 260 RM per person 05 24 . Having to pay in advance you ll pay additional 6 for using PayPal, no fees for cash makes it a bit inconvenient for tourists like us.Because we usually cook vegetarian she changed the menu for us spontaneously and arranged with the 2 other participants! At the class, after us admitting, that we are omnivores, but simply cook veggie at home, she introduced some fish sea food into our menu. I would have preferred a full vegetarian meal, but didn t pay proper attention at the market to ask for this. Kinda my fault.We meet early in the morning for breakfast and a food market tour filled with street food. The whole group was already very full after that! Actually, the street food was too much to cook after, we felt not really hungry even after we had cooked our dishes a bit sad, as this was my main goal of the cooking course.Afterwards we cooked our dishes in pairs. Not every participant does everything, which I usually prefer. This way, I didn t learn each step of the dishes as I was doing something else. I can imagine the bigger the group, the less you ll learn individually.Our food was rather simple, but quite tasty and unusual for Westerners. Nazlinas approach to salad was interesting and the Nasi Lemak was very tasty!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s classes are cool. Firstly, she takes you around the food stalls and the market and explains all about Malaysian ingredients. After that, she takes you to her garden to make some Malaysian dishes. Apart from the experience, she is nice and speaks really good English. It was a really good experience

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s combined market tour and cooking class was excellent. My daughter and I found her most informative and entertaining. During the market tour we learned so much about Malay and Chinese traditions, food and recommendations on places to eat. The cooking class was also brilliant. We cooked three delicious dishes at her home. We both thoroughly enjoyed it. Great value for money in my opinion.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s cooking class was one of our absloute highlights during our trip through Malaysia.The class begun with a short and local market tour where we got information and bought our fresh ingredients. We could also taste some typical meals from Penang s kitchen. Afterwards we went at her house to cook our very own dinner. Nazlina is a great cook and gives clear instructions so that you can learn how to make this delicious meals yourself. Vegetarian options were no problem!Would certainly recommend.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Nazlina s cooking class was spectacular and really fun the food we made and even the food places we visited was delicious and I wish I could eat it every day

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a great time with Nazlina today. After breakfast in a coffee shop and a visit to the market where she took the time to explain everything to us, we cooked 3 Malaysian dishes at her home. Sparkling and full of humor, the morning was full and so were our bellies.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a great morning with Nazlina. Great local breakfast in a cafe, then visit to different markets where she explains different vegetables, fruits, spices, etc... and finally cooking class.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: One of the best presents we have ever received. From the start to the finish of our day we learnt new things about Malaysia and Malaysian cuisine.Nazlina was informative, patient and easy to understand and communicate with. It was hands on and we had so much fun. We have a new appreciation for even the simplest Malay dishes.We were exhausted but full and very happy when we left. Another highlight of the day was meeting some really nice people who were also doing the class.Really enjoyed our experience.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Plenty of points regarding Nazlinas Spice Station were already mentioned in older reviews and I can confirm most of them. We also had a lovely market tour and some snacks and coffee before starting the cooking class. We could ask plenty of questions throughout the day and prepared three different dishes that tasted great.Main point of criticism is the high price as I felt it is overpriced. I understand that prices for groceries increased lately but it s still too expensive for what you get.Second, and I think this is a huge no go Nazlina took aside the remains of our cooked dishes for her son to eat for lunch. I truly would ve liked to take it with me to enjoy some of it in the afternoon as I paid a lot of money for it.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Probably best cooking school at Penang. Its peculiarity is that Nazline the Chef that you to the market first and apart from cooking explains also products. Great way to experience local cuisine.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Really enjoyed the experience of learning to cook Malaysian recipes with Nazlina. She even came up with a brand new recipe in order to teach me how to make a dish suitable for my wife and daughter who cannot eat chilis. The shopping for ingredients was informative and it was fun to see the local morning market. The cooking was hard work, but made for a rewarding lunch! Thank you Nazlina!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Really good class, assuming the best of Penang you can tell the teacher is really involved in the community as she got her product from the local market. The recipe are excellent and the food is really tasty, everything is well explained and she manages to make people participate. Big thanks for the volunteers as well who were always here to clean the dishes. We made quite a mess! To sum up, totally recommended to anyone wishing to learn more about Malay cuisine !

title: Nazlina Spice Station

Nazlina was so kind and had amazing stories. She tailored the menu for us as vegetarians. Could not recommend it enough.

title: Nazlina Spice Station

title: Nazlina Spice Station

It was very educational and she made it fun too. We ate our dishes family style with the other students. We had an amazing experience.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We booked Nazlinas cooking class a last minute thing and we re not disappointed!She started by showing us around the morning markets, foods unique to Malaysia and a good insight into the lifestyleThe cooking class was super fun and I d definitely recommend!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We did a Cooking class with Nazlina and it was a wonderful experience. We asked for vegan dishes up front which was no problem at all. Also we were able to book only one day in advance. The dishes and the class was very nice, including a breakfast and walk over a few markets. 10 10 would recommend.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We enjoyed this experience so much, a real highlight of our trip. The guided food tour provided us with so much insight into the food and history of Georgetown and we were able to ask plenty of questions. We also had plenty of snacks from the stalls which was great! The breakfast and coffe at a local bakery was delicious too. The cooking class that followed was incredible, it was very detailed and fun. It was a pleasure to share the experience with Nazalina and learn from her fantastic skills. I highly recommend this experience and feel it was such good value for money considering it included a very thorough food tour, snacks, wonderful breakfast, hours of Nazalina guiding us with our cooking and then the delicious meals we made! Loved it!

title: Nazlina Spice Station

We cooked 3 differents main course and it was a really nice experience, we loved it.And Nazlina smile all the time and is very nice, we really had a great time.Thank you again!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a fantastic time with Nazlina. You get a food stall and market tour as well as a cooking class! Come hungry. Nazlina explained everything so well and easily adjusted for us vegitarians. Don t miss this.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a great day with Nazlina. We started the morning with breakfast and traditional tea and coffee and then visited a local food market to try some things and get some fresh ingredients for the cooking class. Then we went to Nazlina s house where she has a beautiful kitchen setup in her garden and made some lovely dishes, which we had for lunch afterwards. Nazlina is very kind and funny and tells vert passionately about the local kitchen. Would definitely recommend to book her cooking class!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a great experience with Nazlina making a delicious chicken curry and a yummy strawberry sago dessert. My favourite element was the tour of the market to collect some ingredients for our dish Nazlina was very knowledgeable about all the food on offer and also allowed us to try some samples of different local street snacks. I can highly reccomend!!!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had a wonderful experience with Nazlina s cooking class and market tour and would highly recommend it! Every aspect of this tour was well organized and thoughtful. Nazlina s communication during booking was prompt and clear and the market tour which included breakfast, stops at several street food stands, and stops to purchase all the ingredients for the day s class was informative, interesting, and fun. Nazlina managed to easily corral our group we are a couple, and there was also another group of 6 on the tour through the market, explaining the history of each food we tried, describing each ingredient, and keeping everyone on track. Once we got to her lovely house, she had us try multiple local fruits rambutan and mangosteen, while she got the kitchen organized. We then spent hours in her amazing outdoor kitchen, where she explained each dish, delegated tasks to each of us, and guided us through preparing and eating a wonderful meal. Nazlina was funny, kind, and provided a unique, personalized experience that would be difficult to replicate. There are other market tours and cooking classes out there, but it s hard to imagine anything better than this experience!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had an amazing time at Nazlina s today! We joined a morning session 6 of us total , and had breakfast, food tour, market visit and then went back to Nazlina s and cooked three amazing dishes. Great atmosphere, N has so much knowledge to share about the food, cooking techniques and secret recipes. Really good value for money as well, would highly recommend!

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We had an amazing time with Nazlina with a complete experience around food starting with breakfast, tour of the local market with some pancakes, followed by the actual cooking class in a master chef setting. The class itself was very hands on and we prepared delicious meals. Looking forward to make them again.We highly recommend to everyone who is looking to learn Malay cuisine.

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We really enjoyed our class with Nazlina! The market tour was fun and informative and the cooking itself was great. She easily accommodated our vegetarianism and the food we cooked was some of the tastiest we had in Malaysia. She sent the recipes after the class and gave tips on replacements for ingredients which can be hard to find at home. We re already planning to recreate the dishes now we ve returned from our trip

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We signed up on a whim for a cooking class with Nazlina. Little did I know we would be entering a Malaysian cuisine odyssey of great proportions!! Nazlina is not only one of the world s foremost experts on Malaysian cuisine, her knowledge

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: We spent a wonderful morning with Nazlina, experiencing the wholesale markets, the roti manufacturer, shopping for the ingredients to the recipes we had decided to make, which were customized to suit the tastes of our two children age 8 and 10 . Nazlina is a very engaging and was especially charming with the kids, trusting that they could rise to the challenge of chopping, and kneading, and cooking. Note that you do have to pay in full for the class ahead of time, but Nazlina handles everything very professionally. A great glimpse into tasting Penang.

title: Nazlina Spice Station

Nazlina explained all the sights and smells, sampling as we went. Then back to her house to prepare and cook Roti Canai, Murtabak, Pickled Salad, and Chicken Kapitan. Nazlina is a lovely soul with a very friendly disposition and a wonderful sense of humor. She is serious about Malay food. We loved our time with you Naz. Terima Kasih Banyak!

title: Nazlina Spice Station

title: Nazlina Spice Station

title: Nazlina Spice Station
translated_review: Writing this review as a volunteer from the United States and not a paying customer.At first we were excited to learn cooking and experience malay culture and were planing to stay 5 weeks. After one week things were going okay naz takes the approach of the hells kitchen style chef in her cooking class which is off putting as we were volunteers from across the world not employees. Yelling and making us uncomfortable at the kitchen in front of her customers as if we should know exactly what to do and where things go after 1 day experience. The days in the kitchen were long one day we left the house at 7am and didnt get back until 8 30pm not exactly the hours referenced on the wwoofing page. The environment of the house was off putting and it felt like we didn t belong. Naz never asked us anything about ourselves or tried to connect with us or teach us about malay culture. Basically naz only talked about herself and didn t seem to have interest in anything else then she started to use her Hells Kitchen style attitude at the house and thats when we knew we had to move on. Basically we felt that all our efforts were not appreciated and that naz was generally unhappy and taking it out on us. We stayed 2 weeks when we intended 5. This is not what the wwoofing page is set up for and i do not recommend you stay work for naz. We do not ever leave negative reviews but felt that we had to voice our opinion for the benefit of others. People should not be spoken to or treated in this was especially in a volunteer work atmosphere.Do not support the poor treatment of volunteers!. Look elsewhere for this experience as unless big changes are made to Nazalinas attitude and format this is not the place to receive this experience.

title: Nohutu Ecotourism
translated_review: First time coming here. Beautiful surroundings. The staff is friendly and very helpful. There are only three things that I think need improvement. 1. The phone number given on the official Facebook page and also on Google Maps. It's best to put the phone number of the staff at the actual location. I called the number provided to make a reservation. Because our family came on a public holiday. The answer I got, you can come walk in only. When I asked about the hut that I wanted to be booked by the person who arrived earlier than us, the answer from the owner was no. On the day of the incident, I called the owner because I wasn't sure about the location, but he didn't answer. Luckily there is a number on the sign in front of the entrance for me to call. When I arrived at the registration point, I showed the picture of the hut that I wanted, the staff informed me that it was an old picture and that the new hut on the site had been booked by people for 2 nights. Thankfully there was still an empty hut next to it. Since I am bringing elderly people, so I am looking for a location that is not too far to go down to the water teeth.2. The driveway inside feels like it needs improvement. There is an uneven part of the road that makes it difficult for vehicles to enter. Since the surface of the road in the uneven part is placed medium-sized stones that are quite smooth. Vehicles can still enter, just be a little wary considering the journey to get to the campsite is a little far inland. Worry if damage to the vehicle will make it difficult to return. Since not all visitors come from nearby areas. 3. Bathroom and toilet facilities. The bathroom is very dark and the door cannot be closed from the inside. There needs to be help from outsiders. The floor is slippery. This is quite difficult and dangerous when carrying the elderly. In addition, if possible, to increase the number of existing bathrooms to cover the capacity of daytrip visitors. For the rest, I really praise the staff on duty. Very friendly and helpful. The hut we rented was left by the previous user in a dirty condition but the staff quickly cleaned it and changed the new plastic waste. I requested for an additional chair and it was delivered quickly. The place is beautiful, clean and the river is not too deep. Both children and parents enjoy it. Highly recommended to come and relax here with the family. I have attached a photo of the phone number of the staff who can be contacted to make a reservation.

title: Nohutu Ecotourism
translated_review: We arrived at Notuhu Ecotourism around 4 30 PM for a brief visit and did not stay overnight. The entrance fee was RM6 per person, which is quite affordable. Parking was easy and conveniently located near the entrance.The highlight of the visit was the stunning view of Gunung Kinabalu. Although the peak was partially obscured by clouds when we arrived, the surrounding scenery was still breathtaking. The temperature was pleasantly cool, making it comfortable to explore the area. The river water was exceptionally clean, cool, and fresh, perfect for a quick dip or just enjoying the natural ambiance.Facilities included well maintained toilets and a small surau, which I used. The surau was modest in size, resembling a small chalet, but it served its purpose well. There is also a food stall on site offering local snacks and meals, which added convenience for visitors.One downside is that the facilities provided for the RM6 entrance fee are quite basic, so I would rate the facilities themselves only 2 star. However, the natural beauty and peaceful environment more than make up for this.Internet connectivity was surprisingly good, allowing us to stay connected during our short visit.Overall, my first visit to Notuhu Ecotourism was a very positive experience. The combination of beautiful natural surroundings, cool climate, and clean river made it a refreshing escape. I look forward to returning for a longer stay to fully enjoy all the activities and the serene environment this ecotourism site has to offer.

title: Nomad Adventure Sdn Bhd
translated_review: Came here for some whitewater rafting but did not stay at the camp. Good website, responsive over email and lots of FAQs and guidance to inform you of the trip.Took my parents who are 60 and had never been whitewater rafting before. We had a great time. Not the most challenging rapids but was still good fun with about 22 individual rapids in total.Depending on the river conditions it can take 30 minutes heavy rains, fast flowing river to 2hrs to complete the course with a 20min or so break in the middle for some drinking water and an apple. If the water is low it takes longer but is not as fun and the boat can get stuck. If the river is higher it s a lot rougher but over quicker! They advised the best time is around November to December if I recall for higher waters.Our guides were friendly and fun. They also provided lunch which was quite tasty back at the camp.The site is located in a small kampung and requires a lorry limo transfer circa 30 mins from the home base to the launch site. They provide lockers toilets and showers if neededWould love to come back to gopeng again for another round of rafting or perhaps some of their various other activities in the future.The guide also shares photos via WhatsApp free of charge which is nice. Good customer service!

translated_review: Love the rustic, sustainable, back to nature campsite, called Earth Camp! Their winner adventure programmes include signature activities like white water rafting, high ropes course called Mountain School, which integrates with a cliff wall for a thrilling 52m flying fox , caving in one of the largest and oldest caves in Malaysia and even waterfall abseiling!Sustainability programmes bring participants closer to both nature and its local community, through first hand observation of local environmental issues and hands on local farming experiences. Check out their latest bio tech composters which turn camp food waste into rich compost for the grounds. Like nature, nothing is wasted! Nor will your time be, coming all the way here!

title: Nomad Adventure Sdn Bhd
translated_review: Me and my friend had an awesome time last week at Earth Camp! If you are into adventure this is the place to go! Many possibilities for every level in different activities like climbing, rafting, kayaking and abseiling. The accommodation is simple but contains all you need. A special thanks to our guides Fakrul and Roy for their great sense of humor and for giving us a safe feeling all the time!

I ll recommend Nomad Adventure for all of you want to experience such activity in Malaysia.

title: Nomad Adventure Sdn Bhd
translated_review: Our family of four had an absolutely fantastic experience with this company! In preparation of our trip, the information provided through 1 on 1 Whatsapp contact was thorough and clear, which made us feel well prepared for our adventure. Safety is clearly a top priority for them, and they went out of their way to reassure us following the unfortunate incident that occurred in November not with this company! .Their flexibility was impressive they arranged for an extra guide to join our white water rafting trip with his family just a day in advance. This made it possible for us to have our trip, as their safety procedure calls for at least two boats for a trip so you can keep an eye out for each other. Before the trip, we were treated to a delicious lunch, which was a great start to our activity.The equipment provided was in good condition, and they took ample time for the safety briefing without compromising the fun of the activity. Their most important rule is to keep smiling, no matter what and they explain how smiling helps your safety! . Experiencing the river s strong current and colder temperature beforehand made us feel more comfortable and confident and allowed us to fully enjoy the rafting trip. Halfway through the trip, we were provided with drinking water and a healthy apple, and also enjoyed some time to play in the calmer part of the river.After the trip, it started to rain. They quickly arranged umbrellas for us so we could stay dry on our way back from a nice warm shower. The staff was all very relaxed and hospitable. The guide on the other boat took many photo s and video s on the trip and airdropped these to us afterwards.We also asked them where we could buy a congkak game as a souvenir from Malaysia. They went above and beyond to help us, sending tips via WhatsApp after asking around for us. Tips included the Sunday morning Fleamarket in Ipoh recommended! and a craft shop that unfortunately only sold newly made smaller sets. They even offered us their own if we couldn t find one. Unfortunately, we couldn t find a simple large wooden one, so we stopped by their place again near Gua Tempurung the next day. Although our guide from the previous day wasn t there, the ladies present knew about our request and had just been discussing where to buy one. Soon after, we walked out with a beautiful wooden congkak game and a great story of how we found our souvenir and some great lunch recommendations Ipoh Curry Bread Bun! and cave temples to visit.The staff were incredibly friendly and went to great lengths to ensure we had a wonderful and safe! day. Our son had been there before with his schoolcamp and had had a wonderful adventure too! Highly recommended! Would love to go back again and enjoy some of the other activities they offer ziplining through a cave sounds cool! .

title: Nomad Adventure Sdn Bhd
translated_review: Our recent 3 day, 2 night trip with Nomad Adventure Gopeng was everything we hoped for and more! We had such an enjoyable time diving into all the activities they offered.We started with some great cycling, then got to explore the incredible Gua Tempurung cave, experienced the thrill of whitewater rafting, and even tackled the Mountain School jungle obstacles.What truly made the trip exceptional was the Nomad Adventure team. Thank you to Imman, Kecik, Meor, Zaf. They were always ready to lend a hand, incredibly knowledgeable about all the activities, and genuinely made us feel taken care of throughout our first time experiences.Let s talk about the food seriously, it was delicious! The full board meals were cooked to perfection, and we absolutely loved the unique hearth tea experience, where we got to grill fresh and dried fruits to brew our own tea. Such a memorable moment!For anyone craving an adventurous yet super enjoyable outdoor getaway, you absolutely have to go with the Nomad Adventure team. Highly, highly recommended!

We stayed in Ipoh and make our way to Gopeng which is around 25 30 min.We took the caving and white rafting activity which took a day to complete. My kids loves both activity! Not being an outdoor kids really brings the best out of them.The caving consist of both dry and wet obstacle so be ready to be wet! For the kids, they love the adventure! John our lead from nomad for the day was awesome! He mistook me as a geologist I am by the way the way he explain how the cave form really impress me.In between activity, we had lunch buffet prepared by Nomad and the food is good to my surprise! I thought it s just food in container, my spouse love the way nomad goes above and beyond for this. The rafting took the afternoon session and T was awesome in navigating the boat and explain some historical event about the river. We need to paddle extra hard as my son is a passenger during the event. Overall engaging nomad is the best decision we made during our 2 night 3 days trip down Ipoh.Defiantly recommend the if ur around Gopeng!

translated_review: River Safari Experience A Great Adventure for All Ages!We signed up for the River Safari, which allowed us to choose our preferred rafts. The guides were attentive and could anticipate potential danger zones, giving timely directions to ensure safety. I even toppled once in the kayak, but the situation was handled calmly and professionally by the guide. A big thumbs up to guides Iman and Zaf, along with their supportive team!The toilet facilities were newly built with an open roof concept. They were clean, not smelly, and had ample space perfect for large groups.The complimentary was average, but the fried chicken was especially tasty. We also appreciated the fresh salads served alongside.All equipment provided was in good condition and looked new, which added to the overall sense of safety and comfort.Overall, we had a great time! I definitely plan to return for more kayaking sessions with these reliable and professional guides. Thanks again for a memorable experience!

title: Nomad Adventure Sdn Bhd
translated_review: We came here as a group of 23 to kayak and stand up paddle SUP down the kampar river. The place is very easy to find using Waze or Google maps, despite being about 10 minutes off the main road into a kampung area. The compound itself is huge comprising of a welcome area, canteen, and cabins. It s generally easy to park too. The guides were very professional the briefings were clear and thorough. The river itself was very beautiful. The water was chilly, and the river cut deep into the forest making you feel like you were far far from civilization. We travelled about 9km downriver not much paddling required and the river was very calm before getting picked up at the end and shuttled back to the starting point. Lunch was provided and the food was good too. Highly recommended. I ll definitely be back to do this again in a smaller party. Big groups are no fun because you have to keep stopping in order to keep everyone together.

translated_review: What s not to like? The accommodations are clean and well kept. They re quick to respond to any questions or problems and were very professional throughout our trip.We booked for River Safari and Caving Gua Tempurung and each activity started with a detailed briefing on how to save ourselves and what to do if we couldn t as well as the equipment needed. Caving was actually so fun! We were worried we would be cramped in the cave with the other 50 people but when it actually mattered, we were pretty much alone with our guides and no other group was nearby which was a big plus.One of the staff mentioned Gopeng having very unpredictable weather so be sure to be prepared for any possibilities. Safe to say, she was absolutely right as we were facing the agonizingly bright sun from morning to afternoon and a storm that got all our things wet, including our tent, from midnight to the next morning.Any info regarding food, nearby restaurants or activities will be provided by the staff when asked. 10 10 amazing place.

translated_review: This was our first bike packing trip and this place was a great choice. It was about 1.5 hours by bike from our place. We were welcomed by the owner and were introduced to the plants there. You can scan the QR code to know the characteristics of each plant. That night I also saw the durian flowers and have a chance to try the smell. We set up a tent and enjoyed BBQ there, that night it rained heavily and we moved the tent to the shelter because our tent had a small hole. The shelter protected us from the rain until morning and luckily we slept very well. I found it very suitable for large groups and convenient for families with small children. You can also watch the sunrise in the morning. The toilet and space are very clean. I thank the owner for welcoming us so warmly. I will definitely recommend this place to my friends.

title: Pasembur King (Hussain)
translated_review: I was under the impression from a Grab driver s recommendation that this was going to be fried dough with peanut sauce, like a doughnut. NOT THAT. Truly bizarre food and experience no regrets.Step one get on line 1 and wait to take a very large plate and use tongs to select your choice of dozens of items from the counter.Step two load you plate with a mountain of fish balls, lobster balls, deep fried tofu, deep fried cheese, battered whole lobsters, crabs, squids, octopuses, giant prawns, shrimp cakes all in their shells , cookies, and a variety of hot dogs and sausages.Step three get on line 2 and wait your turn to hand your mountain of fried wonders to the chef. He then chops it all up and tosses it like a salad with julienne cucumbers and douses it a dark maroon nutty chili sauce.Step four eat.A truly new culinary experience for me, truly positive experience novel flavors and textures, salty and sweet, crunchy and a little bit spicy. One was enough! 18myr for my portion, see pics. My plate was about a quarter the quantity of what most folks took. Was plenty for me, and to share.

title: Pasembur King (Hussain)

When the staff prepares food for my plate from inside, with a plastic cover, he turns off the light fan switch and resumes cutting my food. The fried shops next to this shop are also not environmentally friendly. Bbrapa cust come to order here. With a sour face and Xpandang cust Not there .. Not opening . Just not wanting to do business....

title: Penang Street Art
translated_review: If you re in Penang, a walk down Armenian Street in George Town is an absolute must for art enthusiasts and culture seekers alike. The area is a vibrant blend of history and creativity, with its iconic street art installations perfectly complementing the heritage architecture.The murals, many of which were created by Lithuanian artist Ernest Zacharevic, are an essential part of Penang s charm. From the playful Little Children on a Bicycle to the wistful Boy on a Motorcycle, these artworks are not just paintings they re an interactive experience. Watching people pose creatively with the murals adds a delightful energy to the street.Beyond the murals, the area is alive with local businesses boutique cafes, craft shops, and quaint galleries adding depth to the cultural experience. It s also worth exploring the hidden corners, as some of the lesser known artworks and graffiti gems are tucked away in quiet alleys.Tip Visit in the morning or late afternoon to avoid the midday heat. Comfortable shoes and a camera are essentials. If you re here during the festive, visiting at night will have really nice light installations accompanying you too!Whether you re here to soak in the art or enjoy the vibrant street life, Armenian Street truly encapsulates the soul of Penang. A stroll here leaves you inspired and deeply connected to the island s cultural heartbeat.

translated_review: Penang Street Art in George Town is a must see attraction for anyone visiting Penang. Located in the UNESCO World Heritage Site, it brings the city s walls to life with colorful murals and creative installations. The most famous artwork, Children on a Bicycle, is loved by travelers worldwide and makes for the perfect photo spot. Walking through these charming lanes, you ll discover art that blends history, culture, and daily life in Penang. Every corner tells a story whether it s playful, nostalgic, or thought provoking. The area is easy to explore on foot, and you ll also find local caf s, shops, and street vendors nearby. This mix of heritage buildings and vibrant art makes Penang Street Art one of the top things to do in George Town. It s a cultural experience you simply cannot miss if you want to capture the true spirit of Penang.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: The vineyard is spacious with good facilities. However, there were no grapes in the vineyard, perhaps it was not the season yet. There were not so many visitors, perhaps the management should consider having a few activities to attract more visitors. The vineyard also lack visitors guidance. There is no introduction session or information tour or something alike , cos we as visitors are left to wander on our own inside the place without any guidance. Anyway, just enjoy the view and the walk is good and refreshing.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: This place took me somewhere I cannot reach, even though the climates not as chilled as we expected, it s still nice. Plus the workers there are helpful.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: this was also a very nice place But when we reached there it was closed due to construction.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: Thought the place was closed but apparently has to enter through Tmn Buah buahan Eksotik, FOC, just need to register with the guard at entrance.The down side with FOC is no one to explain anything, but you are free to wander around within the park, wherever not fenced off.Apart from grapes, there are also dragonfruits gac, all in small scale meant for visitors, can t really enter the actual farming areas of these fruits.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: To fully enjoy it, I advise only to come here during the weekend or holiday even though it might be cramped.We visited during weekdays though, so hm. A lot of attractions here are not available, can t give food to the fishes in the pond, no ticket counter opened. So we basically just roamed around minding our business, took photos and just went through anywhere available to be entered. Can t even enter the Grape yard. Only outside Dragon Fruit superfruits .

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: The view here is superb!! It's beautiful when taking pictures. There are many fish food standbys. The boys enjoy eating fish.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: Visited at the end of August 2025. Nothing much to be seen. Maybe not the fruit season yet. Beautiful surroundings. Breathtaking view of Wang Gunung, the limestone hill, that s part of Banjaran Nakawan.

title: Perlis Vineyard
translated_review: It's worth going to October for a little wine..dry..pls..I love the spacious place, there are many activities that are provided to attract visitors, they are not well taken care of..it's a bit disappointing for the 1st timer to come..it's like preparing other activities for trees that are not in season..so that you can pick fruit, you can sell it and make a lot of fruit replicas for fun..

title: Pusat Pengembangan Tenun Pahang Diraja Kg. Soi
translated_review: I came here April 9, 2025 with my mother's husband. The male caretaker went out for a quick look, then came back in. I turned on the fan with the light myself as if I was working there. Then, my mother left because she didn't like the service given. My mother is in her 70s, so she feels better. My husband and I are being silly, because we want to buy something before we go back to Dubai as a souvenir. The male guard is at the gallery counter, doing work. Didn't even look up until I asked the price of the bag. Fortunately, they answered when I asked the price. But, I went in hoping for a little information about Pahang weaving that the information board told me was not very detailed. I left this place, my knowledge is the same as before I entered lol. The male guard is not playing with a zikr counter on his finger, a hat on his head, but the human relationship of the buyer is ignored. honey The girl, I could tell from other buyers who came to pick up the goods, that the buyer was angry. Wow, do you remember that you are the king's son? After all, let work be sincere. You represent the Pahang government as the person who markets Pahang weaving, so please be polite. You don't even need to make out, as long as you are polite and courteous.

title: Restoran Selera Nogori
translated_review: Overrated, only word to sum up this joint. The reason we went because we googled place to eat in seremban and saw this. To our dismay, this is nothing more than canteen food with plenty more choices. You can taste that the food is mass produced with little attention to quality. So...It s like...the more dishes the better, and people here just flock to this place. It s called selera negeri but there are Thai style chicken, butter prawn, to name a few so its just a mashed up food place with no unique offering to warrant high rating for my review. Its unlike several places we been to, ie Haslam or Capital Cafe in KL that offer you tasty home made cooking with several noteworthy dishes like their kerabu, or petai sambal or even omelette.The place is stuffy and congested and hardly any space for you to walk. Service? Don t expect any friendly smiles here. In fact the servers keep on asking you to move aside with their stern faces as there s always one behind you carrying plates, glasses etc. Rice is free of charge, but of course its been built in the entire food price. I m sure if I just eat a plate of rice with fried egg I won t be charged just rm1, or will I?Be prepared for smoke attack on you clothes or hair there s smoke coming from the grill fish station into the shop and people who refuse to understand that smoking in enclosed area is prohibited.

The most obvious problem is all dishes are not covered. I took chicken cooked in red salad, and became a victim. My friend took another side dish, it's okay, so clearly the side dish I took was the problem. After eating at this restaurant, I didn't eat at any other restaurant, I just ate home food, my family was all healthy. So confirm the cause of the stomach ache is indeed from this shop. so far, the cleanliness of the shop is okay. It's just that the cleanliness of the food is very bad. Please take care of the cleanliness of the food. Don't play with people's health. If you don't know how to cover a side dish, it's better not to sell it. This is unacceptable!

title: Royal Selangor Pewter Workshop (KL)

title: Royal Selangor Pewter Workshop (KL)
translated_review: I recently visited the Royal Selangor Pewter Visitor Centre and was truly impressed beyond expectations. I learned so much about the fascinating history of the pewter industry, especially its roots and development in Malaya. The exhibits were engaging, informative, and beautifully presented.What stood out most was how well the visit was organized. My guide, Ms. Qi, was exceptional knowledgeable, friendly, and passionate about sharing the stories behind the craft. Kudos to her!The entire tour was free of charge, yet it felt like a first class experience, complete with a well structured presentation and even complimentary drinks at the end.After the tour, we were free to explore the gallery and admire the beautiful pewter creations. Visitors are encouraged to touch and feel the craftsmanship, and there s also a chance to purchase items. I was especially excited to see the impressive collection of special trophies they have crafted including the Malaysian Grand Prix Formula 1 trophy. I ve always wondered about its weight and secretly wished to hold it. Even though most items in the gallery are touch friendly, I decided to be polite and asked the elderly saleswoman first only to be unexpectedly scolded like a schoolkid! It was a surprising moment, but it didn t dampen the overall wonderful experience.I left feeling inspired and grateful for such an enriching visit. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Kuala Lumpur!

title: Royal Selangor Pewter Workshop (KL)
translated_review: We visited the Royal Selangor Visitor Centre during our city tour with Skyline Holidays. Our guide Ilyas brought us here and we are so glad he did. It turned out to be one of the most enjoyable and educational stops of the day.Once we arrived, a guide from the visitor center took us on a fascinating tour through the world of traditional pewter crafting. We saw live demonstrations of casting, polishing, and intricate design work and learned about the history of Royal Selangor and its global legacy. The guide was excellent, informative, engaging, and great at explaining things in a way that kept even our young daughters interested.We loved sipping cold drinks served in pewter cups, and the Petronas Twin Towers replica made entirely from pewter tankards was truly a showstopper. The gift shop was beautiful, offering everything from elegant keepsakes to playful souvenirs. My daughters especially loved the free popcorn samples from the caf and had fun exploring the interactive exhibits.It was a well balanced mix of culture, hands on learning, and fun for the whole family. Many thanks to Ilyas for including this stop in our tour. It was a hidden gem we might have otherwise missed. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Kuala Lumpur, especially with kids.

title: Rumah Makan Perpateh
translated_review: We recently visited Rumah Makan Perpateh after a recommendation from the staff at Habeeb Carpet, and I must say, we were pleasantly surprised!At first glance, the area seemed quiet, with several empty and closed shops around. For a moment, we thought the restaurant might not have many customers. However, once we stepped inside, it was a completely different story there were plenty of diners, which immediately reassured us that we were in for a good meal. No wonder there were so many parked cars outside!The ordering process was straightforward we scanned the menu and browsed through the options. The restaurant specializes in traditional Malaysian dishes, and we found the prices to be quite reasonable. What impressed us most was the speed of service the food arrived quickly, without compromising on quality.Now, onto the food itself absolutely delicious! The gulai ikan tenggiri was outstanding, perfectly balanced with just the right amount of spice. It paired beautifully with sambal ikan bilis tempoyak, which added an extra layer of flavor. We also tried the sambal petai udang, and it was equally good. Every dish tasted authentic and well prepared, making it a truly satisfying dining experience.One suggestion for improvement perhaps adding more traditional vegetable dishes, especially ones that are harder to find in regular restaurants, would make the menu even more exciting.Overall, we re really glad we gave this place a try. The combination of great food, reasonable prices, and a warm dining atmosphere makes Rumah Makan Perpateh a must visit. Highly recommended!

title: Rumah Makan Perpateh

It s an okay food which is not that Wow delicious meals. There are many more restaurants that provide much better lemak cili api and asam pedas. Lemak cili api meals is not spicy even this is famous Negeri Sembilan meals in Negeri Sembilan state. The highlight is the daging salai which is unexpectedly quite good. Vegetable such as sawi pak choy is not not not not recommended at all.Overall, everyone did have their own opinion so this is mine. If you really want to try, go ahead. Also If you are too lazy to cook that day, come here to get the premium feel to have a kampung meal in a nice environment.

title: Ruza Agrotech
translated_review: 24 12 2022, Saturday An interesting experience to share. I am very impressed with Ruza's management idea. Creative. Car parking available. Free.1. Visitors need to buy canteen day tokens worth a minimum of RM15 and a maximum of RM20.2. Adults need to pay a RM1 token to ride the tram to a place called Phase 1. To go home, pay a RM1 token. Total token, RM2. Children 2 years and under free. 3. In Phase 1, you can take pictures in interesting places and go to the shops provided. Only, you have to pay using tokens. Musolla surau is also provided. This one is highly praised because it is very conducive. Toilet No. 2 cannot be pumped ..4. To enter Phase 2, visitors need to pay a token of RM5 per pax. Actually worth it.. the area is very wide. Not just a photo spot. There are also various animals like in the Mini Zoo, suitable for children to get to know animals such as ostriches, hedgehogs and so on. Musolla in Phase 2 is in a bus-shaped container, I prefer it in Phase 1. Overall, great job to the management. We need more of this. Phase 3 near the parking lot will be opened Inshaallah month 2 of 2023 during the Israk Mikraj holiday. Phase 4 also shortly after that.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We had an absolutely amazing time on this food tour! From start to finish, the experience was seamless, engaging, and, most importantly, delicious. title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We had an absolutely fantastic time on this tour, and a huge part of that was thanks to our incredible guide, Ian. Ian was knowledgeable, energetic, and personable. He transformed this food tour into an immersive cultural and culinary experience. If you re considering this tour, do yourself a favor and book it and if you re lucky enough to get Ian as your guide, you re in for an unforgettable adventure!

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We had Kiran as our guide and she was extremely knowledgeable and made sure that we had a great time. The food was great and we were definitely full by the time the tour finished.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We had the pleasure of joining this food tour with our wonderful local guide, Kash, and it truly exceeded all our expectations. From the very beginning, Kash made everyone feel welcome and included, sharing not only delicious dishes but also deep insights into the cultural and historical roots of Malaysian cuisine. His knowledge is outstanding, and his passion for his heritage shines through. As a vegetarian, I was genuinely impressed by the care and flexibility in offering delicious alternatives that matched the original experience from tasting tropical fruits, Mamak style noodles to flipping traditional Roti Canai a hilarious and humbling highlight! . And visiting the Kampung Baru neighborhood gave us a deeper connection to the people and history behind the food. Kash s warmth, humor, and genuine interest in each participant made the experience a shared journey. It was educational, delicious, and so much fun. We wholeheartedly recommend this tour. Thank you, Kash!

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We live in KL but decided to do this tour to show some of our guests a lot of Malaysian food in a short amount of time. The tour is well organized and has fun extra bits like trying to make your own Roti Canai. I tried a few things I d never had even after 2 years in KL and there were definitely a few places I would now go back to. A definite success!

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We loved our tour. This was our first stop after arriving in Kuala Lumpur and was a great introduction to the city and the food. I have several food restrictions. The team was incredibly informative about what I would and would not be able to eat ahead of time and made several accommodations which I really appreciated. Thank you Steve for a wonderful tour!

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We loved the food tour! Ian was incredibly knowledgeable and guided us through the local cuisines with great enthusiasm. He clearly enjoys sharing his knowledge and his passion was contagious, making the experience both educational and delicious. We tasted a wonderful array of dishes and ate so much that we could barely finish by the end of the tour. Highly recommend for anyone wanting a flavorful, engaging, and insightful look at local food culture.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We really enjoyed our food tour and seeing some of the sights of KL. We tried many different dishes and got more comfortable going into little mom and pop shops and ordering dishes among locals.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: We would recommend this tour highly for anyone who is considering taking a food tour. Our host Ian was so friendly and knowledgeable. We learned about and ate foods that we would have never ordered on our own. We learned about the area and the people. The Chow Kit neighborhood is authentic. I loved how Ian was welcomed to each of the places we stopped at, how other people knew who he was. He was respectful with all of the guests and by the end of the night it felt like we all knew each other well. So much food. These weren t just tastings they were meals and we were so full, my husband and I were sharing with a guest we had just met that night, so we could all taste it but not waste anything. Still not a durian fan , but I would never had tried it again if Ian had not gently encouraged it.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings
translated_review: What a great way to spend an evening in KL! The tour takes you slightly off the beaten tourist track and you see and eat the real KL. Our guide Kiran was wonderful, articulate, and a great story teller. It was a privilege to experience this food tour.

title: Sambal Streets Kuala Lumpur Food Tour with 15+ Tastings

title: Sekati Emas Boat Tour
translated_review: Got a number from FB and asked to make a booking, but the information given was not clear. Ckp per head RM30 but tomorrow when you go there you will be charged for 1 boat as the minimum is RM120 for the new boat. We are only 3 people. So as it has reached 2 children under 5 years old, it should be free of charge, I still pay RM120 and up. Its okay to pay, just not informed that if per head for a group tour, it needs to be combined with another group. If the minimum price for 1 boat is Rm120 for a private tour, then people will be more clear. I wasn't even told what a private tour group tour is. When I got there, there was no one to greet me, I had to ask and someone would answer. I think it's better to just walk in next time. When I arrived at the jetty, people like us asked for alms, let's ask us with a serious face like we booked the wrong place. Not a warm welcome as expected from Terengganu people. During the tour, there were various promises on Facebook to take our pictures, guided tour, stories about the places to visit, but for me the guided private boat tour this time was quite ordinary. I have paid the same fee for a guided tour in Langkawi, I think I want to go back because the stories about Langkawi and the island are full and dense. Worth it pay. But I didn't get this one today. We have to ask ourselves the name of the place and this is what the boat people are talking about. Anyway, he is friendly, and willing to take pictures and answer our questions. As for the Boat Tour, I think we can improve. Terengganu is so BEAUTIFUL! Bandar Persisir Laut. But I'm sorry I can't give 5 stars for this experience with Sekati Boat Tour.

title: Sky Ladder Pineapple Farm, Port Dickson
translated_review: Fun, Educational, and Perfect for Families!Visited Sky Ladder Pineapple Farm recently and had a great time. The tram tour was both informative and relaxing our guide was friendly and explained the pineapple planting process really well. You also get a refreshing cup of pineapple juice included with the ticket, and it was honestly some of the sweetest I ve ever had!There are plenty of photo spots with colorful ladders and scenic backdrops definitely Instagram worthy. Kids will love the mini petting zoo, corn picking, and even the archery. There s also ATV riding available for those who want something more adventurous.Only downside the road leading to the farm is a bit rough, so drive slowly. Some of the extra activities like ATV or archery cost more and aren t included in the basic entry fee.Overall, a really enjoyable stop in Port Dickson great for families, couples, or anyone who enjoys a bit of nature and local farming culture.

title: Sky Ladder Pineapple Farm, Port Dickson
translated_review: It s a 5 acre pineapple farm with pineapples at different stages of growth so that the farm can ensure there will be supplies of fresh pineapples throughout the year. The staffs were very friendly and help tourists take photos. We have to take a tram ride for RM10 to reach the farm a short distance from the reception area, and we were entitled to a free bottle of pure pineapple juice which cost RM8. So the tram ride actually cost RM2. They just want the tourists to taste their pure pineapple juice only, and the juice is very nice and refreshing. There s nothing much to see or do except taking photos on the ladders of different colours. The road leading to the farm is paved with granite stones but it s easy to find the place. We passed by a few fishing and boating recreation resorts and a cafe. Tourists can stop by for a drink or a meal on the way.

Can buy fresh pineapple and other pineapple products and the gac fruits.Overall, it s fun fill and indeed a very good knowledge gained tour for 60 minutes. There are a few packages of the tour besides visiting pineapple farm, which I clude ATV, fishing, etc.

He went into the details on the pineapple variants around the globe and how the farm is more focused on education than commerce.Our tour was only 1.5 hours but it ranked as one of the best and worthwhile investment of time as we learnt a lot more about pineapples, all thanks to Jeff s efforts in sharing his knowledge about the fruit.Though the place may look out of the way, it s definitely easy to find with the signages guiding you. Definitely recommend to anyone who s looking for places to visit in the Port Dickson and Seremban areas!

title: Sky Ladder Pineapple Farm, Port Dickson
translated_review: MD2 Pineapple Plantation Visit Negeri SembilanSweet pineapples, yes but it was the interns who left the deepest impression.This MD2 pineapple plantation in Negeri Sembilan produces exceptionally sweet fruit with the signature low acidity of the variety. But what truly made this visit memorable was meeting the group of dedicated young interns running much of the operation behind the scenes.They came from across Malaysia Sabah, Pahang, and beyond bringing not just geographical diversity, but a shared spirit of commitment. Many are students pursuing agriculture related studies, and their decision to intern far from home speaks volumes about their drive.Despite the tropical heat and long hours, they were energetic, articulate, and generous with their knowledge. From explaining soil prep to walking us through the science of pineapple enzymes, they handled it all with the kind of clarity and professionalism you might expect from seasoned guides.One of them even introduced me to gac fruit, a strikingly red superfruit grown onsite, sharing its nutritional benefits and harvesting process with quiet enthusiasm.In short, this was not just a plantation visit it was a masterclass in what happens when youth, learning, and land come together.Highly recommended for anyone keen to witness Malaysia s next generation of agri leaders in action oh, and yes, the pineapples are absolutely worth the trip.

title: Sky Mirror Sekinchan
translated_review: We made a rather spontaneous dash to Kuala Selangor one rainy evening departed KL around 7pm and reached just an hour later, umbrellas at the ready and spirits still high. The goal? To catch a glimpse of the famed fireflies, one of Kuala Selangor s most magical nighttime spectacles.A word to the wise the best time to visit is between 7 and 8pm. Any later and you risk missing the show, as the fireflies start to dim their glow and call it a night. Timing, it seems, is everything when dealing with nature s own fairy lights.Now, if you re lucky and time your trip just right, you might also witness the enchanting blue algae phenomenon where the water lights up in a surreal neon blue glow. It s like the sea threw a rave and forgot to invite the humans. But do note, this only happens during high tide and certain months, with June being the most likely candidate.Each experience fireflies and blue algae has its own dedicated boat tour, and we went with Jet Sky Mirror Kuala Selangor, a name that s built a solid reputation in these parts. The boats are well covered a blessing in unpredictable weather , and the crew clearly prioritise safety and know how, which is always reassuring when you re out on the river after dark.Tours are reasonably priced at RM35 for adults and RM30 for children local rates , though foreign visitors can expect a slightly higher fare. The ride lasts about an hour just long enough to soak up the serenity and take a few ooh and ahh photos without overdoing it.All in all, it s an experience that blends nature, wonder, and a touch of adventure. Just check the tides, keep an eye on the clock, and let Kuala Selangor do the rest.

title: Step Into Batu Caves
translated_review: As a single female traveler, I joined Eddy s tour to get a guide, but I ended up making a friend in Eddy. Having Eddy lead Batu Caves was truly exceptional. Eddy s passion and knowledge made the experience immersive and enlightening. From his engaging storytelling to the stunning views at the top, every moment was memorable. Bonus! He took great pictures of me alog the way, which I appreciated. Eddy s friendly demeanor and knowledge of Malaysia was also great, I got to learn a lot about Malaysian history and cultures. As a bonus, Eddy showed and dropped me off at Petronas Towers, and another sight. I felt like I made a good uncle in KL now and he was so warm and friendly and kind. I highly recommend Eddy as a guide for anyone exploring Batu Caves solo. My time with Eddy became an unforgettable experience that I cherish.

It was hot and he gave me two water bottles throughout the day. After Batu we stopped for quick shots at two of the cities skyscrapers and finally he dropped me off at Central Market to explore. It felt like hanging out with a friend who knew everything about Malaysia. He is very knowledgeable, positive, and kind.

title: Sungai Lima Pulau Ketam Eco Tour
translated_review: Sungai Lima, nestled on the quieter side of Pulau Ketam, offers a refreshing escape from the bustling main village. Unlike its more commercialized counterpart, Sungai Lima embraces a laid back, rustic charm that immediately puts visitors at ease. The moment you step off the ferry, you re greeted by a slower pace of life, where the rhythm of the tides dictates daily activities.The primary draw here is undoubtedly the tranquility and the immersive experience of a traditional fishing village. Stilt houses, painted in various shades and adorned with drying nets, line the waterways, creating picturesque scenes perfect for photography. You can observe local fishermen mending their nets, children playing on makeshift jetties, and the occasional boat ferrying goods a genuine slice of local life.Dining options are simpler but authentic. Fresh seafood, caught just hours before, is the star of most menus. Don t expect fancy restaurants instead, anticipate homely eateries serving up delicious, unpretentious dishes. While there aren t many organized activities, the joy lies in wandering the narrow wooden walkways, soaking in the peaceful atmosphere, and interacting with the friendly locals.Accommodation is basic, often guesthouses run by families, providing a no frills but comfortable stay. It s ideal for those seeking a quiet retreat, a digital detox, and a chance to experience the unvarnished beauty of Pulau Ketam. Sungai Lima is a gem for travelers who appreciate authenticity over tourist traps, offering a genuine glimpse into Malaysian coastal village life.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: 2D1N stay was perfect to cover all activities! The pace was thoughtfully planned, we had time to enjoy each activity. Very friendly and warm service from all staff we met, and breakfast was delicious! I wish they served better coffee at breakfast, similar to proper espresso, which they serve in Kefi Cafe. They provided instant coffee and milo sachets. Poor noise insulation in room, we could hear people going up the stairs. However, once everyone settled into their rooms, we had a quiet night rest. Most activities are outdoor, so pick the right time of the year and pray for good weather! Edited I informed the resort that we were celebrating a birthday during our 12 July stay. On 17 July, they shared a promotion for birthdays free main course or dessert on their WhatsApp status. It states that it s available all July but we didn t enjoy the promotion. When enquired, they told us to visit again to enjoy the promo. Disappointed. We dined at the cafe during our stay and order 2 mains and drinks, bill was over RM100.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: A charming and peaceful mini resort with a great sense of privacy. The buggy service made getting around the area easy and fun. There were plenty of activities available within the resort, which kept things interesting. However, some of the amenities like the pool table and carrom board could use better maintenance. Overall, still a pleasant and enjoyable stay!

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: A charming respite away from the city. A perfect place for anyone looking to slow down and just be nestled in nature. The staff were always warm and responsive, attentive to our every need. Will be back again!

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: A relaxing and quiet place for a retreat or camp. Went with a group of about 30 I noticed the children loved the activities and staff were attentive and dedicated. The rooms were good except that there was no view from the room. We had to close the blinds throughout so it felt somewhat claustrophobic. Unfortunately, it was too hot and humid 36 C to enjoy the view outdoors or to walk around the property. They served a very good menu. We had a nice bbq dinner but the mosquitoes also had a feast. title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Good place to bring kids to disconnect from gadgets and enjoy nature. Swimming pool with mountain water and fruit picking activities were great.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Great getaway with relaxing views and helpful staff. Loved the farm activities. Wished there were more vegetarian meal options. Missed the campfire due to bad weather but will return.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Great place to go with family quiet and back to nature. The rooms are clean and comfortable. Food is delicious and reasonably priced. Staff are helpful and warm, making the stay pleasant.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Group of eight enjoyed 2D1N stay. Rooms were clean and modern, with umbrellas and raincoats provided. Enjoyed farm activities and good food. Grab service limited but staff helped arrange drivers. Highly recommended.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Had a lovely stay at the farmhouse. Room is modern and aircon works fine. Food at the cafe was fresh and tasty. Staff were friendly. Kids loved the double floor room and the small fishing pond. Highly recommended for family trips.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Had a wonderful experience at Talula Hill Farm Resort! The staff were incredibly friendly and responsive. The event hall was flexible to use, and the resort was very family friendly. Highly recommend for a relaxing getaway with loved ones.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Had a wonderful short trip with family. Staff were friendly and made the stay enjoyable. Kids loved feeding animals and DIY art. The room was clean, though there were some ants. Memorable experience overall.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Had our awesome car event at Talula Hill Cafe. We booked the whole restaurant for 60 pax. Delicious food and drinks with an amazing view. Service was spotless too. They even coordinated parking for our members. Thanks for hosting us!

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Hosted a yoga retreat here and had a great experience. Staff were very accommodating, allowing BBQ over our private bonfire. The mountain pool water was refreshing. Highly recommended.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Hotel stay and breakfast were good. Best for kids with activities like animal feeding and vegetable harvesting. Location is quite far but overall pleasant stay with friendly staff.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: I always wanted my little one to experience a farm stay but still have some luxury. This place definitely offers this! There was a pool, fishing pontoon, chicken and goat feeding, crafting and cooking activities, and a campfire at night. After all the fun, we could retreat back to our comfortable room which had a beautiful view of the hills. The staff are very friendly, always smiling and willing to help. There s always a staff within sight to attend to our needs. I observed that the children can be quite inquisitive but the staff are very patient in answering and guiding them. The main building is right on the hill and a distance away but the staff is always ready with a buggy to fetch us around. Grabfood is allowed to be delivered to reception. Cafe food can be a bit pricey but portions are big and the food is good so it s worth it! Our favourite is the pan seared salmon with cream sauce. Thank you for such a fun stay! Mosquitoes and bugs are expected but we managed.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: I loved staying in this amazing nature resort. The food, service and scenery is everything you need to spend some quiet time in nature. Bird enthusiasts can spot variety of birds from their room. I will definitely visit again. Note There are no bugs in the rooms like some of the reviews are saying, just keep the windows closed and it s all good.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: I thoroughly enjoyed my stay with the kid and friend! The staff are extremely friendly, and my kid loved spending time away from the city, embracing the greenery and natural environment. The picnic breakfast is really unique and tasty. Definitely a great place to visit again!

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: It was my first time stay over at this resort. I love the place as it is good place for a retreat and some catching up with others as there are little distractions. All we heard were mainly sounds of birds and nature which makes it a suitable place to be away from the city. Should be a good place to see stars. The layout was good as it was spacious enough for 2 pax. Visit the various activities eg feeding the animals, eco farming and swimming and games at their event hall. The restaurant food may be a tad expensive but it s good. We like taking our drinks to the veranda for a good chat and good view. The only gripe I have is getting the transport up and down from the resort. Need to do a little more to get hold of a vehicle. Resort s contact Alan for transport is a good one.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Lovely getaway with pretty cabin rooms. Great service, food, and family activities like feeding animals and fishing. Nice view, sunrise deck, and bonfire nights. Some steep roads but manageable.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: My family and I had a relaxing and memorable 3D2N stay in a loft cabin. Staff were very friendly and accommodating, even preparing a birthday surprise. title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: My girlfriends and I had a wonderful time. Staff were friendly and helpful. We enjoyed food and activities that bonded us together. The waffles were great, and sunrise view from the deck was beautiful. Memorable stay overall.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: New resort with relaxing atmosphere. Rooms are nice and cozy, swimming pool available. Needs improvement on the access road with potholes. Fun activities like goat feeding and craft sessions available.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

We don t hang outside after dark, but not many mozzies in the day, quite manageable. My favourite is the room. Looks new, clean, beautiful and well maintained. The bed is beside the full height windows overlooking the palm plantation. Downside is if the kids are too young, they might not enjoy as much. And if it rains it gets boring indoor.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
