title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Stayed here with my parents for one night and it was a wonderful experience. The resort is about a 15 minute drive from Kluang town, surrounded by peaceful and spacious greenery. Staff were very friendly, and there were complimentary activities like pebble drawing, visiting the vegetable garden, and feeding the sheep. You can call for free buggy service to travel to different places in resort. We got picnic breakfast which they deliver your breakfast everything in picnic basket to doorstep and you can just grab a picnic mat they provided and eat outdoors. Breakfast was amazing very generous portions for three people, plus two large bottles of milk and orange juice. The Wi Fi was surprisingly strong, and during check out, we even received fresh vegetables from their garden. The room was clean and well equipped with towels, toiletries, umbrella, iron, hangers, kettle, coffee and tea. Toilet and shower were separate, though the shower area was small with few hooks. The room had both aircon and a fan, but the fan couldn t be adjusted. If the weather is clear, the night sky is full of stars, and in the morning you can catch the sunrise from the caf side. A perfect place to stay for a night to relax and recharge.

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: We were a group of 13 families 30 kids mostly between 3 and 9 years of age, and 27 adults and the team at Talula Hill Farm Resort was nothing short of top notch. title: Talula Hill Farm Resort

title: Talula Hill Farm Resort
translated_review: Yet to check out but I feel compelled to post this review now. Place is amazing but more importantly the staff of this place are truly committed and seem to take a lot of pride in delivering top notch hospitality. Buggy services are beyond prompt we made an advance reservation for dinner at the cafe and they already had a buggy on standby for us while we were feeding the goats. Some chap left their shopping at the front desk and they are going door to door to ensure the package reaches its owner. Everyone we spoke to had so much to share about this place. Stayed two nights. Bunk cabin was spacious. Loft cabin had better views. Dinner service can get busy longer wait time if you come during peak period 6 7pm. Cafe reserves the best tables for resort guests and will always offer to reserve a table when you arrive. Lots of walk ins on weekends nonetheless.

title: The Art Attic
translated_review: An art gallery disguised as a souvenir shop intricate jewellery, hand painted ceramics, delicate textiles, and postcards so beautiful you ll want to send one to yourself. The location is perfect, tucked near Gaya Street, with a post office nearby so you can send those gorgeous postcards on the spot.On paper, it s brilliant local artists and handmade souvenirs. But it s not really for the locals. And the price tags reflect that. Sabahans might stop by, admire the work, but chances are they ll walk out empty handed. Even some tourists feel the sting. A budget friendly section wouldn t hurt. Stickers, small prints, little things that don t make your wallet cry. You see them in art markets, so it s not impossible.The staff are friendly and fluent in English and Chinese, which makes life easier for tourists. No pushy sales tactics, just quiet smiles. But it d be great if they knew more about the artists who they are, what makes them special. Even a simple write up could make all the difference. At a glance, the pieces have that polished, mass market Malaysia look bright, colourful, and safe. With a little tweaking, this place could be a true cultural landmark rather than just a pretty and pricey shop.

title: The Art Attic
translated_review: I m not satisfied with the service that was given to me. I asked one of the staff if the item is in stock as I was finding a gift for my friend. She told me she will check it for me and will come back to me for answers. I waited for about 10 minutes in front of the counter and still did not get my answer but instead left me hanging and went to count the coins. I understand it was a busy hour as there are a lot of tourists, but the service I got was so poor compared to the service that was given to the tourists. I don t know if it s because I m local, but it s still unfair to get this kind of service just because I m local. I will still recommend people go to the store, as you can get so many souvenirs and gifts, but I hope the service can improve better and treat all customers the same. But after going through the experience, will never come back again

title: The Art Attic
translated_review: This is a souvenir shop selling items made in, or related to Sabah culture. Prices are a little high, but the goods are intricate and different from the wares in the numerous tourist souvenir shops along Gaya Street. This shop is located along a chill street with laid back vibes and nice shops restaurants, but as it is a narrow road flanked by shophouses, you may get stuck in the slow moving traffic along that road.

title: The Art Attic
translated_review: This little artisan shop is the perfect place to find unique, handcrafted souvenirs that you won t find anywhere else. From locally made jewelry to beautiful ceramics and textiles, every item feels personal and thoughtfully created.The atmosphere inside the shop is warm and inviting, with the scent of handmade goods and the vibrant colors of each piece catching your eye as you browse. The service was incredibly friendly and knowledgeable, happy to share the process behind many of the items, which made my visit even more special.I found the perfect gifts to bring home, and I especially appreciated the variety of items at different price points, so there s something for every budget. It s clear that this shop supports local artists and crafters, which made me feel good about my purchases.If you re looking for something truly unique or a special keepsake to remember your visit, I highly recommend stopping by. you won t be disappointed!

title: The Backyard Glamping Cameron Highlands
translated_review: My son wanted to experience the trending glamping stay.I booked a night stay here for RM210.00, which is a couple tent. My son brought his own sleeping bag to sleep in.There are 3 types of tents 2 unit of each family, couple and single tents. And priced at RM250, RM210 and RM120 respectively with added surcharge of RM30 on weekend, school and public holidays.I was advised by the opeartor to park within the compound of Ad Diniah school. As soon as I arrived and parked my car, I inquired about the parking payment and a school teacher told me to leave and not park my car there. I asked the Glamping operator and he told me to park at the roadside. It was really irresponsible of them to just brush aside security and safety problems of the car park. The roadside is pretty narrow and so, I decided to leave my car in the school compound.To reach the glamping site, there is a 30m hill hike with hand rails to assist.The glamping site also offers a DIY Steamboat set with added cost on service and rental of fire pit.Once there, we were told of the only toilet to use and some tips on staying in the tent. We had the farthest tent at the site and quite a walk to the toilet. The toilet was smelly and totally inadequate. We had to share that one toilet with the other tent dwellers. It is really disgusting.There is not much of activities to do here. The terrain in beautiful. I was hoping to do some hiking and some high or low rope activities here. But none is available here. It would have been great, though. None of the dwellers took the DIY steamboat, either.And early the next morning at about 8.30am, the opeartor came by the tent and cleared the rubbish and waited to clean it. We actually had until 12 noon to check out. But, I felt like we were being chased away. We quickly packed our things and left.I find that glamping is not for me, especially on the issue of dirty and distant toilet. The absence of activities at the glamping site is also another frustrating issue.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Patin 1 10 fishy smell, catfish sambal too salty, snail 0 10 monkey smell mud smell, stuffed squid 0 10 stuffed squid head apparently, 8 10 ulaman ulaman, overall 2 10, repeat 0 10.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: There are various types of side dishes that are served like village dishes, there are small stalls like cendol. Village food is very good value and delicious. A spacious parking area most importantly the atmosphere of the shop is very comfortable to eat.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: There are many choices of side dishes here, I'm satisfied, the fried prawns are hot and delicious. The price of the baked cake is 4.00 for me.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Relaxed port and many authentic Negeri Sembilan side dishes. Tasty, comfortable and satisfied. Keep it up owner!

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Pros nice place and many tables. Even if there are a lot of people, it doesn't feel crowded, there is a wide variety of side dishes at a reasonable price, rice 3, cooked duck fat, duck egg goulash, tempoyak kayu leaves, catfish berlado, long bean clam rendang, viral curd all that for rm44Cons complicated parking arrangement. Maybe you have to hire Rela or someone because there are stupid people who go to the park near the road to go out. Make it difficult for others to cook fatty dishes that are less spicy. Delicious and spicy rendang that can be eaten

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Really love the vibe here and the space are huge! You guys should eat here as they have a lot of local negeri sembilan dishes that being recommended to try and also traditional food. Price range were also affordable with the food I order.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: I made a stir of nasi kandar, took a little of that curry sauce, a little of this curry sauce. The side dishes are delicious. If you want to remove the spicy because I'm the type who can't stand spicy, rinse it with fried cempedak and cendol durian. In conclusion, I am really satisfied eating here. Staff service is also friendly.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Usually when you visit your newborn, you will eat a lot of dishes here. It's almost 3 o'clock today, so I took the snail dish. But when halfway through eating, I got hit by 3 rotten snails.. when I got the 3rd time, I stopped. When I want to give feedback to the cashier, I just got a rotten snail, and it keeps sounding like a new snail today. Maybe the ones who ate before ate a little bit of large size snails.. The one I brought is ready to be placed.. on top of the large size but the small size. The problem is not the payment, but don't deny it when the customer gives feedback. I'm still ckp then do you think I'm cheating? He kept saying sorry. It is expected that the service will increase in line with the price. There are many workers, so I hope that when they scoop the food into the container, they can QC once the quality of the food is there. InshaAllah continue to prosper

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Everything is delicious except for the snails. Fishy smell. Then there are 2 snails when the suction is really slimy. I already told the staff, the staff just said I'll tell the chef later

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Everything is delicious...cook the crab fat in a thick sticky sauce...it's really delicious...

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: It's not a loss to eat here. Crowded but got a table quickly. Tempoyak wood leaves have a great tempoyak taste and the sauce is thick. Grepek sauce is like sour skit. Prawn sauce is a little sweet, the sauce but even the sauce can taste like shrimp. Cooked fatty meat 8 10. Looks like salai, isn't it... Still delicious

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The place is spacious. The variety of food is great. Rice, side dishes, cakes, cakes, desserts, cendol and many more. The side dishes are ok. Some are tasty. Some are not very tasty. The cendol is delicious. The price is reasonable. It is not very cheap and not very expensive. 2 adults eat and 2 children eat for RM60.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: A place to eat Negeri nine cuisine with a variety of options. He made random eyes. Delicious food with an interesting shop atmosphere is indeed the focus of food lovers.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The best place to eat village dishes, bring the family and enjoy very appetizing side dishes.. and the best mak enon's cendol!! When you come back, you can choose other snacks like pudding, dessert, etc.. best!!

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: the best.. Sembilan country food.. lots of side dishes.. patin tempoyak is delicious.. they also sell banana cekodok.. anchovy cekodok.. boiled potatoes with sambal bilis.. I like the sambal bilis.. delicious.. they also sell durian cendol.. empty water is free to take by yourself at the Coway machine.. there is an air-conditioned hall to make a feast.. you can pay using cash, paywave, scan QR code.. you can pay first at the counter and eat at the table later there are staff who come to count after eating and pay at the counter.. overall everything is the best..

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The place offers and serves a variety of Malay kampung style cuisine, especially the famous Minang cuisine, masak lomak cili api. The price range could be considered reasonable, not too cheap, and not too pricey. The ambience and decorations were totally great, making customers feel comfortable eating and enjoying the surroundings, along with Malay traditional songs played.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The right place to try many different kind of authentic Negeri Sembilan and Malay dishes. They also serve cendol, local desserts, fried banana, cokodok etc.We also experienced superb services from their staffs.Though there are plenty of parking lots and seats available, try to be there early to avoid the crowds.Must try restaurant to satisfy your appetite!

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The Rimba is the best. Many choices of food, taste ok and the price is very reasonable. Earlier I ate for 6 people for only RM48 until I thought the employee had miscalculated. It's just that the parking area is not very wide, so you have to park on the side

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: The Rimba is suitable for the whole family. The dining room is spacious and comfortable.. the prices of the side dishes are listed. some are spicy and some are not spicy

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: There are many varieties food offered ranging from Masak Lemak Daging Salai to Masak Assam Pedas and other dishes. It is buffet style where you can choose your favourite food.The location is good and many seats with ample of parking.There are yummy crispies fried banana and sweet potatoes that you can try aftee having lunch.In Syaa Allah, will come again. Recommended to have your lunch here if you are in Seremban.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: There s a lot of variety food. Price affordable easy parking also big space.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: This place was such a wonderful discovery as we were looking to have late lunch after our hike at 4 pm. Lots of good food varieties and the food is fresh too!! Price is reasonable. And it has a nice outdoor ambience. Bring your family.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: To be honest, the food was okay acceptable and quite similar to some of the well known nasi berlauk in Seremban. It s fair for what it is.However, the service, especially in terms of SOP and staff behaviour, really needs improvement. Unfortunately, I had a bad experience during my 1st visit, which is quite disappointg, especially since I came on the recommendation of friends who spoke highly of the place.I was shocked and upset when my food was still on the table and suddenly, the plates were stacked up to be taken away without any apology from the staff. Instead, I felt like the blame was being placed on me. That was very rude and made me extremely angry and disappointed org tgh lapar kot . Honestly, this is the 1st time something like this has ever happened to me at restauran.But, it s okay..everything has been settled Tq . But I do hope the OWNER takes this seriously by reviewing and improving the SOP, and by providing proper training to the staff. I truly hope this becomes a lesson learned and helps improve the customer experience in the future.I m really sorry to say this, but I was genuinely very disappointed.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Too crowded for me.Parking quite hassle.Table alot but need to wait during peak time.A bit pricey

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Try to come before the lunch crowd. You d be surprised how pack it is during that time. Variety of Malay dishes dessert to choose.

title: The Rimba Lavender Heights
translated_review: Wonderful selection. Had this cendawan masak lemak thingy which I haven t had for the longest time. Anyway, let the pictures explain. A must come again.If I could make a request, it would be1. Crispy Goreng Ikan Keli.2. Sambal kelapa ..... please......

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka
translated_review: I went in August 2023. The space was unique, and the instructor was friendly and energetic, creating a great atmosphere. All the ingredients were provided, so you just listened to the instructions and cooked. Being fluent in English definitely helped you make the most of your time, but the instructor tailored the questions to your English ability and explained things slowly. It was a truly enjoyable experience.

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka
translated_review: A fun and delicious experience!One of the most authentic and personal cooking classes that I ve ever taken!Teena is so welcoming and easy going. She told us a lot about Malaysian cuisine and culture. She really cared about getting to know us all while skillfully instructing and explaining to us why and what we were doing.And the food turned out amazing

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka
translated_review: A wonderful homestyle cooking experience. Teena is a great host, vibrant and engaging.The food was delicious too!

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka
translated_review: Absolutely a must do. Great for couples small groups and even perfect for team building exercise. Best part is Tin s and Leas hospitality and opportunity to Cook authentic Malay Melakian food. Great choice Gado Gado as everyone pitched in. Afterwards we ate as a family group and enjoyed the scrumptious food. If your walking by their home just knock and introduce yourself. Last night we had people from Singapore France Canada and USA. Lovely evening thank you so much. An absolute highlight to our stay in Malaysia. Happy New Year everyone

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka

title: Traditional Nyonya Dessert Cooking Class in Melaka

Kayaking and peddle boatWhile waiting for our ATV session, we just went for kayaking and peddle boat beside. title: Uncle Wong Happy Farm

They provide different type of package for the activities. We too the package with kart, ATV ducking paddle. The karts took about 10min, ATV 45 minutes and ducky paddle about 30min. title: Uncle Wong Happy Farm

translated_review: Be sure to put this into your itinerary if you are visiting port dickson. Suggest to put as the 1st stop early before noon during your journey TO port dickson. Since most hotels at PD checks in at 2 3pm. Assuming you are frm KL or seremban, its around 20min drive frm lukut exit. Frm highway. Staff here are very very friendly n helpful. The operators explain one by one to all atv utv riders. N also giv you a free complimentary boat ride. The startdard rm80 per pax adult pricing involves a relatively easy 40mins off road ride thru the surrounding ladang sawit. Route difficukty is suitable for fetching kids 4 and above, but cautious is required. Its by far the easiest n newbie friendly atv route ive experience frm my few times of atv ride. Be sure to wear cool long sleeved clothing, or arm covers, sunblock n insect repellent is great too. A small bottle of water per rider would be sufficient for the ride.

However there are 2 geese put together with them and I spotted one of the geese was having a fight with a duck. Should they separate the geese from the ducks? Oh and we fed the ducks and geese the food we bought at the ticket counter. There are also several poultries, birds, primates, goats, and cows. I just hope that they can take a better care of this place and also the animals,, and promote this place more so this place can keep going on, I mean for the sake of the animals.

translated_review: Our first visit here today, overall experience was good. The place is pretty clean, staffs are helpful and friendly when you ask them questions.Walk in price for adult is Rm 12 per pax, and we bought a packet of foods for the animals which cost Rm 5 per pack.We came here at 9am, but what was disappointing is that the bird show starts at 11am, it s too late for us that arrive by 9am. We took around an hour to finish the whole park, it s not that big but they have cows, kitchen, duck, goose, goat, monkey, fishes, some plants, and birds.So I think if you wish to enjoy the bird shows, maybe should come at 10am or 10.30am instead.The cafeteria was closed too when we were there, dunno is it too early or something.Overall I m quite satisfied with the park, will visit again once in a while.

title: Zaini Salai House
translated_review: For me, eating chilli fat here is not very tasty. Hot chili coconut milk is not balanced. There is no taste of fat in the sauce.. only the spicy taste of chili and rice. When you put the sauce on the rice.. only the rice chili remains on the rice.. the sauce does not thicken at all. So don't cook the rice with chili fat. It's happened 2 times.. I remember that day maybe because there were a lot of people so the chili tasted spicy like it wasn't cooked. But when I went the 2nd time, there was no one.. it still felt the same. Nothing to hype. title: Zaini Salai House

As for me, my friends brought me here to taste for the first time, I came back on my own the next day for lunch.The gravy is cooked to my liking. The lemak from the coconut milk and the level of spiciness is well done. I even drank the kuah from the bowl on my first time there. Loved the chilli kick!They also served several other dishes. And for masak lemak , they have various proteins such as duck, chicken, free range chicken ayam kampung , quail, mushroom and white lipped eel catfish ikan sembilang .As for the desserts, you can enjoy cendol and tapai made from fermented rice .I highly recommend this place. title: Zaini Salai House
translated_review: I rarely leave reviews, but this one is well deserved. I visited their Senawang branch recently and was genuinely impressed from start to finish. The food was absolutely delicious, especially the grilled fish. Not even the slightest hint of fishy smell, which is so rare! It was cooked to perfection and full of flavor.The staff were also incredibly friendly and sincere. After our meal, they checked in to make sure everything was alright, and it didn t feel scripted at all. You could really feel their genuine care and hospitality.Later, after leaving and already on the highway, we realized we forgot to inform the counter that we added extra rice ourselves. I called I believe it was the Ulu Bendul branch that picked up to let them know and asked if I could transfer any additional charges. But the lady who answered was so kind and soft spoken, and simply said it s okay, halal je. Honestly I was touched. It s rare to find a restaurant with such heart, honesty, and genuine service.they left a beautiful impression on me. Great food, great people, and a warm experience overall. Highly recommended!

translated_review: The location is pretty good and the facilities are great. The restroom was clean, but there was no water that was my only complaint.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: There are hills which really test your stamina and back strength. Women s toilets are always clogged, and when you open the lid, you re surprised. The water heater pipe leaks. On the night of March 15th, a bad experience.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: A nice clean campsite with a great view! Perfect for families and groups.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Amazing campsite for families! Everything provided full setup and unbeatable view. We travel a lot and this is one of the best. Can t wait to return!

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Amazing campsite with excellent views and chilling weather. Gets colder at night 18 C . Well equipped with hot showers, clean toilets, pet friendly and Muslim friendly zones. Great hospitality from the Zakka team highly recommended!

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Awesome place, great view. I love the toilet so clean and even has hair dryer! Everything is good except the flies. Must repeat stay again!

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Beautiful and clean campsite, great views, and super cold weather. Toilets are spotless with hot showers. However, there s a strong smell near the back toilets. Bring warm clothes and proper sleeping bags.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Beautiful rocky campsite with full facilities tables, books, foosball, hair dryers, and showers. Excellent for families.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Beautiful view and pleasant weather. Clean bathrooms. Only downside is the rough road very rocky and muddy. Hope they fix it soon.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Beautiful weather but the flies ruined everything. They were everywhere on food, face, utensils. Facilities are above average, clean toilets, hair dryers, and even refrigerators, but no rubbish bins. Dog friendly but area is small. Good for short stays only.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Best facilities clean toilets, water heater, surau, and grocery store. The campsite is clean only issue is many flies in the afternoon.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Camped 3D2N pleasant and fully equipped. Proper heated showers, plug points, and peaceful views. But the flies are horrendous. You can t relax had to carry trash far away to throw. Came back feeling more stressed than healed.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Chill and cold weather makes you feel good all day long! Facilities are complete, clean toilets, friendly hosts. Perfect for campers.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Didn t stay. Not a great welcome from the office wanted to charge RM130 per night for us to park our own motorhome. No thanks.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Good place with friendly staff and dogs. Bathrooms well maintained but flies are unbearable. Hot afternoon, freezing at night. Nice weather overall.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Spent 2 nights here, not gonna lie there were a lot of flies! Making the whole camping experience not as pleasant when you re cooking and eating. Small stones are hard to sleep on, so be prepared. We bought a pack of 10 sheets fly trap, and used everything on our 2nd day of stay here. Overall facilities provided are great tho. Weather is cold at night, but be prepared with the scorching heat from 10am 5pm.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: The campsite had too many flies and poor water situation. Low pressure, dirty sinks with brownish water, and bad smell. For the price paid, the condition is not worth it. The flies make it impossible to relax or eat. Won t return unless major improvements are made.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Third visit here still good but cats keep stealing my food while cooking. Hope management can manage them better.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: This is my second visit to Zakka and it will be my last. There are a lot of flies around the campsite it s disgusting. Water pressure in the toilet is so low I could only see water dripping out. Water heater not working. Had to shower in 15 C cold water. No rubbish disposal service had to bring our trash down to the entrance. Although the view is nice, there are too many downsides to consider this campsite worth the price.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Toilet okay, Maxis has strong line. But the flies lalat are damn annoying when enjoying coffee. Bring a dome canopy with mosquito mesh nothing else works to repel them.

title: Zakka Campsite

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Unbearable heat between 9 30am to 4pm . Ground is freaking hard to nail bring hammer. Pet friendly but rocky base makes it hard to walk. TOO MANY FLIES! Felt like kena trap they told us only after payment. Dirty sinks splash gray water, smelly toilets, poor maintenance. Management didn t return deposit. You have to drive out to dump trash. One time camp, not recommended.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Very disappointed. The place is rundown compared to last year. No customer service waited a week before and after stay with no reply. Tent had dead flies and cockroaches. Fridge issue too. Dirty campsite, full of cockroaches. Not recommended.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Was in campsite D, if you don t have an air mattress it would be uncomfortable. Otherwise, a great campsite with good amenities and amazing views.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: When it comes to leaving Google reviews, I try not to leave bad ones but this experience was really disappointing. First visit was fine though there was no rubbish bin provided. On the second visit to the new car campsite Lot G, there was water ponding, heavy rain caused flooding, and low water pressure. Then, electricity cut off suddenly during a shower. Customer service was unresponsive they replied one week later! I ll never set foot here again.

title: Zakka Campsite
translated_review: Wonderful experience for 3D2N. Toilets are clean, staff are super friendly, and the scenery is great. Staff even brought us around the vegetable and corn farm to buy at special price. But yes, many flies come prepared.

title: Kilang Kerepek Mak Jah
